reviewDescription: I did not find these very effective. I put up about a dozen of these things around my kitchen, and in a week's time, they caught about 10 fruit flies. At the same time, I put down three plastic containers with a few inches of apple cider vinegar and a drop or two of dish soap in each one, and they caught about a hundred or so fruit flies during that same stretch of time. Henceforth, I'll just use my homemade vinegar traps.
Do not work as well as other brands. Not nearly as sticky either. Won’t buy again
These aren't really working for my fruit fly problem. The flies don't stick to the yellow shape. I've trapped a few but the flies seem to be to light to stick. I've killed more with a hand vacuum.

reviewTitle: Poor design and materials makes using it a big pain. Change often or suffer ant consequences
reviewDescription: Let’s get 1 thing straight. The butterfly design is largely for our eyes only. It serves no purpose other than looking cuter than a bland circle or square shape. No normal bug is going to see that giant yellow butterfly and make the decision to land on it based on the shape. That being said, putting these traps in the garden was a huge pain. The paper kept ripping and removing it [even following the manual] to set the trap was just an ordeal. The adhesive is strong but inconsistent, so when removing the paper sides carefully, the paper gets caught at varying points where the adhesive is stronger, ripping the paper. It could be worse, the paper could be torn at places that render parts of it useless. The trap base is so flimsy that the included shovel is comically a useless necessity. Even after loosening the soil, I find the trap difficult to insert into the dirt. The shovel falls apart easily when in use... so again, it’s useless in function but necessary to install the trap. The traps also sway easily in the wind even when inserted deep enough. Keep well away from the leaves as it can pull parts of the leaves off. Also if you don’t change it often enough, the caught bugs are essentially ant food. Somehow ants are immune to adhesive while larger bugs aren’t... I now have ants in my raised garden beds It does catch bugs though in a relatively short span of time. Holds up to watering the plants too

Exit: after a week of use, these traps started to loose stickiness. They are no longer catching gnats and the gnat infestation is returning. This product claims to be long lasting and both waterproof and UV resistant. I don’t know if it’s due to the water or the light/temperature that has caused them to loose the ability to catch gnats. I hope the seller will fix this by sending replacement as I was excited to give this product such a great review. But am now rethinking. In just 6 hours I’ve caught 92 fungus gnats. I paired these sticky traps up with diatomaceous earth and it has been working so well. It’s definitely worth your money. These will definitely make it easier to clean since the gnats won’t just die all over the house due to the diatomaceous earth. Unlike the vinegar method, these don’t smell up the house.

reviewTitle: Good idea, but didn’t work for us
reviewDescription: Didn’t work for us. Placed 10 yellow glue stakes by several house plant, so far nothing stuck after 30 days. Do see some gnats flying around freely in the surrounding areas.
These didn’t really get anything
Sticky, but hasn't caught any knats. Isti, Didn't catch k a Didn't catch knats

I followed the instructions to put the stickers in the refrigerator for some time to help with the stickiness… still so sticky I couldn’t get the paper off without getting it on my hands or getting it stuck to something else. They do what they’re supposed to, but work like all other traps that are less sticky.
It is sticky, but the gnats just walk off (room temp at 72°F). It might work better with other insects. This product just wasn't useful for fungal gnats.

reviewTitle: Don't buy this. Make your own!
reviewDescription: Well, I got duped by this product. It's mostly my fault. I needed a way to get rid of fruit flies that made their way into my home. I had made my own trap with apple cider vinegar and dish soap, but the vinegar was really bad and stunk up the house. I searched for fruit fly traps and came across this. I gleaned over the description and didn't bother with the reviews (two things I usually never do) and ordered it. It arrived the next day (which is why I bought it) and I opened the package. Much to my surprise and disappointment, it was just a small bottle of apple cider vinegar with soap in it. Man, I felt dumb, but I decided to try it. The cap is a joke. It makes the bottle taller when open and easier to knock over so you can spill it everywhere. Also, it leaks if you close it and don't keep it upright. I poured some into a glass pudding bowl, covered it with plastic wrap and sealed it with a rubber band around the bowl. I poked four 3/8" holes in the wrap, set it out near where the flies are and left for a few minutes. When I came back the flies were all over the bowl. Once they make their way in through the holes they're essentially trapped. You will get the same results with apple cider vinegar and dish soap with this setup (because that's all it is) and over time it'll be cheaper. Don't buy this or any other similar brands. Just make your own trap and save the $8 that I stupidly spent.

reviewTitle: More still flying around than dying
reviewDescription: I've used Aunt Fanny's ant spray in the past with good results. So I thought, why not try the fruit fly solution for those pesky pests? Well, here's a good reason: it doesn't work very well. Sure, I see some floating in the liquid, but there are at least ten times that are still flying all around my kitchen. Then I decided to try one that sticks to the wall with good reviews. But those fell off the wall and I will return them. So I will buy the ones I should have all along, Terro traps. They worked great and I'm embarrassed that I bothered with anything else. I just wish Terro sold the liquid which how this all began.

I had a THICK CLOUD of fruit flies descend in my kitchen! Researching my options, I found this product & ordered a bottle. While I was desperately awaiting its arrival, however, I fell back on 2 older remedies—beer and apple cider vinegar. Maybe 1/8 of a cup of each of these were poured into separate clean cat food tins with some dish soap for viscosity, then covered with plastic wrap, and punctured with small holes. Once the product arrived, I could easily compare the attraction each liquid possessed. Beer? The winner! Apple cider vinegar? A very close second! Lagging way WAY behind is this product. Granted, it’s got a small number of snagged victims, but nothing like the other two. Save yourself some money. You also get the freebie bonus of finishing off the beer that didn’t get poured into the can!
Doesn’t work any better than anything else, which isn’t saying much

This item did not work for me. We followed the instructions and while it did catch some fruit flies, they mostly just hung around the open lid and didn’t actually go into the container. I feel like the design is a bit flawed with that because if the flys go in there is nothing trapping them and they can just fly out. It is easy to use but not very effective. I think if you poured the liquid into something that would actually catch the flys themselves it would work great but again I think the design is a bit flawed.
Items arrived leaking, with one container's top fully off and the other half off and the plastic wrapping the two torn. The fruit flies were having a field day on my counter!

Don’t waste your money. Good old apple cider vinegar works better. Pour 1/4c of acv into a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap and poke small holes in the plastic. It actually works better and doesn’t emit any smell like this product.

Gnats (fruit flies) just hang around the liquid but don't attempt to get in. I suggest the company to make these containers very small with a sticky plastic inside so they can get trapped. The idea is great but the designed is terrible. I don't know what to do with it but I am probably just going to have to throw it away unless the company offers a refund.
Two of the four bottles had loose lids and leaked. Waste of my time and money. Need to return.
Didn't work well. Strong smell

I put 5 of them around the kitchen 2 days ago. We have quite an infestation so was anxious to see them caught quickly. So far, only 1 has flown to and stuck to the trap, and when I last looked, it must have gotten loose and flown away! Extremely disappointed, but at least it didn't cost very much.

reviewTitle: Flies are not attracted to them
reviewDescription: I am sorry to say that these have not worked at all. The little flies are still around and none are on either of the sticky sides of these traps that I have left in several of our plants throughout the house. I was so hoping that they would have worked!

Something is off, the flys just flew by a hundreds of times and did not catch any of them. Maybe the shade of yellow is off. I had similar product from other vendor before (https://a.co/d/7tUHfP1) that one just kept catching thousands!

This product was ineffective against the gnats that were swarming the kitchen. Had I not used apple cider vinegar, this product would just be sitting just like it’s now in my kitchen. I had opened and used it, gave it few days, before figuring out it was useless. The designers either should have done a better job or marketed it for its color and shape. Don’t buy it, unless you have kids who want to play with sticky shape.

Since July 15, when I received this product, not one single fruit fly, gnat, lie, fly or anything has been caught. Still looks like it did the day I set it out. It is sitting by my sink where things go down the garbage disposal where sometimes it will have a couple of gnats but nothing is ever stuck, but I will see still see gnats and flies. If there was a zero rating, that’s what I would give.
This is worthless! It's just a 2 sided sticky cutout. Does not attract the gnats or anything. Had actually ordered something different with a plug in device.

These do not work! A complete waste of money. The fruit flies are out of control this year and they don’t even stick! I feel like they’re just laughing at me. I have the stickers on planters and all over the house w nothing on them. I saw a bunch in the sink so I put one in just to check and nothing!

reviewTitle: Does not work. Save your money.
reviewDescription: Save your money and sanity. This product does not work. I had it sitting in the middle of a fruit fly cluster and not one fruit fly was trapped. I had it there for weeks. No flies in the trap but plenty still annoying me flying around my kitchen sink. I also tried another brand of sticky traps similar to this and they didn’t work either. I also tried Stem and Raid organic sprays and they are oily and smell horrible (citronella and I don’t know what). They gave me headaches. What did work somewhat were those little apple traps. But nothing has been magic. Most successful has been a homemade funnel trap with pineapple juice. Hope this helps.
Waste of money.

reviewTitle: BUYER BEWARE: NOT SOLD BY COMPANY THAT MAKES THEM.
reviewDescription: Rip off. Did not come in a box. Came in a zip lock bag where they hardly fit. Try fitting them back in the bag, with the stingy piece of wax paper... these things stick to everything. And they didn't work at all. They seemed to attract fruit flies into my room (from the kitchen), but in almost 3 weeks, not one fruit fly stuck to the thing. (I can prove this item attracted fruit flies into my room, because I stopped bringing fruit into my room 3 weeks ago, I bought a covered garbage can, and the fruit flies kept coming into my room kept annoying the heck out of me. Not to mention they're unhealthy. I threw away the sticky fruit fly catcher and bam, no more fruit flies in my room. Still in the kitchen, but not in my room anymore. Buy yourself a covered garbage can. Keep your sink clean. Leave fruit in refrigerator or in a bag until it's ripe and then put it in refrigerator. But mostly, don't buy this brand. Whoever sent these to me was NOT the company advertised. Again...see the photos. Another 3rd party Amazon rip off.

Almost impossible to remove the screen covering the liquid and tons and tons of warnings and after saying its safe around kids and pets but also keep away from everything. The smells is also to strong
It stinks really bad and it does not work maybe one or two flies will fall into the water, but the rest of them are smart enough to just sit around the room or run away

reviewTitle: Does not work, at least for me
reviewDescription: This item stinks and only attracted two flys in one month of trying to get them to go or drown in this stink pot. My recommendation is that I will not get this product ever again and I suggest you do the same.

El olor es lo de menos,!, pero mis mosquitos pequeños continuaron igual de molestos y hace un mes lo tengo en mis mosquitos pequeños Cosina, soy extremadamente limpia no tengo frutas ni alimentos a la vista y aún hay pensé se desaparecerían pero no!!! Quizás mis mosquitos son especiales?!!
This did not catch one fly while it was sitting in my bay window for an entire week! All it did was make my whole house smell like vinegar. Does not work save your money!
It's literally apple cider vinegar. Just put some in a jar and poke some holes in the lid
Waste of money had it sitting out for two weeks they would go around it but not inside

It doesn't work. I have had it for 3 days and it hasn't attracted any of the bugs. The vinegar smell is horrible and permeates the whole room. It's states the it is safe around kids and pets but it's an open jar of smelly vinegar that if knocked over would spill all over. I am surprised a Raid product is so poorly manufactured.

reviewTitle: Save your money - Look up DIY & make your own with ACV
reviewDescription: I am sorry to say, that this is nothing more than Apple Cider Vinegar poured into a small container. It was almost $7.... Has caught maybe 2 fruit flies in about 4 days. They land on the edge, get a drink & fly away. Buy a bottle of ACV & use your own small container, if you want to try this

I don't know what else to say other than don't waste your money. Get a bulk size of apple cider vinegar and swish some dish soap in it, because that's what this is (and you'll likely avoid an extreme pungent smell of the vinegar by choosing a strong-smelling dish soap, as this product's smell will knock your head back). There's also the matter of the size - it's overpriced for what you get and the shape (deep and narrow) seems off-putting to them. With this, yes it caught a few but I observed mostly them perching on the edges for a few moments then taking off. Just use a white/pale shallow dish/bowl with actual surface area for them to "rest" on.

reviewTitle: Does not work on house flies.
reviewDescription: This device worked to catch a few gnats, and I suspect might work for fruit flies but it does not work house flies like the description says it does. I had my doubts but decided to try it out as I was dealing with a lot of flies this season and they were consistently working their way into the house. I put up two of these and left them on 24x7 and checked the sticky pads daily and never caught a single fly with them despite being in a heavy fly traffic area in the house. I don't recommend if your looking to remove flies. I think the light is a blue LED not true UV but whatever the reason it fails to do what is claims in my experience.

I did not like this product. I bought the VEYOFLY Fly Trap Indoor to get rid of small flies (fruit flies) in my kitchen, but unfortunately I did not see any effectiveness. Even using the device continuously and placing it on the counter where I usually keep fruit, the small flies kept appearing as usual. I used it according to the instructions and with the included refills, but I did not notice any significant reduction in insects.

reviewTitle: OK everyone repeat after me… DOES - NOT - WORK!!!!
reviewDescription: One star - Do not waste your money on this useless device. You have been warned. I bought this hoping to get rid of the annoying gnats and flies that seemed to have found their way in thanks to a party and a…let’s just say misguided person who didn’t have proper aim with their fruit. Needless to say we found a nice surprise behind the trashcan along with bugs. In any case, this thing turned out to be a complete disappointment. It does not catch any bugs, no matter what kind of bugs they are or where I plug it in. I tried the living room and the dining room and the kitchen. I even bought the two pack. It didn’t catch anything, no flies no gnats not even a spider or anything else. It is just a piece of plastic that has a steady blue light that does absolutely nothing but waste electricity and take up an outlet. I had more luck, making a concoction of apple cider vinegar, sugar, and dish soap then I had with this. (And that wasn’t as great as some have made it out to be) This is a just waste of money and time. I do not recommend this product to anyone. Save yourself the trouble and look for something else. This plug in bug catcher is a scam. The worst part is that I tend to give things time to work. I ended up giving it so much time that I’m past the return window. I’d just look elsewhere for something else. Edit: I have raised the rating to 2 starts since one of them caught 1, yes 1 gnat. No flies or anything else. I have seen other reviews where it has been successful so maybe it works in certain situations. I think the description needs to be updated to depict the scenario where it will actually be useful. I still would not recommend it at all.

reviewTitle: Bugs just laugh as they fly by
reviewDescription: Bought this to control fruit flies in my kitchen. Used it for one week and caught three flies. Didnt live up to the hype

reviewTitle: Nice-looking, simple concept but poor design
reviewDescription: The concept of these traps is simple and typically very effective at catching flying insects. We've used similar products with excellent results and I am fairly sure these would have also worked quite well. However, I ended up returning these and buying the Safer Home traps, which looks very similar to these because these have a (completely unnecessary IMO) ON/OFF button on them that does not remember what state it was in, so every single time the power goes out, even for the shortest amount of time, I have to go around and press the button on every trap to turn them back on. Seriously people, why??? It's an insect trap and its job is to be on catching insects, so why overcomplicate things by adding an ON/OFF button? And if you really must have an ON/OFF button, make it mechanical, so it will stay in the current state. Initially when I plugged the first one in, I thought it didn't work, then realized it had that button, which is not that noticeable (and you know I didn't read the instructions). Also, while I still think these would work, I feel that the LEDs on this trap are not as effective as those on the Safer Home units and these seemed to light up the room more, even though the LEDs don't seem as bright (I think they are a slightly lower wavelength of UV than the Safer Home units use. In any case, I cannot recommend this product due to the above, as there are other choices that are better designed and work exceptionally well in the same price range.

reviewTitle: Not really a great fly trap
reviewDescription: The ease of assembly and quality of the item is well made. I like the blue light on it for a night light. However, it only catches a few of the fruit flies and one mosquito in almost two weeks. I will change out the sticky pad in another week or so but I’m not impressed. I would not buy this item again. A little bit of red wine in the bottom of an open bottle catches more fruit flies and I’ll probably go back to that.
Didn’t catch flies that would sit next to it

Zevo has always been my go-to brand and I decided to give this one a shot instead. I ordered the two plug ins with six cartridge refills and after a few days of use, one of the plug ins stopped working altogether. The light wouldn't stay lit and you'd have to press the on button a dozen times for it to MAYBE light up, only to turn back off shortly after. As far as catching things, not one fly was caught. A small gnat was caught after a few days of use. I returned the product and bought Zevo. Within a matter of hours, I had caught more flying pests than what I did in three days with this product. I will not be purchasing this again. If you've been a Zevo customer in the past, you should stay one. I'm giving it two stars because it did at least catch a gnat.

Product is well made, easy to put together. This product has been in use for over a week and caught about 3 fruit flies. I made sure to follow instructions for best process to catch fruit flies, and still not working.

reviewTitle: Not worth the trouble or the money
reviewDescription: Bugs do stick to it but same as if you used regular fly paper the attraction light is really just a gimmick or at best a cool blue night light.

reviewTitle: Effective, but hard to peel paper off butterfly shape.
reviewDescription: These work but it's always a fight to peel the paper off the butterfly shape. Would love to have the option for all 54 of just the flower shape.

reviewTitle: Good for plant care/maintenance. Not great if you have an infestation.
reviewDescription: I wanted these to be amazing, but I am slightly disappointed. I'm used to getting the "name brand" rectangular yellow sticky traps from my local hardware store, but saw these for much cheaper instead. I was hoping they would still do their job, and they do catch flies, pest, etc. However, The pieces are absolutely huge! I have cut the pieces down and attach them to the sticks in order to fit better with my indoor house plans. They still catch pests, however my rectangular sticky ones definitely catch more bugs more than these.
I bought these because they are cheaper and comes with a lot more but they are not as sticky, I would not buy this one again, should have paid a little more for the previous one I bought.

Good for what they are but I would not use these long term as they are not cost effective when compared to treating the cause of the reason you are using these. Would only recommend for short term use and for that they are great. They don't smell funny or anything like that but they do have a decent enough amount of adhesive to help catch insects aand work great. They last for quite a bit of catches and are small and fit nicely anywhere you want, very minimalistic design.

reviewTitle: Need to use in combo with a BTI product to beat fungus gnats - also annoying packaging
reviewDescription: These are super sticky. I used them in my houseplants and and always came out COVERED in fungus gnats after a day or two when I had a pretty bad infestation. I went through several 48-packs before doing more research on fungus gnats - what really works is a product with BTI like Mosquito Dunks or Mosquito Bits. Use them in your watering can (let soak overnight, then water). After a few months you should see a dramatic decline. I still rate the sticky traps as good at their job, and they can help cull the problem because the Dunks kills a huge percentage of the larvae, while the sticky traps catch a good number of the adults. However I think these are only a part of the equation. Also, the 'star' shaped ones are fine, but the butterfly shapes are annoyingly difficult to remove the stickiness cover part without ripping (and then you have no tab to grab to remove the rest of it - just touching super super stickiness while you fight the product). It's like the people that designed these never actually used them. I ended up developing a very careful technique again after buying MULTIPLE of these 48 packs that works most of the time, but I'm surprised it takes so much effort just to remove the film from the sticky thing.

The traps I received do not have the non-sticky part that you normally can stick in the dirt. The entire surface is sticky and leaves the glue residue all over your fingers when you are trying to place them in the plants. You get like 6 of the clips for 96 traps. I have ~30 plants so most of these I had to place directly in the dirt. Will not purchase again. The do catch the fungus gnats though so they work as expected when it comes to that, so this is more of a inconvenience when compared to other gnat traps I've purchased on Amazon.

reviewTitle: Watched a gnat walk right across this!
reviewDescription: I've had them out for about a month now. I have a plethora of indoor plants and as such have the occasional fungus gnat. While inspecting them for bugs I watched a fungus gnat walk across the surface like it was nothing. So far I've only caught one. I'm a little disappointed. The product seems sticky enough. I'm not sure why I haven't caught more. My sundew has caught more than these traps have.

I ordered the 40 pack and while the issue was almost resolved, I figured I would get another pack. These work great. Very sticky. However the smaller pack has unsticky triangles to insert them into the dirt. These all have external ways of attaching them. My hands get sticky every time

reviewTitle: May not work so well for fungus gnats
reviewDescription: May not work so well for fungus gnats. We got some, but they mostly avoided these it seems.

It's sticky and attracts the fungus gnats. However, the butterfly shape was super hard to peel off the paper. It ripped every time even though I tried to be careful. The other shapes were ok, but the base is too wide and won't go into the dirt easily. The little shovel was cute.

reviewTitle: Mid Tier - wasn’t blown away but also not terrible
reviewDescription: It worked, I suppose? I still have some fruit flies buzzing around, and even in a clean, closed room with three of these, they weren’t completely effective. I really didn’t care for the fact that it was just a wide-open jar. Most products like this have a topper with holes for the flies to go through, but this was just open with nothing on top. Did it catch some of my flies? Yes, it caught a bunch. Did it solve my problem? Not completely. Did it work any better than the little red Apple product? No, and I think the product really suffers from not having a cover on it. Usually, those help to trap whatever flies have scented the bait but haven’t yet drowned. Most of them can’t fly back out once they’ve flown in. This doesn’t have that. It was a bit of a flop for me, but it’s still useful.

This trap has about as much success as a timeshare salesman at a bankruptcy convention. Sure, it gets their attention. Fruit flies definitely show up to the party. They buzz around it like tourists at a gift shop, checking it out, maybe taking a few selfies, but actually committing to the product? That's where things fall apart. The fatal flaw is expecting these tiny anarchists to voluntarily take a swan dive into what's essentially their own demise. Most fruit flies are apparently smarter than we give them credit for. They hover, they consider, they probably write a few Amazon reviews of their own, then buzz off to raid your banana bowl instead. Pro tip: Slap some sticky traps around the perimeter. Think of it as setting up a security perimeter for the world's most underwhelming honey trap. Without backup, this thing is basically expensive fruit fly entertainment. Does it work? Eventually. Is it efficient? About as efficient as using a fork to eat soup. Your mileage may vary, but don't expect any fruit fly apocalypse scenarios

reviewTitle: Didn’t work as well as expected
reviewDescription: Apple cider vinegar with dawn worked way better. I bought this hoping the smell wouldn’t be as strong but equally or more effective at getting rid of fruit flies. It was neither.
Not bad for the price, but didn't attract fruit flies as well as I thought it would.

We work are a small retail shop. I forgot how bad apple cider vinegar smells. Also, most of the fruit flies landed on the edge of the cup, and probably 25% got caught in the fluid. However, the price was cheap, so I guess I got what I paid for.
I mean it was ok. It did catch a few, but only if they happen to land in it. I wouldn’t say it attracts them.

If you are considering purchasing these to try, I would advice don't. Basically all this is, is a jar of apple cider vinegar that has likely been sweetened. They did work pretty well, but no better than a homemade solutions of ACV with sweetener added to it. At the price of $6 and something a jar, I'll just buy the bottles of ACV and make my own solution.
Does a great job of attracting them but a lot of the gnats just don’t land. I put a yellow sticky trap (sold on Amazon in a 54 pack) inside the jar and it greatly increased the catch.

It does smell strongly of apple cider vinegar. I can see it attracts the fruit flies, but after 3 days I only see about 10 dead inside. The rest are still swarming around. No idea if they die from drinking the mixture or if it attracts them and a few happen to drown.
These work but smell rancid

reviewTitle: So disappointed! No longer recommend. Seller never responded.
reviewDescription: FRUIT FLY SEASON IS RAPIDLY APPROACHING AND I'M DETERMINED TO PREVENT LAST SUMMER'S INFESTATION. Update : I contacted the company multiple ways and multiple times. Amazon and the company totally ignored my legitimate complaint. Obviously they don't care about their customers dissatisfaction. It's not about the money - it's about blatant misrepresentation and unethical behavior displayed by both companies. I couldn't even get Amazon to include the "frequently returned" box on the item's page. I bought three bottles of auntie's product on Amazon 6 days ago to replace my DIY liquid attractant of appl cider vinegar +brown sugar +dish detergent. It's been 5 days and can confirm the other negative reviews that the product CAN'T contain the same liquid attractant - the fruit flies feed on the liquid and are not trapped - they can fly away!!! I unintentionally performed a side by side experiment using this WORTHLESS product against my attractant. Both liquids were placed next to separate hanging cylinders of bright yellow sticky fly paper. I actually watched the flies fly from the auntie's product into my liquid. Unlike the auntie product, any flies that were not trapped in my DIY liquid were trapped in the fly paper. After 5 days I had to replace the fly paper adjacent to DIY liquid. in contrast, the 3 bottles of Auntie containing who knows what had A TOTAL OF LESS THAN 20 IN THE BOTTLES AND ABOUT 10 TO 20 TRAPPED BY THE FLY PAPER. I am so angry about wasting money on products from Amazon with good reviews. This product has overall rating of 3.8. But that rating is CUMULATIVE - The much older reviews are generally positive compared to more current negative reviews. I conclude this is due to a change in formulation that neither amazon or the seller made clear. I was so pleased that Good Housekeeping had recommended the best way to eliminates fruit fly infestations in a recent article. The GH panel incorrectly included this product as the only commercially effective liquid attractant since it contained malic acid as part of the formulation. The FRUIT FLY SEASON article made me remember that I had used Aunties successfully a few summers ago and loved their esthetics and effectiveness. I already emailed a request for them to provide evidence of its effectiveness using a recent bottle. I am requesting a refund from this seller based on my results and will update the status with both responses. Meanwhile if you are battling with fruit flies, infestations made worse by the higher temperature (I live in Connecticut) in the Northeast, read below. First understand that you must use the following steps throughout the fruit fly lifecycle (over several weeks so that the original fruit flies are trapped as well as the their progeny hatched from the hundreds of larvae deposited on whatever they fed on. {I am a biochemist and patent attorney so I needed to know what was going on}. Keep in mind for DIY solutions or other traps that you must attract and rid them from escaping. Otherwise you'll have dead flies near and flying around the attractant. A single fruit fly will deposit hundreds of larvae on fruit and vegetables and existing food sources. Follow these steps : 1. Clean all surfaces daily with your typical all purpose cleaner (I use Fantastic with bleach) wherever you find these annoying bugs. For us this is primarily the kitchen in addition to the bathroom and TV room. REMEMBER THEY ARE ATTRACTED TO LIGHT AND 1 FLY WILL DEPOSIT EGGS THAT MATURE IN about 5 to7 DAYS. 2. Unfortunately surface cleaning every 1-2 days is necessary to eliminate flying flies you see and larva that will hatch that you can't. 3. Immediately thoroughly wash all fruit and vegetables (existing or new) and refrigerate. 4. Eliminate All food debris from kitchen counters, microwave, garbage cans, toaster oven, pet water and food bowls, etc. and keep everything covered or washed like used knives, glassware and plates. 5. If you think this is drastic like I did, then you will see an infestation that feels as if those little things won... 6. in the article, Good housekeeping recommended this product as using malic acid which is found in apple cider vinegar. It was part of discussion on using a DIY solution see ingredients above. Any solution must also contain a detergent which causes the flies eating on the attractant liguid to sink in the solution. They can't fly away and begin a new cycle of depositing eggs. At the beginning of the infestation I used the ingredients above along with standing/hanging yellow sticky fly paper I also bought from Amazon. I am very frustrated that I didn't do the research and work upfront instead of wasting money trying to find the non-existent magic bullet all summer!! As much as I love tasty vine ripened tomatoes and other summer fruit and vegetables, it's just not worth it. I think the high temperatures in July throughout the North East as well as nationaly/globally contributed to the problem experienced by me and almost everyone I know.

reviewTitle: Six Traps Set - One Fly Caught. 80 Bucks Wasted
reviewDescription: What I liked: It's easy to use. What I disliked: It arrived 2 days late. It doesn't work. It's a spill hazard. It's extremely expensive considering it's vinegar, water, fruit juice and soap. I paid $80 to catch one fly. That about sums it up. We get these fruit flies every year but this year is the worst. I've had the drains cleaned and treated professionally and I'm pouring drain cleaner and fly treatment down the drains daily. I was going to buy the decoy traps that look like an apple. I've used them before and they were great. I couldn't find any in stock, except for one that had bad reviews. Then there was Aunt Fannie's. There's no special formula even though Aunt Fannie's has a patent on their formula. It's different because first, it's not a decorative decoy trap that looks like an apple or mini pumpkin. Aunt Fannie's looks like a spice bottle. When I opened the cap, I expected to see some kind of funnel mechanism on top. That's how the other traps work. The flies are attracted by the sweet formula inside. They slide down the funnel right into the solution and can't get out. Aunt Fannie's just has a wide-mouth opening that can very easily spill. I placed 4 in my kitchen, one in the dining room and one in a bathroom - where the flies congregate as the directions tell you. I watched the flies swarm around the traps and sat back waiting for them to dive in and never check out. They landed on the bottles, walked around, climbed down inside, climbed back out, walked around a little more, then swarmed around. One little fly fell in and couldn't swim. So $80, several days of waiting, then placing these open bottles of liquid all over the place, and I caught one, single fly. Just one. I was convinced to buy this product because I saw so many great reviews. It seemed like a no-brainer when I couldn't find the product I actually wanted. I've never had this happen before. I usually buy stuff with 4 stars or better and when I do, I agree with those reviews. In this case, I was completely wrong and I've wasted time and money.

I purchased this because I saw the reviews. BUT it did not work for my house not one fruit fly met its death with this stuff. I was thinking maybe its because the opening is the size of the packaging. Most fly traps have tiny openings so they cant get back out. I purchased the apples ones as well. Once those were opened they went right to them. Lots of dead flies in those! So I believe this is a hit or miss for anyone who purchases.

I had this near my bunny cage where i often get fruit flies. I will say I poured a little in one of those tomato looking fruit fly cups and left this open to see if there was a difference because this doesn’t not have holes, just a plain open cap, neither collected flies, even days later. I will not purchase again. Does not work.

Aunt Fannie’s Flying Punch is a joke. Three bottles out for one month while fruit flies are doing summersaults around my face taunting me as they exclaim “I’m s that all you got?” Seriously don’t buy this. It doesn’t work. Just donate the money to someone who needs it on the street.

I haven’t caught not one gnat or fly I placed it on my kitchen sink counter around my plants! I have a zevo and sticky traps that work. Every thing catching them except for the punch I even sat it in the pot of one of my plants and still didn’t catch not one gnats

reviewTitle: Works terribly; less effective than DIY traps and smells terrible.
reviewDescription: My high rise condo is currently having trash chute issues, so I came home from a weekend trip to a fruit fly problem. I hastily bought this, a blue light/sticky trap, and placed out a few cups with old wine and a drop of dish soap. Left all of these out for 2 full days throughout the kitchen to eliminate my new roommates. The fruit flies are all now gone, but no thanks to this product (or the light trap, while I'm at it). The 2 cups of old wine, however, successfully caught and drowned dozens of fruit flies each. The light trap managed to catch one. This product caught zero. It also reeks of vinegar, strongly enough that I notice it immediately upon entering the apartment. I highly recommending skipping this entirely and putting out old wine or juice with dish soap instead, save your money and your sense of smell.

If I could rate this zero stars or negatives stars I would!!!! This sucks I have a fruit fly infestation I have let this out and the flies are going nowhere near it. It’s literally nothing but apple cider vinegar I coilda bought a bottle cheaper . I WANT MY MONEY BACK

I have never been as disappointed in something from Amazon as I was with this stuff! I had seen them mention it on a TV show. Im thinking maybe GMA...but anyway....so then i checked AZ and it had a ton of positive reviews so I just knew this was going to be the answer to our fruit fly problem. In another few days it will be an entire month that these 2 bottles have sat out in our house where we see these flies the most. Believe it or not....not one single fruit fly is in the liquid of either of these! We've stood and watched them fly to the bottle, and even down in it but pretty soon they fly right back out! Finally yesterday I decided to top each one of them off with a bit more apple cider vinegar..thinking POSSIBLY it hadnt been strong enough originally. NOPE! It's still not working. But yet there are still people saying it worked great for them. So Ive finally started a return today since I feel like Ive given it a decent chance with no success.

reviewTitle: Product does not deliver advertised results...
reviewDescription: I am not sure where they came from (or keep coming from), but I have a small swarm of fruit flies in my kitchen that I have been trying to get rid of for several weeks. I take my trash out daily, have cleaned my kitchen from top to bottom including the drains and trash can, no longer leave fruit (or any other kind of food) out on the counter, and have moved my cat's food and water dishes to another room. I also have tried several DIY natural remedies. Yet the fruit flies persist. I do not want to use an insecticide spray in my home because I have multiple chemical sensitivities. I read about Aunt Fannie's online -- the product got good reviews, so I thought it would be worth a try. The Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch! arrived and I put it out on the counter above where my kitchen trash can is and waited hopefully for it to work its magic. It has been five days and I am still waiting. In five days, Aunt Fannie's has trapped five fruit flies. In the same time period, I have swatted down 29 fruit flies and a sinkful of soapy water that I soaked a couple of pots in for an hour one day trapped seven fruit flies. I am really disappointed in this product -- it was definitely not worth the money. At this point, I am not sure what will work to rid my kitchen or fruit flies, but I feel fairly certain that it will not be Aunt Fannie's.

reviewTitle: It is a hit or miss product.
reviewDescription: This is a hit or miss product. It did not work for me at all. It's a cheap alternative but you risk not having the results you want. Strong vinegar smell. Wouldn't recommend.

reviewTitle: Just apple cider vinegar in a cup !!
reviewDescription: This is nothing more than apple cider vinegar in a cup! You can Google home fruit fly remedies, and they all suggest to put apples cider vinegar in a cup with A drop of dish soap so that the flies sink into the liquid. Once I open this product, it was clear that it is just apple Cider vinegar that you can put in a cup for less than a quarter. Yes, this is effective, but you can make yourself at home. Don’t spend six dollars on this.
I can't tell you if it works or not because the smell is awful. If you like the smell of vinegar airfreshener, then you'll be ok. The next day after opening it, I put the top back on!

I was expecting more for the extra money I spent on this Raid product. Like maybe a liquid product that wouldn't spill stuff all over when I opened it. When I unscrewed the lid, Raid had one of those protective plastic coverings over the opening of the jar that you have to peel back by gripping the extra piece of attached material that's supposed to make it easier to peel, but doesn't actually WORK since it's glued on so firmly. Oh well. OK. So I grabbed a sharp knife and carved away the temporary protective covering, which exposed the jar of apple cider vinegar that is said to kill the fruit flies. For the price, I expected another lid with holes in it to prevent it from spilling when I put it down near my compost pail. But no. It was just a jar of open liquid that had already spilled once when I carved away the protective covering! So I had to place the $8.18 jar of Raid in a sealed plastic sandwich bag with holes in it. I don't know yet how well it works. But here's the issue. If I wanted to create a DIY fruit fly disposal system, I would get my own jar of apple cider vinegar and put it in a sealed sandwich bag with holes in it. AND I would have saved myself $8.18! There are other, better designed fruit fly apple cider products on Amazon that have holes in the lids to prevent spillage. I just expected more from a big brand name like Raid. Silly me.

I bought this with the hopes of catching gnats. It only caught two. I watched the gnats just walk along the rim. I wouldn’t recommend it. I ended up doing baking soda and vinegar in my drain and that worked.

This was not as effective as advertised. It collected a small amount of fruit flies, but it also served as an attractant that they wouldn't touch. I had it open for 2 weeks and the amount of flies only got worse. At the end of the 2 weeks there were maybe 20 in the jar, and still dozens in the air. As soon as I closed the jar, they were gone in about a day. I would not recommend this item. It also smells awful- if using it indoors, prepare for your house to smell like vinegar-soaked work socks.

It makes your whole house smell like vinegar with a hint of sweetness. The smell is very strong and spread throughout the entire house overnight. I had to close it up and run a fan to get rid of the smell. It only caught one gnat overnight, even though there was a light trap in the same room plugged into an outlet (Zevo) that had just caught over 50 gnats in the previous 48 hours.

I have several apple cider vinegar/dish soap traps all around my house already. I bought this to take it up a notch because they aren't working! I had high hopes, until I opened the container. It smells just like apple cider vinegar. Very strong apple cider vinegar. I put it on the counter anyways, hoping there is some specific secret ingredient to attract and catch them. I have massive amounts of these awful annoying little bugs. 48 hours later and there are 2 in the Raid jar. I am so disappointed! Also, the liquid being completely open on my counter doesn't give me the best feeling. I feel like maybe a grid design on top or something to block spillage would be a good idea. I cannot recommend this product.
Draws little fruit flies. Had out on counter for a week but flies still around

I do not usually write reviews on products but felt compelled due to my disappointment in this. First- I have had this massive amount of fruit flies that have appeared out of nowhere. I ordered this, as I thought the reviews were great. When first opened, as other products said- wow! That is a strong odor that is not terribly welcoming. But I was thinking that I was more than willing to deal with it if it meant that I was fruit fly free. Sadly, those pesky bugs seemed to have outsmarted the trap and only a fraction of them have lost their life to this product. I somehow think (irrational and though it may be) that this product has somehow made them not only stronger but smarter. If you have a small amount of irritants, this may be helpful. But for a larger swarm, I am looking elsewhere.

The light on this does not attract bugs. I am having a fruit fly problem in my potted plants. I own a Zevo and it works wonderfully. So I figured I would try this much cheaper alternative. I put it out on the other side of the house, expecting to get the same results as the Zevo. I literally caught zero bugs the first night. The next night I switched locations. I put the INRAGEO in the location I had the Zevo previously, and the Zevo where I had plugged in the INRAGEO. The Zevo caught lots of bugs in the new location, and this one, again, caught zero. If I put them side by side, the light color is definitely different. So it could be that I got a faulty one.....but why chance it?

Unbelievable. These haven't caught one gnat, fly or anything else. The one I bought (same brand) from the store is full. I even moved them around to see if maybe it was a location issue, but that's not the problem. I just made a bad purchase. Buyer beware. I don't think these are the real deal.

reviewTitle: Doesn't attract insects like the brand name version. I would not purchase this again.
reviewDescription: Does not seem to attract bugs like the brand name system. My inlaws have the brand name system and it seems to really attract the small insects (including fruit flies). This one does not - even though to my eyes the blue light seems to be the same. I followed the directions and put it up the day we received it (3 November). It is in what we would call a 'high traffic' area for bugs. I just looked (9 Nov) and there is one (1) fruit fly on the sticky side while others fly around the area. Not what we had in mind. I would not purchase this again.

Would get zero stars,but not an option. Nothing but the smallest gnat in the world sticks to this. Have had plugged in for.2 weeks and there are 2 very small gnats on it. Trying to give it a chance, butttt

reviewTitle: Not real Ultra Violet completely ineffective
reviewDescription: I actually bought this by accident thinking it was the name brand version. When it failed to trap a single fruit fly in 72 hours I decided to see if the light being emitted is actually UV or just a blue LED bulb. I use black light extensively in my recording studio so I took some phospherous items from that area over to my kitchen and held it up to the fly trap. Other than being visiable in an otherwise pitch dark room the plastic of the dayglo items were non reactive. Scam.
Absolute garbage! Didn't catch/trap anything! Replaced it with a Zevo and the very next day the zevo had caught several mosquitos! Absolute waste of money, don't waste your time and money

reviewTitle: Two weeks without catching a single bug!
reviewDescription: Yeah, it was easy to set up, but I’ve had it plugged into my room for a couple weeks now, and it literally has not caught a single bug in it. There’s plenty of little moths, and mosquitoes flying around my room. Which was particularly why I had bought this item as when I searched the features. It said that it would catch mosquitoes. It does not. I would’ve given the zero stars if that had been an option.
This product worked well for 2 days and then quit working all together. It won’t turn on. Don’t buy this product, complete waste of money.
Had this plugged in for 3 days and does not work at all! We still have fruit flies flying around.

Doesn't work and definitely not woth the money. Been plugged in for over a week and had only 2 fruit flies in it. I can still see others flying everywhere in my house. Went and got apple cider vinegar instead and caught 4 in matter of 2 days. This was a huge waste of money. Avoid and go with another option.

reviewTitle: Didn’t work at all…. Perhaps it was the bug not the product?
reviewDescription: This is a long story. I bought canned cat food from Amazon, as usual I put it in the cupboard and went on with life. Fast forward, a few days later little bugs started flying around my kitchen, I thought they were fruit flies. For two days I put up with them then bought this. Basically this is apple cider vinegar mixed with sugar. You can make the recipe yourself, which I would do instead of buying this. Now, upon a week after the food was put in the cupboard I opened it and was attacked by these little bombers with wings. The gray of cat food was infested, hundreds with eggs. The can of food in the second tray which was covered with the standard cardboard and outer plastic was split which made a great environment for these little jerks. It took me a week to hand kill these things- thankfully they were slow. Perhaps they were not fruit flies and that is why this product didn’t work. This is not why I am giving it two stars. I am giving it 2 starts because of its application. Once the top is flipped open it is easy to tip over, it is not balanced. The top is completely open because the goal is for the flies to go in for a drink and drown. Well, unless you put this inside a mug it is very easy to accidentally tip over. The smell is nauseating, but that is not the fault of the product, sometime the “medicine” is just awful. I also think it is a little overpriced. $8. For ingredients that would cost at most $.75. I know it is the ease of use and when you are being overtaken by bugs you are willing to pay anything. I am giving it 3 stars because I am indifferent of its effectiveness. I am not sure if these were fruit flies- if they were, then this product didn’t work at all, if they weren’t then I am judging the other aspects of the product: ease of use, quality, cost. Etc. Quality: Nice looking label, The high pungent smell of vinegar is enough to make you gag- so, it is the real deal and not watered down. Ease of use: just peel off the thin protective film and flip open the lid- voila. Cost: I think it’s a little pricey for the ingredients which you could easily make yourself. (They give the recipe online) But, isn’t it easy to press the “buy now” button and have the delivery guy drop at your door 48 hrs later?

This stuff worked okay. Maybe some batches are better than others. I bought the two-pack and one seemed to attract a lot of gnats while the other caught very few gnats. I tried switching the placement to see if that helped but it didn’t seem to make much difference. It’s very easy to use-just open the package, flip open the lid and it’s ready to go. I’ve made a similar mixture before with comparable results so I probably won’t buy this again.

The product did not work for me, so I originally posted as one-star. Also, it is not eligible for return, so I was stuck with three jars. I am giving three stars now, because of the communication with Aunt Fannie's. I wrote to them hoping they could help with a return. Kelly wrote back promptly and said the return could be done only through Amazon. But, she was very friendly in e-mail and was sorry the product had not worked. She sent some questions and pictures, wondering if what I have is truly fruit flies. As it turned out and upon further research by me, what I seem to have are drain flies. I have eliminated them with baking soda/vinegar and also baking soda/essential oils. I would recommend to anyone that if FlyPunch is not working, consider another fly besides fruit flies. Also, it is worth writing to the company. I found Aunt Fannie's to be very helpful. Update 12/14/17: I have phorid flies, and they are very difficult to get rid of. My baking soda/vinegar/essential oils invention kept them at bay but didn't truly eliminate them. However, I have found a "trap" by happy accident: ruby red grapefruit juice. The little suckers are actually attracted to an open plastic glass filled with juice. It takes a while for them to figure out that they should jump right in, but they are. I am joyfully seeing dead bodies float. This, after going through frustration trying to kill them. I tried adding a drop or two of dishwashing liquid but am finding they are more attracted to just the juice alone. No ACV, orange juice, beer, or red wine for my visitors. Ruby red grapefruit juice. Who knew.

I really like this product when it works but it seems inconsistent. Some bottles I have purchased catch a zillion fruit flies and other bottles seem to be totally ineffective. Not sure what's happening there. Appreciate that there is no awful smell and the design doesn't both me sitting out in my kitchen.
I must have very smart fruit flies because I don't see this product to be very effective as of yet and it's been a couple of days

reviewTitle: Didn't seem to work as effective?
reviewDescription: I didn't feel that this fly trap got rid of all the flies. There were a few at the top, but noticed a lot of flies that managed to avoid being near this item. I have a second one that came with the purchase and will try again next summer if it works. The strong is very pungent fyi.

reviewTitle: Has an effect over time, just not immediately
reviewDescription: Smells like an open jar of red vinegar. No film to trap flying insects in the solution, They basically sit on top of the open jar & nothing precludes the insect from flying away. Maybe it intoxicates them & they fall in & drown. Eventually some bugs do wind up dead at the bottom of in the solution. I did notice a significant decrease in the number of small flying insects in 7-10 days; just not an immediate impact. Cats do not like the vinegar smell & leave it alone wherever you put it (off the floor).

Does it work? Yes. Do I still have the same type of flies that I had before? Yes, yes I do. Have I tried moving it around where I see flies? Yes, I have. If I had to guess I have 6 flies/gnats, and the bottle has 3 of them. So I gave it a 3/5 rating for getting me 50% of the flies.

Effective, but stinky! Got all the critters quickly, but it stinks up the kitchen with strong apple vinegar smell. I have used others before that did not make such a strong smell. Next time I will just buy the little apple shaped ones. Not a great choice in a house with sensitive nosed people, and the container is quite large, always afraid it will get knocked over.

reviewTitle: It has a smell that is a bit off-putting
reviewDescription: I only gave this a three because of the aroma. It does work.

Update earlier review....tried product a few more days & is attracting knats but not taking care of all of them. They are still flying around & not satisfied product works to eliminate. Will pay for product still not happy with purchase.
Was not impressed. Did not catch the flying moths. Was very unsatisfied.

reviewTitle: Worked great until it stopped working
reviewDescription: Worked great until it stopped working.

reviewTitle: Device seems to attract better than name brand, BUT bugs don't stick
reviewDescription: I found this device for a better price than the Zevo Max when I had 2 outbreaks away from wall plugs so needed the corded option. I actually think it ATTRACTS more bugs than the Zevo Max does in same location. Have tried knockoff brands for cartridges that were not as sticky as Zevo's, and definitely didn't work as well, so I expected that going in. THESE Inrageo cartridge however are bottom of the barrel. Had both cartridges that came with it in about 6 hours when my Zevo replacement cartridge order arrived. In that 6 hrs I saw many fruit flies attracted, but they could literally walk around on the sticky part. One cartridge had 2 tiny fruit flies on it, the other had 3 or 4. I replaced the cartridge with only 2 fruit flies caught with a Zevo refill cartridge. Photo shows the bugs caught in 24 hrs time on the Zevo side of device, and 30 hrs on the Inrageo side. There are FIVE on the Inrageo cartridge side, not even going to try to count fruit flies on the Zevo side, and also caught 2 house flies. So the cartridges are 0 stars. But I do like the device base itself, which is brighter than the Zevo and attracts more flies. It is also at least $10 cheaper than the Zevo Max, hence the 3 stars. So I would recommend the device, but know the sticky part is wholly ineffective, so also order Zevo refill cartridges for effectively trapping bugs for about the same price.
Doesn’t seem to attract house flies. Needs to be in kitchen in order to trap fruit flies.
They're good replacement when time to
Offbrand. Not the same as photo. Still seems to work!

reviewTitle: Fruit fleas not go thru most
reviewDescription: Fruit fleas not go thru
It’s not working as good as I figured it would. I’m a little disappointed.

I ordered the wrong one. The sticky cartridge does not fit securely in place like the other brand, it falls out easily when tipped over a little to far. It is triggering the sensor to turn on the blue light, which is good; time will tell how well it attracts and traps the flying insects, especially mosquitoes. Had a hard time turning the plug to keep from unplugging it everytime we have to open the mirrored door of our medicine cabinet. I will keep it as the only real issue for me is the secure fit of the sticky cartridge.

I must have very smart fruit flies because I don't see this product to be very effective as of yet and it's been a couple of days
