reviewTitle: It has a smell that is a bit off-putting
reviewDescription: I only gave this a three because of the aroma. It does work.

reviewTitle: So disappointed! No longer recommend. Seller never responded.
reviewDescription: FRUIT FLY SEASON IS RAPIDLY APPROACHING AND I'M DETERMINED TO PREVENT LAST SUMMER'S INFESTATION. Update : I contacted the company multiple ways and multiple times. Amazon and the company totally ignored my legitimate complaint. Obviously they don't care about their customers dissatisfaction. It's not about the money - it's about blatant misrepresentation and unethical behavior displayed by both companies. I couldn't even get Amazon to include the "frequently returned" box on the item's page. I bought three bottles of auntie's product on Amazon 6 days ago to replace my DIY liquid attractant of appl cider vinegar +brown sugar +dish detergent. It's been 5 days and can confirm the other negative reviews that the product CAN'T contain the same liquid attractant - the fruit flies feed on the liquid and are not trapped - they can fly away!!! I unintentionally performed a side by side experiment using this WORTHLESS product against my attractant. Both liquids were placed next to separate hanging cylinders of bright yellow sticky fly paper. I actually watched the flies fly from the auntie's product into my liquid. Unlike the auntie product, any flies that were not trapped in my DIY liquid were trapped in the fly paper. After 5 days I had to replace the fly paper adjacent to DIY liquid. in contrast, the 3 bottles of Auntie containing who knows what had A TOTAL OF LESS THAN 20 IN THE BOTTLES AND ABOUT 10 TO 20 TRAPPED BY THE FLY PAPER. I am so angry about wasting money on products from Amazon with good reviews. This product has overall rating of 3.8. But that rating is CUMULATIVE - The much older reviews are generally positive compared to more current negative reviews. I conclude this is due to a change in formulation that neither amazon or the seller made clear. I was so pleased that Good Housekeeping had recommended the best way to eliminates fruit fly infestations in a recent article. The GH panel incorrectly included this product as the only commercially effective liquid attractant since it contained malic acid as part of the formulation. The FRUIT FLY SEASON article made me remember that I had used Aunties successfully a few summers ago and loved their esthetics and effectiveness. I already emailed a request for them to provide evidence of its effectiveness using a recent bottle. I am requesting a refund from this seller based on my results and will update the status with both responses. Meanwhile if you are battling with fruit flies, infestations made worse by the higher temperature (I live in Connecticut) in the Northeast, read below. First understand that you must use the following steps throughout the fruit fly lifecycle (over several weeks so that the original fruit flies are trapped as well as the their progeny hatched from the hundreds of larvae deposited on whatever they fed on. {I am a biochemist and patent attorney so I needed to know what was going on}. Keep in mind for DIY solutions or other traps that you must attract and rid them from escaping. Otherwise you'll have dead flies near and flying around the attractant. A single fruit fly will deposit hundreds of larvae on fruit and vegetables and existing food sources. Follow these steps : 1. Clean all surfaces daily with your typical all purpose cleaner (I use Fantastic with bleach) wherever you find these annoying bugs. For us this is primarily the kitchen in addition to the bathroom and TV room. REMEMBER THEY ARE ATTRACTED TO LIGHT AND 1 FLY WILL DEPOSIT EGGS THAT MATURE IN about 5 to7 DAYS. 2. Unfortunately surface cleaning every 1-2 days is necessary to eliminate flying flies you see and larva that will hatch that you can't. 3. Immediately thoroughly wash all fruit and vegetables (existing or new) and refrigerate. 4. Eliminate All food debris from kitchen counters, microwave, garbage cans, toaster oven, pet water and food bowls, etc. and keep everything covered or washed like used knives, glassware and plates. 5. If you think this is drastic like I did, then you will see an infestation that feels as if those little things won... 6. in the article, Good housekeeping recommended this product as using malic acid which is found in apple cider vinegar. It was part of discussion on using a DIY solution see ingredients above. Any solution must also contain a detergent which causes the flies eating on the attractant liguid to sink in the solution. They can't fly away and begin a new cycle of depositing eggs. At the beginning of the infestation I used the ingredients above along with standing/hanging yellow sticky fly paper I also bought from Amazon. I am very frustrated that I didn't do the research and work upfront instead of wasting money trying to find the non-existent magic bullet all summer!! As much as I love tasty vine ripened tomatoes and other summer fruit and vegetables, it's just not worth it. I think the high temperatures in July throughout the North East as well as nationaly/globally contributed to the problem experienced by me and almost everyone I know.

reviewTitle: Six Traps Set - One Fly Caught. 80 Bucks Wasted
reviewDescription: What I liked: It's easy to use. What I disliked: It arrived 2 days late. It doesn't work. It's a spill hazard. It's extremely expensive considering it's vinegar, water, fruit juice and soap. I paid $80 to catch one fly. That about sums it up. We get these fruit flies every year but this year is the worst. I've had the drains cleaned and treated professionally and I'm pouring drain cleaner and fly treatment down the drains daily. I was going to buy the decoy traps that look like an apple. I've used them before and they were great. I couldn't find any in stock, except for one that had bad reviews. Then there was Aunt Fannie's. There's no special formula even though Aunt Fannie's has a patent on their formula. It's different because first, it's not a decorative decoy trap that looks like an apple or mini pumpkin. Aunt Fannie's looks like a spice bottle. When I opened the cap, I expected to see some kind of funnel mechanism on top. That's how the other traps work. The flies are attracted by the sweet formula inside. They slide down the funnel right into the solution and can't get out. Aunt Fannie's just has a wide-mouth opening that can very easily spill. I placed 4 in my kitchen, one in the dining room and one in a bathroom - where the flies congregate as the directions tell you. I watched the flies swarm around the traps and sat back waiting for them to dive in and never check out. They landed on the bottles, walked around, climbed down inside, climbed back out, walked around a little more, then swarmed around. One little fly fell in and couldn't swim. So $80, several days of waiting, then placing these open bottles of liquid all over the place, and I caught one, single fly. Just one. I was convinced to buy this product because I saw so many great reviews. It seemed like a no-brainer when I couldn't find the product I actually wanted. I've never had this happen before. I usually buy stuff with 4 stars or better and when I do, I agree with those reviews. In this case, I was completely wrong and I've wasted time and money.

I purchased this because I saw the reviews. BUT it did not work for my house not one fruit fly met its death with this stuff. I was thinking maybe its because the opening is the size of the packaging. Most fly traps have tiny openings so they cant get back out. I purchased the apples ones as well. Once those were opened they went right to them. Lots of dead flies in those! So I believe this is a hit or miss for anyone who purchases.

I had this near my bunny cage where i often get fruit flies. I will say I poured a little in one of those tomato looking fruit fly cups and left this open to see if there was a difference because this doesn’t not have holes, just a plain open cap, neither collected flies, even days later. I will not purchase again. Does not work.

Aunt Fannie’s Flying Punch is a joke. Three bottles out for one month while fruit flies are doing summersaults around my face taunting me as they exclaim “I’m s that all you got?” Seriously don’t buy this. It doesn’t work. Just donate the money to someone who needs it on the street.

I haven’t caught not one gnat or fly I placed it on my kitchen sink counter around my plants! I have a zevo and sticky traps that work. Every thing catching them except for the punch I even sat it in the pot of one of my plants and still didn’t catch not one gnats

reviewTitle: Works terribly; less effective than DIY traps and smells terrible.
reviewDescription: My high rise condo is currently having trash chute issues, so I came home from a weekend trip to a fruit fly problem. I hastily bought this, a blue light/sticky trap, and placed out a few cups with old wine and a drop of dish soap. Left all of these out for 2 full days throughout the kitchen to eliminate my new roommates. The fruit flies are all now gone, but no thanks to this product (or the light trap, while I'm at it). The 2 cups of old wine, however, successfully caught and drowned dozens of fruit flies each. The light trap managed to catch one. This product caught zero. It also reeks of vinegar, strongly enough that I notice it immediately upon entering the apartment. I highly recommending skipping this entirely and putting out old wine or juice with dish soap instead, save your money and your sense of smell.

If I could rate this zero stars or negatives stars I would!!!! This sucks I have a fruit fly infestation I have let this out and the flies are going nowhere near it. It’s literally nothing but apple cider vinegar I coilda bought a bottle cheaper . I WANT MY MONEY BACK

I have never been as disappointed in something from Amazon as I was with this stuff! I had seen them mention it on a TV show. Im thinking maybe GMA...but anyway....so then i checked AZ and it had a ton of positive reviews so I just knew this was going to be the answer to our fruit fly problem. In another few days it will be an entire month that these 2 bottles have sat out in our house where we see these flies the most. Believe it or not....not one single fruit fly is in the liquid of either of these! We've stood and watched them fly to the bottle, and even down in it but pretty soon they fly right back out! Finally yesterday I decided to top each one of them off with a bit more apple cider vinegar..thinking POSSIBLY it hadnt been strong enough originally. NOPE! It's still not working. But yet there are still people saying it worked great for them. So Ive finally started a return today since I feel like Ive given it a decent chance with no success.

reviewTitle: Product does not deliver advertised results...
reviewDescription: I am not sure where they came from (or keep coming from), but I have a small swarm of fruit flies in my kitchen that I have been trying to get rid of for several weeks. I take my trash out daily, have cleaned my kitchen from top to bottom including the drains and trash can, no longer leave fruit (or any other kind of food) out on the counter, and have moved my cat's food and water dishes to another room. I also have tried several DIY natural remedies. Yet the fruit flies persist. I do not want to use an insecticide spray in my home because I have multiple chemical sensitivities. I read about Aunt Fannie's online -- the product got good reviews, so I thought it would be worth a try. The Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch! arrived and I put it out on the counter above where my kitchen trash can is and waited hopefully for it to work its magic. It has been five days and I am still waiting. In five days, Aunt Fannie's has trapped five fruit flies. In the same time period, I have swatted down 29 fruit flies and a sinkful of soapy water that I soaked a couple of pots in for an hour one day trapped seven fruit flies. I am really disappointed in this product -- it was definitely not worth the money. At this point, I am not sure what will work to rid my kitchen or fruit flies, but I feel fairly certain that it will not be Aunt Fannie's.

I was originally going to write my review yesterday morning. I was going to give the product 3 stars. My original review was going to stat that the product was very sticky and had a good stick power so it worked great if the bugs got on it, but it has no attractant. When I hang fly tape in my house in spring the tape would literally be covered in 2 days. I hung this up for 2 weeks and had some bugs on it, but still tons of them sitting in my window. So unless they happened to touch the trap, it didn't really help. I was going to give it 3 stars because its sticky, just doesn't have an attracting power that I need in my old farm house in spring. Also, the little hanger it comes with is a super cheap flexible plastic, so you have to figure out something else to hang it. And since its so flexible you'd almost need to tie a wire or string around the trap itself because your can't trust the little hanger. Then, later that morning, I changed my mind to give it 1 star instead. The crappy little hanger it comes with gave out and the trap fell to the floor. My cat went to investigate, and got the trap stuck on him. When I found him the trap had been stuck across his mouth, the side of his face and neck, and his paw was stuck. He had been struggling to pull it off his face and hurt his face. I had to cut his fur off his face and paw to get the trap off and had to pull it off his mouth and it made his mouth bleed. I had to give him a bath in vinegar to get his one eye open and all the stick off his fur. I will stick with fly tape that attracts the bugs and pins to the ceiling. Plus when I hang fly tape, all the bugs are on it, there are never any still hanging around my window like with this thing. Plus for $1 more you get a box of 10 fly tapes instead of one trap.

reviewTitle: I don't know if Black Flag changed the way these are made or what but this was a waste of money
reviewDescription: I've used these before and while never great, and pretty gross, they're cheap, so I bought them again and this time it was WAY more difficult to open and I ended up scrubbing sticky goo off my hands for a good long while and then the roll did not simply unroll as I remembered; instead, it came out twisted and I had to fiddle with it for another 10 or 15 minutes which, given how it's sticky paper, was both challenging and unpleasant. But, I had a tool shed that had gotten, for some reason, a sudden swarm of flies so I was highly motivated to get it unrolled so I could install it inside. And I finally did, and I put it right in the middle of dozens and dozens of large flies and then shut the tool shed door. The next morning, the flies were nearly all gone but there was not a single one stuck to the trap. And every day for a week I checked and every day, I saw zero to a handful of flies but never one fly trapped on the paper. Mind you, this product does not repel flies, so their absence is not due to the item working. On the contrary, it's supposed to either attract or passively trap them. It does neither. In reviewing the description, I do not see any mention of any attractant, so I guess the idea is to capture flies by dumb luck, and I guess I am just unlucky. But given how difficult and unpleasant it was to unroll and install this, I would not do it again. Indeed, I threw the unused rolls away. I remember this product from many years ago and it was easy to use and moderately effective so I do not think this is the same fly trap Black Flag has made for decades, but something cheaper and less effective.

Cannot get product our of container. Fly paper (very sticky) rolled up tightly inside what looks like an old 3mm film vile. You cannot remove without: A getting sticky goo on your hands B product is twisted up and mangled upon removal. USELESS JUNK

Fly trap? No, no, no. What this really is is an object you'll find lying on the floor at some point. You'll see it from across the room and wonder, "What in the world is that white cardboard thing? Did someone make something out of posterboard and leave it laying around the house? Maybe a cardboard airplane?" You'll bend over to grab it, and your one of your fingers will invariably touch the sticky trap surface inside. You might try to stick it back to the window, but it didn't stick the first time when the adhesive was fresh, so it won't likely stick with a second attempt. These seem like a great idea, keeping the fly carcasses out of sight, but they just don't stick. These are the ones you really want! http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00AN8BWG0?redirect=true&ref_=cm_cr_ryp_prd_ttl_sol_56 They're clear, so they don't show until they start doing their job. If you have a BBQ, or the kids and their friends are in and out forgetting to close the door behind them, and lots of flies get in the house, they will eventually go to the windows to try to find their way out. They tend to start at the top of the window and work their way down, always ending up at the bottom. So if you don't have curious children in the house, you can mount these down low. I happen to have a very high arch window in the living room, and I put two of these on the bottom of that window, at each side, and it catches so many flies! I put them in lots of windows when in and out traffic is very heavy. It doesn't take long until we notice that we haven't seen any flies for a while. Simply pull the trap off, fold it in half, and toss it in the trash! It sure beats stinky chemical sprays! The only thing more fun for killing flies is the new and improved Bug Zapper! Five bucks at Walmart keeps the kids busy for a long time, especially outside at night near the porch light!
variant: Size: 1 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewDescription: No catchabilty. It’s fly season. I put three in the flies favorite sunny windows, after a week I got 3 flies. They sit on it, they don’t go in. I dropped in some bits of bread and honey and fried chicken. They don’t go in. I’m desperate, I cut one open, it’s only sticky on one side! I made a smorgasbord of tiny treats on the sticky side and put it on the kitchen counter. Nothing. It’s got an anti-fly something going on. The only thing that sort of works are those long sticky ribbons that are just disgusting. The reviews look like this window box has 4.2 stars. But it’s for all the fly catchers and wanna be fly catchers. Not the little triangular shaped box.
variant: Size: 1 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewTitle: Just buy an old fashioned fly swatter!!!
reviewDescription: I recently purchased the Black Flag Fly Stick Insect Trap with high hopes of ridding my home of pesky house flies. My door is frequently opened by my grandchildren, which unfortunately results in an influx of flies. I despise house flies, so I was looking for an effective, hands-off solution. After setting up the Fly Stick, I was disappointed to find that it only trapped three fruit flies over a period of two weeks. Despite being placed in a high-traffic area where flies are known to gather, the Fly Stick failed to capture the numerous house flies that constantly buzz around my home. The product did not meet my expectations. It was frustrating to realize that I had essentially wasted my money, as I still had to resort to manually chasing down and eliminating the flies myself. For anyone in a similar situation, I cannot recommend the Black Flag Fly Stick Insect Trap. It's ineffective for larger house flies and not worth the investment. In summary: Pros: Easy to set up. Cons: Ineffective against house flies, only trapped three fruit flies in two weeks, ultimately a waste of money. For those dealing with a significant house fly problem, I suggest exploring alternative solutions. This product, unfortunately, does not deliver on its promise.

So I'm sure there is a fly type out there that this product, when used as directed, works well for. The flies I had, it was irrelevant to them however. SO after a few days of having this repurposed paper napkin roll left out, with syrup in the bottom dish, i have caught a total of zero flies and one tiny gnat. Fly swatter i had managed to get all the flies over the course of owning this. It didnt work. One time a fly did land on the white top thing but it needs to land on the yellow tube for the glue to work. Now that the fly outbreak is resolved i have kept it out for the 0.00001% chance a random fly will decided to land on the glue....and maybe then...the 6 tubes for $18 will have been worth the price paid. Right now, i regret the order. Not only do i have tons of packaged unused rolls that ill never use, but it was 100% ineffective and unable to catch even a single fly when at the time the room being used was loaded with flies due to forgetting to take the trash out and leaving a window cracked when away for a long period of time. Even in this environment, a small room swarming with flies, it managed to catch nothing. You are better off opening a window, or, keeping a fly swatter in reach. I wouldnt waste your money unless you are sure you have flies that will care about putting syrup or other things flies typically like in the dish, and then have them land on the yellow glue tube.

The window strips seem to be just 2-sided pieces of scotch tape. Flies fly all around it and crawl around it and somehow never manage to crawl or land onto it. For all I know it might repeal flies. Sure they are cheap but the value is zero.
variant: Size: 1 pack, Style: Fly Window Strip

reviewTitle: Waste of money, not sticky enough, regular flies just hop right off it
reviewDescription: I bought a 6-pack of these sticky traps to put in my garage and by the trash can outside that gets a lot of flies buzzing around in the warm months. The trash can is close to the house so I don't want to use the stinky disposable bag kind. I've had 3 of them out for 2 days, in areas that are swarming with house and horse flies, and they've caught nothing but a few gnats, a couple tiny flies, and two little sweat bees. It's not that the bugs aren't landing on them, either. I literally watched a housefly land on it, struggle for a second, pull itself free and fly away! The picture was after 3 days, before the bottom fell off and the sticky part fell to the ground. I put a Rescue brand sticky trap in the exact same spot and it's FULL of flies after just 4 days. These are a total and absolute WASTE OF MONEY. These are going in the garbage and I'll pay more for the Rescue brand traps that bugs actually get stuck to.

Garbage. I would've been better off buying two 4-packs from the dollar store. Those have never failed me. These have been up over 6 hours and NOT ONE FLY has landed. They just fly around it. Maybe it's old and the scent has faded. There's no expiration date but the year 2017 is on bottom of box. That's probably when they were made. I doubt it has a 8 year shelf life. It's still sticky but doesn't work regardless. Amazon likes to sell expired/expiring products so that's probably why it's not working. And it came late. I had to spend an extra night trying to sleep in a fly infested room. The only good thing that came out of ordering this is, I realized how useless Prime is. So much for overnight and next day delivery. The shipping logistics aren't what they used to be!

I received this product today and upon opening it, it had already been used. I mean, I’m glad to see that the product works, but it was slightly upsetting for me to receive the package in a used condition. Aside from being grossed out, I’m hoping it’ll work the same way for me.
Looks nothing like the add. We'll see if it works - too soon to tell, but it's very cheaply made. Buy the name brand instead.
I didn’t pay attention that this wasn’t the Zevo brand. Initially I was going to keep it, but it’s large in size. I prefer the smaller profile of the Zevo.
I have 4 of these in my kitchen but I still have flying insects!

reviewTitle: Not as good as the VEVO original traps

reviewTitle: Not sure why the insects avoid this device.
reviewDescription: This trap does not attract any insects.

I have a Zevo 3 feet away from this one and it doesnt catch nearly as many fruit flies as the Zevo. Ive even swapped these to see if it was the position that was causing it. It was not. I would buy the Zevo again if i ever need another cuz its just way more effective. I mean like 5 times more effective.

reviewDescription: Veyofly catches about half as many flys as Zevo when placed adjacent to each other.
I have had it up for days and captured nothing.
It works well, not sure how many bugs it kills or maybe I just don’t have a ton at its location. I do find some so obviously it is effective and the quality seems good.

During the summer we occasionally have a fruit fly or two but never a huge issue until this year. Trying to find a solution that takes them completely out is hard to find so we started trying everything. We set out this light where they always hangout and after a couple weeks it’s only caught a maybe 10-15… not giving it one or two starts bc it’s done the job of attracting them and if you have a small issue but definitely recommend just doing dawn dish soap and apple cider vinegar in a shallow bowl is you have a major problem. The home remedy caught threes times as much as this trap.
We had such high hopes. It didn't work cery well for us. At the most we got a gnats. Wouldn't buy again.
After 3 days the trap caught 1 fruit fly, apple cider vinegar in a jar caught over 10.
Had them for a week and only got a few bugs.
Not many flies have been trapped by the light but the amount of mosquitos has diminished a bit it seems.

reviewTitle: Not very useful for capturing insects
reviewDescription: the glue doesn't really keep bugs stuck (moth and German cockroach both escaped after I saw them stuck). THe scent does not attract fruit flys, they seem to just ignore it.
Does not catch as many bugs as competitors units.

Purchased this and the fruit ninja liquid traps at the same time after 1 week the liquid trap had a ton in it this had 2 stuck to it literally 12 inches apart both by the sink sink flies are gone but not because of this.

reviewTitle: stupid bussiness or stupid rip off do not buy this iteam

I have had this in a heavy fly spot (there was some flooding) for like 2 weeks. I have filled a few fly tapes and there is still zero flies on this trap. I would have given a zero but not allowed.

This does not work. It's just a sticky trap with a blue light plugged into your wall. I only caught a few bugs on the sticky place. It's on the back of a square piece about 3 by 3 inches. It makes no sound waves or anything to encourage bugs to come to it. I wasted my money.

We have had some fruit fly issues. I purchased a 2 pack and installed them in close proximity to the issue and each other. Not a single fruit fly or any other flying insect have been caught by them. We have been joking about how ineffective they are, calling them our “fancy blue nightlights” as that is the only purpose they serve. I do not recommend buying these. They do not work at all.
I have not found veyofly trap to be effective at all.i am finding the cheaper sticky ribbons more effective.

This product is horrible. I never do reviews, literally ever. But I just had to warn people to not buy this. I had high hopes as I really had a bad gnat problem, but this didn’t even catch a single one of them and it’s been plugged in for four days if not longer. I’ve turned off all the lights around it, I even put an apple cider vinegar trap by the light, nothing helped. I got the box of two and put them both in two different locations where it should’ve helped, but it didn’t. Neither of the traps caught a single gnat. A waste of money.

I read other reviews of this product so I know it was probably not going to work but decided to give it a try.I have many fruitflies at the spot where I put this to the test. After two days, it had captured nothing. I then put my homemade trap of plastic cup with banana chunks, water and a little detergent covered with plastic wrap punctured with very small holes. After several hours, the cup had captured at least 20 flies. And the winner is - homemade!

reviewTitle: Not as good as the original
reviewDescription: Don’t like these as well as the one with the bulbs. The bulbs ones are amazing - these don’t work as well .

I went into this purchase in the hopes that I could get rid of the annoying little gnat and fruit flies that keep getting into my home office. I followed the directions to the letter. The product just did not work. The quality of the base unit is good and it even has a rotating two prong socket so you can have the unit mounted straight up, or to the side. Sadly, the sticky cards just did not entice those pesky flies, and I have to chase them down with a old fashioned fly swatter. I am mad at myself for wasting $20 on this purchase. I will not recommend this product to anyone. I called Amazon about my disappointment in the product, but was told that since I had opened the box, and I had opened one of the fly sticky packets, that I could not return the product. Lesson learned.

I saw so many positive reviews I was excited to get this product and finally find a way to get rid of my fruit flies. They landed near it and on other sticky yellow traps I have (took care of a few by hand), but not one found my Black Flag fly paper attractive enough to actually land on the four strips I've had hanging for the last two days. Disappointed! NOTE: Will come back to change my review if things change.

Back story: we had a sudden influx of aggressive flies that appeared after we had the gas company contractor came to repair a wall in our basement where the washer and meter were. They replaced the mercury regulator. I kept accusing the husband of leaving the door open for the cats too long. I asked our WhatsApp ladies in the cul de sac if they had any more flies than usual. None of them did. One did suggest that flies loved natural gas… while trying to figure out the source and deal with the problem I bought several of these because they were cheap and would arrive the next day. I hung it in our kitchen and filled it with syrup and the flies ignored it. My research also indicated that they might be drain flies… But none of them had the flight profile, wing shape that I could see when they alighted to say for sure. Inspiration struck and I poured boiling water down the basement drain that our washer drains into that also protects if the water heater leaks and that seemed to stem the influx of new flies. Also, I noticed more flies in my Zevo blue light traps and when viewed up close it does appear that we had drain flies. As others have said, gnats seem to love the yellow sticky sticks so I count that as a win. It catches the overflow from the Zevo. The yellow sticks are very sticky and have no odor like I remember the old fly paper wands. While it didn’t work for me that doesn’t mean it wouldn’t work for others.

We had an overabundance of flies this past summer. The flies did not seem to be attracted to this. I tired a different product and the flies piled up on that so fast I had to throw it away after two weeks. The other product was purchased at WalMart and was the rigid tube sticky fly paper. I would recommend that over this any day.

Since I don't have many places to hang these where I need them I like to keep them in the box with 3 sides open. Box flimsy and hard to cut to open the 3 sides. Don't catch as many flies like a different brand that actually has perforations to open 3 sides on the box. Other brand significantly more expensive but works better. Not going to mention other brand name but do a search and you will see it. Comes in 4 or 6 per box. Box mostly white and red in color.

I bought this to get rid of one fly. That pesky fly would buzz my head once or twice a day for several days. After it flew by, it would disappear. Normally I would hear it or see it again, but not this fly. No, this fly was crafty. After he buzzed my head, he'd be gone. I had a can of flying insect killer nearby, but by the time I grabbed it the fly would be gone for the day. This product arrived and I happily hung it up in a nice open area, in plain view of any fly. However, the fly ignored it. I don't know if it has an attractant built in, but whatever it was, my fly didn't care for it. After a few more days, my fly stopped coming. I assumed it had died somewhere. Then, after a two-day absence, my fly reappeared. This time I was able to grab the can of insect spray and locate the fly. He got a good dose and that ended the life of the fly. The Black Flag product waved the white flag and went into the trash, untouched by any flies. I mounted the fly's head on my wall as a trophy of our week long struggle.

reviewTitle: Super sticky, but didn’t catch a damn fly
reviewDescription: Bought this 6-pack because my dad leaves his doors open like he's trying to invite every fly in a 3-mile radius. Followed the instructions exactly and used high-quality Kirkland organic maple syrup as bait (because I'm fancy like that). After two days? Caught maybe six gnats. Not a single housefly, which is what I was actually trying to kill. Yeah, the stick is sticky—credit where it’s due—but that’s about all it has going for it. Without a real attractant included, this thing’s just a sad yellow pole waiting for bugs that don’t care. Unless you already have a killer attractant recipe or just enjoy watching flies ignore your traps, I wouldn’t recommend it.

I've tried putting them in the flight path of the flies that zip by this way and then that but they don't lite on it. I'm not sure they land anywhere; I can't even swat them. It's as if they're desperate to breed, not eat or rest and they just keep going until they run out of energy and die. I wish these would attract them but they don't seem to. Once I tried adding a bit of canned dog food to the cavity at the bottom. It may have helped a tiny bit but I'm not sure and I hate to have to add rotting dog food. :) I tried a Victor M380 Outdoor Reusable Fly Magnet once and that sure did attract flies. But it stank too much to use indoors. I've hung these between the lights in my grow tent. I may have caught two in as many weeks. I hung one on my back porch. That one has caught several flies but those weren't the ones that bother me. They drive my dog crazy just zipping by. I hate to use spray because I have to pick up water bowls, search for hidden treats, etc. but that's what I usually have to resort to. I was hoping if I gave these a second try they'd maybe work this time but no such luck.

reviewTitle: Very sticky, but flies ignore it
reviewDescription: The fly paper is easy to prep and is certainly sticky, but there doesn’t appear to be any embedded attractant. Flies simply do not care about the paper at all. I’m sure if a fly accidentally bumped into it, it wouldn’t be able to leave. I looked at the company’s other products and noticed that some say to apply syrup or honey to them (the product I bought had no such instructions) so I tried that. Still nothing. We have so many flies, so having 3 of these up for a couple of days with zero flies falling victim to the paper is surprising and disappointing. The fly paper does have a smell when you first open it, but it’s not terrible and I haven’t noticed it spreading to the rest of the room in any way. If the produced attracted flies in any way, I’m sure it would be an excellent product. But as it stands, it’s just useless.
Sticks to everything. Messy. Got two flys in a week!

My first time purchasing this fly paper. I had a problem with fruit flys in my apartment and I thought these would help. Definitely didn't help with the bugs but stuck to everything else, a mess waiting to happen. I'd say the consistency is something like a very thick sticky honey or like tree sap. I do not recommend this.

reviewTitle: but that sucker is on there good. So I rush him in the bathroom
reviewDescription: First off I'll tell you that the sticky part works. I found that out first hand when I placed it on the counter to grab honey to add in the little dish, and my cat decides to inspect this new thing on the counter. He touches it with his paw when my back is turned, FREAKS OUT, and then jumps, while the trap is stuck to his paw! The trap manages to get stuck on his back and I turn around and catch a glimpse of orange dash by with the trap attached. He's meowing the whole time and I'm freaking out because now he's pissed and is hissing and hiding and running. Eventually I catch him and try to take the trap off, but that sucker is on there good. So I rush him in the bathroom, place him in the tub, and this stupid cat jumps while I'm holding the trap! It comes off him, but chunks of glue is missing for parts of the trap and other parts of it have chunks of hair. I quickly grab him again in the tub and turn on the water. I don't want him to lick the remaining glue so I need to wash it off. Well. WARNING: IT DOESN'T WASH OFF. Freaking out, I lock him in the bathroom and Google what to do. It says use oil. So I grab some vegetable oil and rush back. My poor cat is sitting there meowing his little heart out. I grab him again, place him in the tub, and pour the oil over the glue spots. I begin massaging out the glue, his fur is all weird. And it starts to come out. Thank god. He doesn't let me finish washing out the oil before he runs. But we got it out and he's in no danger. So.. yeah. adding the honey sucked because it's a tiny little dish. And the little clip to hang it up is super small. But it came in fast and its super effective. I just don't think I'll ever buy more of these and try to find something ready to go.
Didn’t work

These are very sticky which is what everyone would want. The problem with mine was when I pulled out the top portion to extend the sticky trap it stuck to itself so much that it wouldn't expand like it was designed to do. So instead of a wider surface area I wound up with a long slender trap. Still works but I'm a bit disappointed that it's not working the way it should.

reviewTitle: Pretty disappointed. I really needed these to work.....
reviewDescription: In the Oklahoma area right now, the flies are so bad, it is like the one of the plagues from the Bible! Inside, outside, everywhere! Our neighbor got these and recommended them to us and after reading a lot of the reviews, I decided to give these type of "fly traps" a third try. I have used this type (not sure if they were the same brand as this) of trap 2 other times in my life and I was unhappy. They caught mostly gnat type guys and the tiniest little flying insects that I have never even seen before - if they were alive and flying around, they would be invisible to everyone around... Long review short..... When these tube traps were delivered this last week, I was so thankful and excited and put up 3 of them in the rooms where these diabolical kamikaze flies congregate... followed all the instructions.... They have been hanging just waiting to catch some flies but to No Avail! Not one actual Fly has been caught up on them..... And as I am inspecting them 5x a day, hoping to see a dead fly, I get dive bombed by the flies that are still alive in my house! Like they are taunting me! I am glad it seems to have worked for some of the other reviewers.... but these have not and are not working for me. I really wish they would work for these flies, because I am loosing my mind LOL Any advice is welcomed please! I tried to "ask a question" instead of leaving this review but I couldn't.
I hung it in the barn. A bird got stuck on it. It is very sticky and catches everything that lands on it!
Hard to open and unroll, must be a technique to it that I don't get, what I did get was sticky fingures that nail polish and alcohol could not remove.
Nasty looking but, this works great for the lil pesky ones! Good price shipping not bad

Really supper sticky - certainly will catch flying pests without lure, but nesting chickadees got stuck to traps & despite carefully bathing birds in olive oil - twice - with a rinse in soapy water (dish soap) birds died. Too many feathers were stuck on the trap. Perhaps others may want to know this trap can be harmful to birds.
Does not attract or kill flies. Very lower performance than the old one that I had. Sufficiently sticky.
They catch flys but it is real hit and miss. Bait doesn't work for me. Works best on its side and not vertical.

reviewTitle: Don't buy this. Make your own!
reviewDescription: Well, I got duped by this product. It's mostly my fault. I needed a way to get rid of fruit flies that made their way into my home. I had made my own trap with apple cider vinegar and dish soap, but the vinegar was really bad and stunk up the house. I searched for fruit fly traps and came across this. I gleaned over the description and didn't bother with the reviews (two things I usually never do) and ordered it. It arrived the next day (which is why I bought it) and I opened the package. Much to my surprise and disappointment, it was just a small bottle of apple cider vinegar with soap in it. Man, I felt dumb, but I decided to try it. The cap is a joke. It makes the bottle taller when open and easier to knock over so you can spill it everywhere. Also, it leaks if you close it and don't keep it upright. I poured some into a glass pudding bowl, covered it with plastic wrap and sealed it with a rubber band around the bowl. I poked four 3/8" holes in the wrap, set it out near where the flies are and left for a few minutes. When I came back the flies were all over the bowl. Once they make their way in through the holes they're essentially trapped. You will get the same results with apple cider vinegar and dish soap with this setup (because that's all it is) and over time it'll be cheaper. Don't buy this or any other similar brands. Just make your own trap and save the $8 that I stupidly spent.

reviewTitle: More still flying around than dying
reviewDescription: I've used Aunt Fanny's ant spray in the past with good results. So I thought, why not try the fruit fly solution for those pesky pests? Well, here's a good reason: it doesn't work very well. Sure, I see some floating in the liquid, but there are at least ten times that are still flying all around my kitchen. Then I decided to try one that sticks to the wall with good reviews. But those fell off the wall and I will return them. So I will buy the ones I should have all along, Terro traps. They worked great and I'm embarrassed that I bothered with anything else. I just wish Terro sold the liquid which how this all began.

I had a THICK CLOUD of fruit flies descend in my kitchen! Researching my options, I found this product & ordered a bottle. While I was desperately awaiting its arrival, however, I fell back on 2 older remedies—beer and apple cider vinegar. Maybe 1/8 of a cup of each of these were poured into separate clean cat food tins with some dish soap for viscosity, then covered with plastic wrap, and punctured with small holes. Once the product arrived, I could easily compare the attraction each liquid possessed. Beer? The winner! Apple cider vinegar? A very close second! Lagging way WAY behind is this product. Granted, it’s got a small number of snagged victims, but nothing like the other two. Save yourself some money. You also get the freebie bonus of finishing off the beer that didn’t get poured into the can!
Doesn’t work any better than anything else, which isn’t saying much

This item did not work for me. We followed the instructions and while it did catch some fruit flies, they mostly just hung around the open lid and didn’t actually go into the container. I feel like the design is a bit flawed with that because if the flys go in there is nothing trapping them and they can just fly out. It is easy to use but not very effective. I think if you poured the liquid into something that would actually catch the flys themselves it would work great but again I think the design is a bit flawed.
Items arrived leaking, with one container's top fully off and the other half off and the plastic wrapping the two torn. The fruit flies were having a field day on my counter!

Don’t waste your money. Good old apple cider vinegar works better. Pour 1/4c of acv into a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap and poke small holes in the plastic. It actually works better and doesn’t emit any smell like this product.

Gnats (fruit flies) just hang around the liquid but don't attempt to get in. I suggest the company to make these containers very small with a sticky plastic inside so they can get trapped. The idea is great but the designed is terrible. I don't know what to do with it but I am probably just going to have to throw it away unless the company offers a refund.
Two of the four bottles had loose lids and leaked. Waste of my time and money. Need to return.
Didn't work well. Strong smell

I have bought lots of these types of products in the past, so I may be a little extra critical. These work just as well as any and come with all the extras I like (the green sticks to stick them in the dirt are my favorite). I like that they don't have the triangle under the shape - that never works for sticking it in the dirt and look silly if you would rather hang them. The little shovel is worthless and they should save the plastic. I bought this set because my previous favorites are no longer available and these seemed to be the closest thing. What makes them not as good - the paper is MUCH harder to get off these! I hate fighting with them and getting them stuck to my fingers. The shapes aren't as cute as those I've had before. The mushroom and bird are fine, the bug and butterfly are from a strange angle and look weird. OK, but I'll look for something else next time.

reviewTitle: Not impressed, too big for smaller-sized plants
reviewDescription: The sticky traps caught a few fruit flies, but they do not work as good as the liquid traps. We put them all around the house and most of them have not caught anything yet. The metal tabletop stands it came with also tip over easily and the traps themselves are a lot bigger than I thought they would be. Not a good choice if you have smaller plants.
Some worked some didn’t. Maybe my fruit flips/gnat were on to it lol

The starburst was fine; the butterfly set was not. Something wrong with each butterfly sticker. The paper could not be peeled off as easily. The backing paper shredded when trying to peel it off, and glued itself to the sticker. We're they old? We're they heated and cooled? Something wasn't right. So, half the order was not good.
This product is ok.. the fruit fly take there time to stick

Sticky traps might do well for a sick houseplant...but they were powerless in the face of a major infestation. I had bugs all over my kitchen cabinets, walls and ceiling due to the compost container not be emptied in a timely way. The flies did not seem to be attracted to the trap at all unless it was in a spot where large numbers of them had gathered, and even then the traffic was light. Many of the traps had no customers at all. In fact I actually watched some of the flies check the trap out and then go around it....so they are not stupid! But what DID work, and work very quickly, was my vacuum cleaner. My little Hoover sucked those puppies up without mercy, after which I merely changed the bag. It has taken several sessions to really get them all, and I still have to treat my plants and the drain (where they lay their eggs) to keep from being reinfested, but this is soooo much better than resorting to chemicals or waiting forever for them to throw themselves onto a sticky trap because that was not happening.
This product is not as attracting as I thought it would be.

Does what it’s supposed to do. However, the complex shapes make it so the paper rips very easily when you try to peel it off. I took these photos after *carefully* peeling. Definitely needs a better design, I end up having to use my finger nails to pry most of the paper off and get sticky every single time. (Second photo is the point I give up and just stick them in my plants). Will look for more basic shapes in the future.

There are three traps in the photo attached. The one to the far right is completely bugs, however the other two are dirt. I don't know how or why but all of the traps pictured either one side or both side are completely covered in dirt, making them ineffective. I've replaced these three times and while they do catch some bugs, my plants are still covered in bugs. I had to use both these and bug spray, which I was trying to avoid at all cost, but as you can see using the traps alone my plants are still suffering from the bugs. As for the traps themselves, they are sticky but they are not sturdy. I know a large complaint is the shovel. I didn't agree originally with that being a waste of plastic, I thought it was super cute and would be so helpful, but your finger is much more effective for digging up dirt than the shovel is. It's like taking a plastic spoon and trying to dig but the end pops off.

reviewTitle: These will trap the white flies but I don't believe it attracts them.
reviewDescription: I bought these for a white fly infestation on my indoor Hibiscus. They are not easy to assemble..which is silly because it is a simple design. I didn't find the flies attracted to them but by shaking the plant branches the flies swarmed out and waving the sticky traps around them caught many. I'll be using a pesticide when I bring the plants back in for the winter.

reviewTitle: Quality went down!!!! Holes and only one sticky side
reviewDescription: First few times ordering from this company, the fly sticky traps were great! On our last order all of the fly traps came with a small hole on the top which wasn’t a deal breaker just not great. But about 1/2 of the traps came with only one side sticky! I didn’t notice it until I used a few already or I would’ve returned them. I don’t recommend this company… obviously the quality control went down dramatically.

These did not catch a *single* fruit fly for me. I set them up in every room. Even tried putting the well-known vinegar concoction next to them. I don’t know what I could have done wrong or if my fruit flies are of a different breed lmao Two stars for the cute packaging though lol and ease of use

reviewTitle: Don’t waste your money on “accidentally trapping” flies.
reviewDescription: I have over two dozen indoor pools plants, seedlings and herbs. I also have a compost can nearby. Fruit flies everywhere! (We also live on a hobby farm and have an abundance of peaches, pears, grapes, figs and muscadines. I strategically placed 6 of these and after a week I had about six flies trapped. I purchased a “bait-based” trap and after 3 days had about a dozen and no new ones On this product. Sorry- I liked the idea but the function doesn’t work.
I had these all over the house and caught maybe three fruit flies. They totally ignored this item.
It's nearly impossible to take off the covering as the sticky has hardened. Some the cover won't come off at all. Either old or overheated. I've never had this problem before with these type.

reviewTitle: Doesn't work as well as other brands
reviewDescription: This trap only caught 3 young gnats. As you can see in the picture, the other brand I have on the right (Safer is the name) caught SO many more.

This does not catch nearly as many bugs as the brand I had before it. I got this one because it came with better sticks and holders, which are nice but the sticky traps themselves don’t work as well for me sadly. Also harder to get the paper off them.

reviewTitle: Use as a tester NOT as an eliminator - Diatomaceous Earth
reviewDescription: With 22 house plants had a major infestation and these traps were catching and killing hundreds, but not putting a dent in their proliferation. diatomaceous earth completely eliminates these bugs. After months of filling these sticky pads a friend turned me on to the Diatomaceous Earth, watched a YouTube video, dusted the top of all the plants soil, and less than one week later "poof!" No more gross bugs. These traps are great to see if they are present, but Diatomaceous earth is a game changer.

I have purchased a different brand in the past and thought this was a good deal....more for my money. However, each flower/butterfly sticky is covered with a protective paper on both sides. Great idea, I thought. No....... some paper pull off easily and others come off partially. It defeats the purpose of a sticky patch when it’s covered in paper that won’t come off. Even when I tried to scrape it off with a fingernail, my hands were so sticky I couldn’t do anything. I’ll gladly spend more money on the other brand
half of the gnats just ran across it. it did catch some but very hit and miss.

Serious design flaw! All the gnat traps I've used in the past had one portion, the part you stick in the soil, that was NOT sticky. These are sticky all over so are almost impossible to peel. I couldn't get the cover paper loose because there was nowhere to get it started. It took me several minutes per piece to peel them! Other brands take a few seconds. They ARE very sticky and started collecting gnats right away. But they're so sticky they stuck hard to my hands and destroyed any plant leaves they touched. Because I already used them to discover how bad they are, I can't return them so they're going straight in the trash. I wouldn't want to subject anyone else to them so am not even giving them to Good Will. If I could give negative stars I would, just for the aggravation factor.

reviewTitle: Did nothing to help us get rid of fruit flies
reviewDescription: Our fruit flies conveniently flew by it, sat near it and had a great time without being bothered by this product. I don't know how it worked for other people but for us, not even 1 fruit fly sat on it.

reviewTitle: Did not catch a single fruit fly, unfortunately
reviewDescription: Only bought it to combat the fruit flies in the kitchen a few months back. NONE were interested in these patches, we tried for weeks, unfortunately. These have no smell at all, chemical or otherwise. I do not know how these perform with gnat though.

reviewTitle: They no longer work. Do not buy.
reviewDescription: I have bought these for several years, and they worked well for trapping fruit flies. I started having a problem with the flies again, so I ordered a large 120 pack to have plenty on hand. Well, I completely wasted my money because they no longer work! The fruit flies will land on them and then just fly off because they aren't sticky enough. They have made these extremely cheap now, and they are a total waste of money I would give them zero stars if I could.
It’s sticky but somehow fruit flies do not stick to it

reviewTitle: Not worth the cardboard it's made of
reviewDescription: The original items I bought several years ago were large bright yellow recangles with a sticky coating. They weren't cute or pretty, but they really cought the meal moths. This time what I recieved was a pack of flower shaped 2" circles placed on a triangle like a cutsey flower. They are next to impossible to stick on the plastic sticks they included and are just plain useless. Absolute rubbish plus the plastic sticks don't look to be recyclable. I'll figure out how to use them because it's what I have, but don't make my mistake

reviewTitle: DOES NOT WORK ON FRUIT FLIES. PRODUCT IS A JOKE!
reviewDescription: DOES NOT WORK ON FRUIT FLIES. DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. I HAVE SWARMS OF FRUIT FLIES AND NOT A SINGLE ONE WAS CAUGHT ON THE STICKY TRAP. I HAD TO MANUALLY PUSH A FEW FLIES INTO THE STICKY GLUE AS THEY WERE SITTING ON THE VERTICAL EDGE OF PRODUCT. THEY WERE LITERALLY USING IT AS A HANG OUT...SITTING ON THE EDGE AND FLYING AWAY. THIS PRODUCT IS A JOKE!!! DO NOT BUY!!!!!!!

I bought these because I've been inundated with those little fruit flies. Many people said they worked great in the reviews, but for me, there are maybe a handful of flies on the 6 traps that I put on over 3 weeks ago. They don't work for me
Useless for fruit flies. Placed these around the house and no fruit flies got stuck on them.
WORTHLESS!! PUT 7 OF THE OUT NOT ONE HAS CAUGHT ONE BUG! NOTHING!

When you unroll these by pulling them down, they won’t stay extended. As soon as you let go, they curl right back up. I tried using packing tape and attaching a screwdriver for a little extra weight to keep it straight, but after about a day, even that small amount of weight caused the hanging hoop to separate from the strip and fall to the ground. Unfortunately, the design just doesn’t hold up under even minimal tension. I won't buy them again! Don't waste your money!

No matter how slow you attempt to open, or how you twist it to avoid tearing, it seems like all of the glue is only on one part of the trap and it’s hard to get open. If you manage to get it open the bottom, that has NO glue, tears or the entire thing rips in half. Very cheaply made, even for fly ribbons

The first time I bought a brand name fly strip and I regret it. The first one didn’t pull out properly didn’t separate. Had to throw that away. The second one did not separate properly, but at least it was somewhat usable from now on I’m going to use off brand fly strips because apparently buying brand new means nothing now
I hung this in the kitchen where more houseflies are near. I did NOT catch even one single fly with this. I took it down after a few months. Did the company test this before they sell?

Don't waste your money. I put it up a week ago and still nothing. I realize it doesn't specifically say it's good for gnats, but I thought I would try it since nothing else I have tried seems to work. (and, yes, I have tried apple cider vinegar and a drop of soap.)
They do not attract flies, been hanging for 3 days and not one fly on it but they are flying all around the magnets and my garage 😡

reviewTitle: Did not work for catching flies.
reviewDescription: Could not catch a fly if you gave it directions. It was a waste of money.
TERRIBLE PRODUCT! IT IS SOOOOO OLD THAT YOU CANNOT EVEN GET THE STICKY PART OUT OF THE CONTAINER

I saw mixed reviews so I tried it. Usually they suck with big flys.Flys.. but gnats are usually dumb and you can always catch those!.. Nope! I put one up in the afternoon.. 2 actually in the high traffic parts.. and we have TONS of gnats. I saw not only saw no Flys on it hours later.. but I saw lots of Flys get real close like they were gonna land.. then sped off away from it fast! They must not like something on here.. they should be attracting them not chasing them away! Sigh. I woke up the next mng thinking maybe I need to give it a night where ppl aren't walking around and be patient.. NOPE. not one single gnat. They were chillin ALL around it! Smh like they knew.. lol now after I returned it the next day... I looked and saw 3 lol and no more since then. That was yesterday. STILL I could have killed three with my hands.. that's how I've been killing them.. lol so this is pointless! (Tip ❤) when you wanna catch a gnat with your hands and kill it that way.. with a clap.. get your hands wet with lots of water! When they feel wind they fly off. When they feel water droplets pulling them in.. They are trapped! 😉) works like a CHARM

reviewTitle: this cannot be set up by one person. the tack they come with was totally uselesst
reviewDescription: this is impossible to set up by one person. you have to twist it flat, which i understand, but it is impossible to do alone. every time the end slipped out of your hand, it would spring back, and you would lose just about all of the progress you had made unwinding. thus they are not able to be used just hanging from anything, as springing back, you just had a short tight curl, instead of a hanging strip. the tacks for the end that they sent were absoluty useless, and i tried to just stick the end of the strip to the wall and it would not stick, meanning that end had to be held in one arm while the o keep hold of the first end to be able. i am now waiting for another delivery from Amazon for something to tack the ends of these things to top of doorway, or across open areaways where they would actually have the opportunity to perform the job they were bought for intending to do. and, in touching the tape with your fingers, it took me an extremely long time to get my fingers to get unsticky. numerous washes, and rubbing. if i am able to finally be able to use these things, I will get back to you with an update.
I placed these all over the place, and they did catch fruit flies, but did not make much difference in getting rid of them. I’m still fighting the situation..

reviewTitle: One package was great, the second one was very difficult to peel off the paper.
reviewDescription: One of the packages was very difficult to peel the paper off of the plastic. Had to try and it took multiple tries w/ small amount of paper coming off each time. The second package worked fine
Wasn’t impressed. Cheap enough but not really needed for plants. However did catch a few flies & gnats, I sat up around fresh veggies brought in side from garden.
It did not meet its specified features did not capture any fruit flies
You think if you put enough out you should get a few flies but no
I would NEVER buy these again. Too sticky. EVERYTHING will stick to it. Bees, flowers, leaves, fingers and lizards. I feel so bad about the lizard. :(

reviewTitle: Doesn’t catch flies or any flying insect
reviewDescription: Useless. Doesn’t catch flies or any flying insect
I guess if insects fly into it by accident, they'll stick

reviewTitle: Very Flimsy And Paper Hard To Remove
reviewDescription: The paper is extremely hard to remove the trap structure is very flimsy. Will not buy again.

reviewTitle: I my opinion, they are difficult to work with.
reviewDescription: I do not think that they work any better that the easier to work with products.
Bugs flew everywhere in my house except onto these traps. The only thing that stuck to them was my husband's fingers, my cat's paw, and the rice I used to stand them up in a bowl.
These did not work at all. Maybe others have bad luck. I do not recommend.

reviewTitle: They do not work very well.
reviewDescription: These fruit fly sticky traps do not catch many of my fruit flies. I have had them out for 3 or 4 weeks and they have only captured 6 flies and they are placed within inches of where I ripen my tomatoes and figs. I still have fruit flies getting into everything. The little clear plastic cups with liquid bait work much better even though they can be messy. I would not recommend this product.
This one does not work well (only a few bugs stick on it) as compared to the one I used before. I have many indoor plants and there are many small black bugs fly in my house.

These are cute and easy to use, and feel very sticky. But unfortunately the bugs just didn’t care about them. I bought these after a new houseplant turned out to have aphids (shudder!) and I was trying to save it with both liquid treatments and these. But the aphids just ignored the yellow things entirely, even after trying with a new one several times. Keeping on hand in case of other pests, but not expecting much.

The product I selected for color said beige. It is not beige it is yellow. Please correct your selections I thought the picture was just a general picture and yellow being one of the colors not that they put yellow for beige. My Knat problem was too big to send back and wait for a new order. I really dislike the yellow and it clashes with my plants and walls.

reviewTitle: Not really doing what I wanted
reviewDescription: I really wanted something that would attract / catch annoying house flies. I'm not an expert on species of flies. It doesn't advertise "house flies" so I guess I can't be to disappointed. It is sticky, I guess it would catch anything that landed on it. The problem is if it doesn't attract the flies, the likelihood it will catch anything is very slim :-(

While I maybe caught two or three gnats with these, I had them placed in various areas of my house and could not tell that they did anything. No trace of gnats being trapped on them and the gnat population did not decrease. Would not buy again.
Unfortunately this was a gimmick. I’ve had them for weeks now and the most they have attracted are 6 gnats. Not the miracle I was hoping for, very disappointing.

These are the worst! Yes, they are super sticky but getting them into soil is near impossible. Even after watering to soften the soil, these do not want to stick into the ground. The adhesive got all over the place and tore up some of my plants when they got stuck too. I ended up cutting these and putting them around the rim of my pots. Very disappointed.
The quality in the thickness of plastic, stickiness & ease of removing covering of sticky pads was disappointing

reviewTitle: Don’t work, glue is not strong, bugs still can get out from the glue
reviewDescription: Sticker ( glue) is very poor

reviewTitle: Gnat flies stick to it but doesn't look like it's on purpose.
reviewDescription: If flies pass by they stick but they don't seem to be attracted to it.

reviewTitle: I'll have to wait until it gets warmer to see if they work
reviewDescription: They're in every house plant, I have gnat but none are on the sticky traps
Gnats are not attracted
It's overall usage was not effective enough for me
It works
These were NOT very sticky and the bugs escaped.

reviewTitle: Things are good but the effect is mediocre
reviewDescription: Because I was too eager to clean up those flying insects and didn't take pictures. I thought this post could attract those flying insects, but in fact it was just the flying insects hitting it. I put a glass of water next to the flowerpot to have the same effect and even efficiency. Higher, which disappoints me.

I had lots of fruit flies after bringing in tomatoes from the garden before frost. Suddenly had all the flies so I ordered these traps because of appearance and description. Fruit flies were still all around but not attracted to the traps. I placed them in the kitchen and in some plants. Wish flies were more attracted to the traps!

reviewTitle: Save your money - cheap wine and suds are far better
reviewDescription: I wanted to like the ease of use and disposability of this product, but it simply did not work and in my experience, was poorly designed. Like most fruit fly-killing concoctions, it had vinegar and dish soap. But judging by the scent, that was about it. No sweetness I could detect (admittedly I don’t know what they are attracted to precisely).Judging by the way they swarmed fruit juice or wine, that seems like a requirement. Also, while the slim container is convenient, a short open glass provided significantly more surface area, which presumably made it easier for them to land. Bottom line, the side by side trial I ran with a glass of red wine, white wine and this product. No comparison. Wines in a landslide of fly carcasses.

This product does work, sort of, for attracting fruit flies. However for the price, you can do just as well to buy the little red plastic apple baits at the grocery. Even better, just use cider vinegar in a trap, because that's basically what this is. I use it as an attractant for a heavy duty fly stick, but red wine or cider vinegar work just as well. I didn't actually catch a whole lot of flies in the little jars.
Not as powerful as making home remedy

This has been a particularly bad year for fruit flies and gnats down South, and so I needed a little extra help. I love Aunt Fannie's household products, but this one is not one that I will purchase again. The solution inside these containers do kill the pests, but it also attracts them. For every 10 little darlings floating in the bottle, there are 20 more buzzing and crawling around the container, which defeats the purpose. Also, the open container design emits a very strong vinegar odor throughout the house...which I suppose is better than a chemical smell, but it still is unpleasant. The open container design also invites spillage is accidentally knocked over, which is easy to do when participating in food prep.

I wanted this product to work on my influx of tiny flying insects. Called fruit flies. We found them moving up & reproducing. This product didn't work well for us. Thank you!. I so wanted to be successful . I wanted to it to make iyy outdoor

reviewTitle: So far, the flies are not going for it.
reviewDescription: The products was supposed to attract the flies and kill them, right? Well the flies are still buzzing around happily. If however, the product was meant to be Birth control for flies, its efficacy remains to be seen I suppose.

I can’t speak to how well this works but I can tell you almost everyone of the 6 I bought leaked. I could smell vinegar before I opened the box, when I got into it the box was wet as were each of the jars. Every lid was loose and even with an inner seal they had all leaked. What a mess, had to clean them up before even trying to use them. I can’t imagine ordering any more.

reviewTitle: I'm beginning to think it's attracting more bugs, rather than eliminating them.
reviewDescription: I bought two, and when I open the lid, I get lots of little fruit flies around the jars -- and most especially on the lid rather than inside the jars. It seems to attract more fruit flies than I saw initially. I tend to wonder if flies from elsewhere (outside?) are finding their way inside to get to these little jars. I have cleaned and cleaned, and I can't seem to find a source of these little bugs. I have no fruit sitting out, or any other exposed food. I'm wondering if they're coming from the kitchen drain, so I bought some stuff to pour down the drain that's supposed to work for killing a variety of flies, but it hasn't arrived yet. So the fight continues... sooner or later, I'll rid myself of these little buggers.

reviewTitle: We had high hopes this would be the answer
reviewDescription: Our office goes through periods where we seem to be deviled by very small flying insects, they could be gnats or fruit flies, we don't know for sure but this product has the ingredients that are recommended to work on either. We have never found a dead insect in the bottles and we still had the problem on going. We didn't have that many but they were persistent and annoying. We are in a lull currently and I'd love to say it was this product but neither of the two bottles I bought have shown any dead in them so so far, we'd have to say they didn't work for us.

This product didn't work as advertised. I had ordered another similar item and also used a "homemade" solution of apple cider vinegar and a couple of drops of dish soap. The other approaches worked well, trapping several dozen fruit flies/vinegar flies. This only trapped three.

reviewTitle: I'm Not Sure Why There's a Lid
reviewDescription: The lid seems to just be there for cosmetic effect. It closes, it seems secure, but you can still smell the vinegar coming through. Turn the bottle upside down, and it leaks out pretty heavily. I discovered this flaw after my cat knocked the bottle over and, closed lid and all, about 1/4th of the fluid leaked out everywhere. I'm sure it works well as a product, but a product you can't secure and will you can smell through the lid isn't great. I don't know if it's jut my lid that's the problem, but the packaging seemed to have quite a few flaws.

The first time I ever used this product it worked great! So I decided to order it again, and now it lo longer works. It hasn’t caught one fly at all! Not at home nor at work. Disappointed. And it evaporates way quicker than the original!

Although generous amount of liquid and super simple to set up, it just isn't doing the job. These are definitely fruitflies & this product isn't working well. After setting next to kitchen sink there's about 10 dead ff in the container, but at least 50 still flying around. Last year when I had fruit flies I microwaved to warm up a bit of apple juice & set that out & the ff loved it, but wanted something easy so tried this Aunt Fannies. I believe the liquid is basically vinegar with some sort of dish soap in it. I can't recommend this product to others.
...and around, JUST NOT INSIDE!! Ha! It definitely attracts fruit flies but they just refuse to fly into the stuff. I did better with cider vinegar and soap.

I hoped that this product would help, but the fruit flies were not even attracted to it. The smell is horrible. It's vinegar. But I wish I could say it worked. But it didn't. I have more luck with the sticky flower pads, they work wonders and catch many fruit flies.

Ok, so I was so hopeful when I saw the reviews for this. We had a fruit fly infestation for a few days, i managed to get it mostly under control (thanks to my dyson and quick reflexes) with just some lingering fruit flies. I would see maybe 3-5 at a time. I put out this solution as directions stated for 5 days… not ONE fly succumbed to this. Very disappointing. Continuing to hunt these things with my dyson, down to about 1-2 a day now. Wish me luck! And don’t waste your money on this

reviewTitle: Save your money make your own for about 2 CENTS
reviewDescription: Apple cider vinegar & a few drops of dish soap is cheaper and exactly the same as this. The active ingredients are malic acid and laureth sulfate. I did a direct comparison a few drops of dish soap in apple cider vinegar gently stir about 2 seconds same smell, consistency and performance. It is effective at reducing the number of fruit flies, but will never eliminate them completely without removing garbage and cleaning the area. But do not buy, make your own for about 2 cents and a spare jar or bowl

I didn’t realize when I bought this that it was basically vinegar in a plastic bottle. I had been using the plastic apples with apple cider vinegar in it and it was catching the fruit flies but I was looking for something different, something better. I saw this and thought that I’d try it but it was pretty much the same as what I already had been using. It hasn’t caught any fruit flies and it’s been a few days. I’m afraid that the plastic bottle will topple over and spill vinegar so I put it in the window sill. I hope it works for others, it just hasn’t worked for me.
Does not work. Useless

I'd say there were about 40 or 50 fruit flies trapped in it but that's about it and I had about 100 fruit flies it did somewhat work but but only enough to make a slight impact on my fruit fly infestation.

reviewTitle: Works but deploying was hit or miss
reviewDescription: Works well if you’re able to deploy it correctly. Half of the ones I attempted to deploy fell apart as I was trying to pull the sticky strip out of the cardboard outer lining.
Doesn't seem to attract flies like other fly strips I've used. The flies just seemed to ignore them. We took them down after a week of catching almost NO flies.

Cons: It's very hard to unwind. It tends to want to stay spiraled up. It does a poor job of attracting flies. After two days, it has only caught 2 fruit flies. Pros: It doesn't smell as bad as the other types of fly traps-- namely super effective pouch fly trap mixes that smell like rotting carcasses.

I have had this up in the corner of my kitchen for 24 hours now. We live in South Florida and flies are a huge nuisance. Can’t open an exterior door without multiple flies making their way inside. Often there are 4 or 5 flies in the house at a time and I spend hours trying to zap them before I start preparing dinner. So I ordered these because I’ve used other Terro products and they worked. This sticky fly contraption has not captured one fly. I assume this is also supposed to attract the flies but so far, nothing. They don’t seem interested at all. I’m still needing to sneak up and zap them myself which takes forever.

reviewTitle: Good for house flies not for fruit flies.
reviewDescription: May work ok for house flies but the little fruit flies were not attracted to it, we caught maybe 3 and that was over the kitchen sink.
One of the traps had no glue trap it was just a cardboard cylinder

Just didn't work very well. Didn't catch any flies. I used this in conjunction with a small bait trap (the TERRO Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors). The fly tape caught about 2 flies, and the traps caught a million. Whatever is supposed to attract the flies on the tape just doesn't work. It's also pretty ugly, so I can't say I'd recommend this over the traps.

I literally saw a fly land on this, pause a moment, and then fly away. Another landed, walked around a bit, walked some more... I got tired of watching and left. The point is, the paper is not sticky enough to have maximum effect. A few bugs seemed to get tired and die on it, but others just went on their way. I have used fly paper before, but this just hasn't worked well enough. 2/5 stars, will not purchase again if I can help it.

Bought it based on the good reviews it has to trap the flies, but when I set it up, it looked ugly dangling from the ceiling(I stuck it to the chandelier). It sat there for a week and ended up getting hold of ONE fly. Didn't work at all for us. Returning.

reviewTitle: Doesn't attract crap! But sticky enough to not unroll!
reviewDescription: **Notice, I'd love to have a picture of these, but they went into the trash bag because 3 out of 6 went there while hanging! The rest are in the drawer! _________________________________ The ease of unrolling is fraught with trying to untwist it & then try to hang up.... The included thumbtack is more of being able to slice your fingers than anything! It bends so easy & just won't go through drywall, firm cardboard or something else. To unroll it would be great if red string was looped out of the roll. Then before you do that STOP! Pull string out & mount with duct tape or something very strong. And from there start un-ravelling(twist) to straighten out.... But(yes another BUT) put a weight on the tube end to keep from twisting back up... Confused or annoyed with my review? Try doing it... And then you will understand.... Sadly

We had made our own fruit fly traps (apple cider vinegar with a few drops of dish soap) but read about these so decided to try them. When we received, I realized they are…apple cider vinegar with a few drops of dish soap. And, our homemade version has caught more fruit flies, are much cheaper, and these are more precarious and easier to accidentally knock over than if you build your own in a mug. Don’t bother.

reviewTitle: Does it get worse before it gets better?
reviewDescription: Could this be a “it gets worse before it gets better” situation? I bought the trap because I noticed only a few flies (maybe 5) around my kitchen I’ve only had the trap out for 24 hours now but I’ve noticed MORE fruit flies than before as if it’s attracting more flies with its smell. There are maybe 10 fruit flies in the trap but at least 10 more flies flying in places around the trap. I’ll try keeping it up a while longer to see if there is improvement but otherwise I might have to throw it away and try something else.

Basically the same as using a home vinegar mix except this worked less than a home mix.
