We had an infestation of fruit flies and gnats for a short period of time and even with using 2 of these in different locations of the house, we only caught 3 in a week long period. Caught more using home made mix which was much cheaper and a bug zapper.
Its not working and fruit fly still around
I did not care for the ACV all the time. Also with the completely open lid it was not very accident friendly or capable of keeping a bug in unless it was fully covered in ACV.

reviewTitle: Works decently, but homemade remedies are just as good
reviewDescription: This product is okay. It definitely attracted fruit flies but a homemade container of red wine vinegar and dish soap did the exact same level of job. I still have a fruit fly problem and it seems to have multiplied.
Didn't have any fruit so I guess they came up the drain. Too slow to work and too few dead. I can do the same with just apple cider vinegar

Basically, it did nothing but stink up the kitchen. I placed it in the kitchen where flies zooming around. In a week the flies were still airborne and not a single fly in the jar. I think it's design is poor. Just a tall and narrow jar, it is easy to tip over, and unlike similar products the top is nothing but an open jar. Other products to kill fruit flies I have used are lower and wider making them more stable and have several small holes in top so if it does tip it isn't a complete loss.
I don’t think it was worth the money or even works at all. I have had it out all night and all day today and only see 2 fruit flies in there. Not worth the money

reviewTitle: After the first week, fruit flies stop getting trapped - maybe the scent wears off?
reviewDescription: After the first week of using these fruit fly traps, fruit flies stop getting trapped - maybe the scent wears off? I wasn't impressed with these.

reviewTitle: So disappointed! No longer recommend. Seller never responded.
reviewDescription: FRUIT FLY SEASON IS RAPIDLY APPROACHING AND I'M DETERMINED TO PREVENT LAST SUMMER'S INFESTATION. Update : I contacted the company multiple ways and multiple times. Amazon and the company totally ignored my legitimate complaint. Obviously they don't care about their customers dissatisfaction. It's not about the money - it's about blatant misrepresentation and unethical behavior displayed by both companies. I couldn't even get Amazon to include the "frequently returned" box on the item's page. I bought three bottles of auntie's product on Amazon 6 days ago to replace my DIY liquid attractant of appl cider vinegar +brown sugar +dish detergent. It's been 5 days and can confirm the other negative reviews that the product CAN'T contain the same liquid attractant - the fruit flies feed on the liquid and are not trapped - they can fly away!!! I unintentionally performed a side by side experiment using this WORTHLESS product against my attractant. Both liquids were placed next to separate hanging cylinders of bright yellow sticky fly paper. I actually watched the flies fly from the auntie's product into my liquid. Unlike the auntie product, any flies that were not trapped in my DIY liquid were trapped in the fly paper. After 5 days I had to replace the fly paper adjacent to DIY liquid. in contrast, the 3 bottles of Auntie containing who knows what had A TOTAL OF LESS THAN 20 IN THE BOTTLES AND ABOUT 10 TO 20 TRAPPED BY THE FLY PAPER. I am so angry about wasting money on products from Amazon with good reviews. This product has overall rating of 3.8. But that rating is CUMULATIVE - The much older reviews are generally positive compared to more current negative reviews. I conclude this is due to a change in formulation that neither amazon or the seller made clear. I was so pleased that Good Housekeeping had recommended the best way to eliminates fruit fly infestations in a recent article. The GH panel incorrectly included this product as the only commercially effective liquid attractant since it contained malic acid as part of the formulation. The FRUIT FLY SEASON article made me remember that I had used Aunties successfully a few summers ago and loved their esthetics and effectiveness. I already emailed a request for them to provide evidence of its effectiveness using a recent bottle. I am requesting a refund from this seller based on my results and will update the status with both responses. Meanwhile if you are battling with fruit flies, infestations made worse by the higher temperature (I live in Connecticut) in the Northeast, read below. First understand that you must use the following steps throughout the fruit fly lifecycle (over several weeks so that the original fruit flies are trapped as well as the their progeny hatched from the hundreds of larvae deposited on whatever they fed on. {I am a biochemist and patent attorney so I needed to know what was going on}. Keep in mind for DIY solutions or other traps that you must attract and rid them from escaping. Otherwise you'll have dead flies near and flying around the attractant. A single fruit fly will deposit hundreds of larvae on fruit and vegetables and existing food sources. Follow these steps : 1. Clean all surfaces daily with your typical all purpose cleaner (I use Fantastic with bleach) wherever you find these annoying bugs. For us this is primarily the kitchen in addition to the bathroom and TV room. REMEMBER THEY ARE ATTRACTED TO LIGHT AND 1 FLY WILL DEPOSIT EGGS THAT MATURE IN about 5 to7 DAYS. 2. Unfortunately surface cleaning every 1-2 days is necessary to eliminate flying flies you see and larva that will hatch that you can't. 3. Immediately thoroughly wash all fruit and vegetables (existing or new) and refrigerate. 4. Eliminate All food debris from kitchen counters, microwave, garbage cans, toaster oven, pet water and food bowls, etc. and keep everything covered or washed like used knives, glassware and plates. 5. If you think this is drastic like I did, then you will see an infestation that feels as if those little things won... 6. in the article, Good housekeeping recommended this product as using malic acid which is found in apple cider vinegar. It was part of discussion on using a DIY solution see ingredients above. Any solution must also contain a detergent which causes the flies eating on the attractant liguid to sink in the solution. They can't fly away and begin a new cycle of depositing eggs. At the beginning of the infestation I used the ingredients above along with standing/hanging yellow sticky fly paper I also bought from Amazon. I am very frustrated that I didn't do the research and work upfront instead of wasting money trying to find the non-existent magic bullet all summer!! As much as I love tasty vine ripened tomatoes and other summer fruit and vegetables, it's just not worth it. I think the high temperatures in July throughout the North East as well as nationaly/globally contributed to the problem experienced by me and almost everyone I know.

reviewTitle: Six Traps Set - One Fly Caught. 80 Bucks Wasted
reviewDescription: What I liked: It's easy to use. What I disliked: It arrived 2 days late. It doesn't work. It's a spill hazard. It's extremely expensive considering it's vinegar, water, fruit juice and soap. I paid $80 to catch one fly. That about sums it up. We get these fruit flies every year but this year is the worst. I've had the drains cleaned and treated professionally and I'm pouring drain cleaner and fly treatment down the drains daily. I was going to buy the decoy traps that look like an apple. I've used them before and they were great. I couldn't find any in stock, except for one that had bad reviews. Then there was Aunt Fannie's. There's no special formula even though Aunt Fannie's has a patent on their formula. It's different because first, it's not a decorative decoy trap that looks like an apple or mini pumpkin. Aunt Fannie's looks like a spice bottle. When I opened the cap, I expected to see some kind of funnel mechanism on top. That's how the other traps work. The flies are attracted by the sweet formula inside. They slide down the funnel right into the solution and can't get out. Aunt Fannie's just has a wide-mouth opening that can very easily spill. I placed 4 in my kitchen, one in the dining room and one in a bathroom - where the flies congregate as the directions tell you. I watched the flies swarm around the traps and sat back waiting for them to dive in and never check out. They landed on the bottles, walked around, climbed down inside, climbed back out, walked around a little more, then swarmed around. One little fly fell in and couldn't swim. So $80, several days of waiting, then placing these open bottles of liquid all over the place, and I caught one, single fly. Just one. I was convinced to buy this product because I saw so many great reviews. It seemed like a no-brainer when I couldn't find the product I actually wanted. I've never had this happen before. I usually buy stuff with 4 stars or better and when I do, I agree with those reviews. In this case, I was completely wrong and I've wasted time and money.

I purchased this because I saw the reviews. BUT it did not work for my house not one fruit fly met its death with this stuff. I was thinking maybe its because the opening is the size of the packaging. Most fly traps have tiny openings so they cant get back out. I purchased the apples ones as well. Once those were opened they went right to them. Lots of dead flies in those! So I believe this is a hit or miss for anyone who purchases.

I had this near my bunny cage where i often get fruit flies. I will say I poured a little in one of those tomato looking fruit fly cups and left this open to see if there was a difference because this doesn’t not have holes, just a plain open cap, neither collected flies, even days later. I will not purchase again. Does not work.

Aunt Fannie’s Flying Punch is a joke. Three bottles out for one month while fruit flies are doing summersaults around my face taunting me as they exclaim “I’m s that all you got?” Seriously don’t buy this. It doesn’t work. Just donate the money to someone who needs it on the street.

I haven’t caught not one gnat or fly I placed it on my kitchen sink counter around my plants! I have a zevo and sticky traps that work. Every thing catching them except for the punch I even sat it in the pot of one of my plants and still didn’t catch not one gnats

reviewTitle: Works terribly; less effective than DIY traps and smells terrible.
reviewDescription: My high rise condo is currently having trash chute issues, so I came home from a weekend trip to a fruit fly problem. I hastily bought this, a blue light/sticky trap, and placed out a few cups with old wine and a drop of dish soap. Left all of these out for 2 full days throughout the kitchen to eliminate my new roommates. The fruit flies are all now gone, but no thanks to this product (or the light trap, while I'm at it). The 2 cups of old wine, however, successfully caught and drowned dozens of fruit flies each. The light trap managed to catch one. This product caught zero. It also reeks of vinegar, strongly enough that I notice it immediately upon entering the apartment. I highly recommending skipping this entirely and putting out old wine or juice with dish soap instead, save your money and your sense of smell.

If I could rate this zero stars or negatives stars I would!!!! This sucks I have a fruit fly infestation I have let this out and the flies are going nowhere near it. It’s literally nothing but apple cider vinegar I coilda bought a bottle cheaper . I WANT MY MONEY BACK

I have never been as disappointed in something from Amazon as I was with this stuff! I had seen them mention it on a TV show. Im thinking maybe GMA...but anyway....so then i checked AZ and it had a ton of positive reviews so I just knew this was going to be the answer to our fruit fly problem. In another few days it will be an entire month that these 2 bottles have sat out in our house where we see these flies the most. Believe it or not....not one single fruit fly is in the liquid of either of these! We've stood and watched them fly to the bottle, and even down in it but pretty soon they fly right back out! Finally yesterday I decided to top each one of them off with a bit more apple cider vinegar..thinking POSSIBLY it hadnt been strong enough originally. NOPE! It's still not working. But yet there are still people saying it worked great for them. So Ive finally started a return today since I feel like Ive given it a decent chance with no success.

reviewTitle: Product does not deliver advertised results...
reviewDescription: I am not sure where they came from (or keep coming from), but I have a small swarm of fruit flies in my kitchen that I have been trying to get rid of for several weeks. I take my trash out daily, have cleaned my kitchen from top to bottom including the drains and trash can, no longer leave fruit (or any other kind of food) out on the counter, and have moved my cat's food and water dishes to another room. I also have tried several DIY natural remedies. Yet the fruit flies persist. I do not want to use an insecticide spray in my home because I have multiple chemical sensitivities. I read about Aunt Fannie's online -- the product got good reviews, so I thought it would be worth a try. The Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch! arrived and I put it out on the counter above where my kitchen trash can is and waited hopefully for it to work its magic. It has been five days and I am still waiting. In five days, Aunt Fannie's has trapped five fruit flies. In the same time period, I have swatted down 29 fruit flies and a sinkful of soapy water that I soaked a couple of pots in for an hour one day trapped seven fruit flies. I am really disappointed in this product -- it was definitely not worth the money. At this point, I am not sure what will work to rid my kitchen or fruit flies, but I feel fairly certain that it will not be Aunt Fannie's.

Otherwise 1. Guys. This overpriced apple cider vinegar with a drip of dish soap. The DIY way works way better. Get a clear up, put a few drops of dish soap in it. Pour in apple cider vinegar so that there is a light "soap lining" over the top. Then cover the glass with well stretched cling wrap. Use a fork to poke s few small holes (easy to get on, hard for them to get out which is a big thing this product misses). The fruit flies smell it and crawl into the holes, ho toward the food-smell and get stuck from the layer of soap. They can't crawl out because of the plastic wrap. Bingo. $1 worth of supplies

reviewTitle: Easy, it just did NOT work
reviewDescription: Fruit flies were not attracted to it. Easy to use, just remove the lid. The scent is fine, not even detectable, it is a great concept but the t e r r o apple shaped fruit fly traps work 100x better. I actually even used both in my home at the same time and this container was knocked over (This is easy to knock over & waste ALL) and hardly had any caught fruit flies in it when the t e r r o apples had caught as many flies as they could catch, so the one minute it takes to squeeze the liquid into the little apples is definitely worth it even though this natural product just requires a person to remove a lid. Would not buy again, and I hate that because this product is reasonably priced and easy to use. It just didn't work.

reviewTitle: But I've got to say I'm pretty disappointed this time
reviewDescription: I purchased Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch last summer due to an extreme amount of gnats and flies in our home. I felt the product worked extremely well and I knew I was going to buy it again a year later. A year later came, and i purchased 5 of them, one for each room that the gnats usually show up in. I received the 5 and not a single one of them I can get open due to the cap not coming off. I have to take a small flat head screwdriver and pry the cap off and then I'm able to get it off. When I finally get it off it also rips part of the seal off as well, so it makes a mess getting the liquid inside all over. I'm not sure if this was a production error or it's a problem with the new design of the product. But I've got to say I'm pretty disappointed this time.

I bought these because the description on the web site really seemed promising. I put them where we get these damn fruit flys. Bathroom sink, a few areas in the kitchen. The pictures above aren’t good, but there are only 2 out 3 flies in that bottle after over a week. Same with ALL of the bottles I placed. Please- don’t waste your time or money. NOT WORTH IT!!!

reviewTitle: Lot Of Money for Apple Cider Vinegar
reviewDescription: Not very effective. Caught some fruit flies but caught way more with homemade traps.

The positive is that I really like the bottle that it comes in. The negative is that it is just, in my opinion, diluted apple cider vinegar. This does not work! I did buy a three pack, and added a few drops of Dawn dishwashing liquid into each bottle and then it worked like crazy! I did have to tweak it in order to get it to work. The first 24 hours, before I added the Don dishwashing liquid, it did nothing

We just recently went on a vacation. Before we left one of our kids left a peach on the counter that rotted. When we got home we were super infested with fruit flys. So I bought this product to help get rid is of them. After 3 days it had caught just 4 fruit flies out of thousands of them.

Purchasing this product was a waste of money. First of all, the package was insecurely packaged. One bottle was empty when l opened the package. The liquid leaked all over the place. Secondly, the product caught very few gnats. I would have been better off just buying fly stickers or using apple cider vinegar and dish lotion.

My house has become infested with these stupid little fruit flies/drain flies/gnats whatever you want to call them. I have tried the homemade DIY traps and they catch some but not enough. This started out pretty good. I think my apple cider vinegar and dish soap attracted them more though. After having it out for a few weeks I finally just threw it away. I’m putting a pic of how many it caught. But keep in mind I still have them everywhere. And I stopped buying fresh fruit a couple months ago to try to help the situation(which it did improve but I miss fruit). Overall I would buy it again but there has to be a better way, a permanent way, to get rid of these things!!! Every time I purchase something online, I always check the reviews. I count on others to help me choose the best option. My reviews are always my honest opinion and I try to include photos if possible. If my review has helped you, please click the "helpful" button below, and if you have any questions feel free to ask! Thank you!

Bad fruit fly problem this summer. Got these amongst other products because I was desperate. Sad to say it didn't work. The flies would hover around it but never actually went in. If anything, I think they took a drink then flew away. Homemade apple cider vinegar traps worked better. It did arrive in a timely manner, however.

reviewTitle: Don't buy this. Make your own!
reviewDescription: Well, I got duped by this product. It's mostly my fault. I needed a way to get rid of fruit flies that made their way into my home. I had made my own trap with apple cider vinegar and dish soap, but the vinegar was really bad and stunk up the house. I searched for fruit fly traps and came across this. I gleaned over the description and didn't bother with the reviews (two things I usually never do) and ordered it. It arrived the next day (which is why I bought it) and I opened the package. Much to my surprise and disappointment, it was just a small bottle of apple cider vinegar with soap in it. Man, I felt dumb, but I decided to try it. The cap is a joke. It makes the bottle taller when open and easier to knock over so you can spill it everywhere. Also, it leaks if you close it and don't keep it upright. I poured some into a glass pudding bowl, covered it with plastic wrap and sealed it with a rubber band around the bowl. I poked four 3/8" holes in the wrap, set it out near where the flies are and left for a few minutes. When I came back the flies were all over the bowl. Once they make their way in through the holes they're essentially trapped. You will get the same results with apple cider vinegar and dish soap with this setup (because that's all it is) and over time it'll be cheaper. Don't buy this or any other similar brands. Just make your own trap and save the $8 that I stupidly spent.

reviewTitle: More still flying around than dying
reviewDescription: I've used Aunt Fanny's ant spray in the past with good results. So I thought, why not try the fruit fly solution for those pesky pests? Well, here's a good reason: it doesn't work very well. Sure, I see some floating in the liquid, but there are at least ten times that are still flying all around my kitchen. Then I decided to try one that sticks to the wall with good reviews. But those fell off the wall and I will return them. So I will buy the ones I should have all along, Terro traps. They worked great and I'm embarrassed that I bothered with anything else. I just wish Terro sold the liquid which how this all began.

I had a THICK CLOUD of fruit flies descend in my kitchen! Researching my options, I found this product & ordered a bottle. While I was desperately awaiting its arrival, however, I fell back on 2 older remedies—beer and apple cider vinegar. Maybe 1/8 of a cup of each of these were poured into separate clean cat food tins with some dish soap for viscosity, then covered with plastic wrap, and punctured with small holes. Once the product arrived, I could easily compare the attraction each liquid possessed. Beer? The winner! Apple cider vinegar? A very close second! Lagging way WAY behind is this product. Granted, it’s got a small number of snagged victims, but nothing like the other two. Save yourself some money. You also get the freebie bonus of finishing off the beer that didn’t get poured into the can!
Doesn’t work any better than anything else, which isn’t saying much

This item did not work for me. We followed the instructions and while it did catch some fruit flies, they mostly just hung around the open lid and didn’t actually go into the container. I feel like the design is a bit flawed with that because if the flys go in there is nothing trapping them and they can just fly out. It is easy to use but not very effective. I think if you poured the liquid into something that would actually catch the flys themselves it would work great but again I think the design is a bit flawed.
Items arrived leaking, with one container's top fully off and the other half off and the plastic wrapping the two torn. The fruit flies were having a field day on my counter!

Don’t waste your money. Good old apple cider vinegar works better. Pour 1/4c of acv into a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap and poke small holes in the plastic. It actually works better and doesn’t emit any smell like this product.

Gnats (fruit flies) just hang around the liquid but don't attempt to get in. I suggest the company to make these containers very small with a sticky plastic inside so they can get trapped. The idea is great but the designed is terrible. I don't know what to do with it but I am probably just going to have to throw it away unless the company offers a refund.
Two of the four bottles had loose lids and leaked. Waste of my time and money. Need to return.
Didn't work well. Strong smell
Dried up and didn’t catch any gnats.
DIDN'T WORK VERY WELL

I had such a bad gnats problem in the house and I was recommended these. As much as I wanted them to work, they barely did anything. I ended up investing is some Zevo lights and those work wonders. No more gnats or flies for that matter.

Didn’t work for me. I think I have even more bugs than before! I think the produce inside may work but the opening is narrow. I’m in Texas where these bugs are everywhere so it’s disappointing it didn’t work.

I had gnat-sized fruit flies in the kitchen, then noticed they would trawl around the house- bumbling just above the side tables in the living room, then I'd see them in the windows too. I thought I'd try Aunt Fannie's Flypunch (the vinegar scent smells CLEAN to me); it did attract them, but I watched them- this one-of-many species of fruit fly was smart enough to land on the sides of the jar inside, take a few steps to drink the liquid, then back up and fly out. When the jar was empty, there was a grand total of TWO flies in the bottom. I had high hopes, but no luck... My hubby had better luck with the little butterfly-shaped sticky stakes. He hung one a few inches under the shade on the side table lamp, and cut a slit in the lid of an old plastic margarine dish to slide another one into. Later, his little stakes had 40+ on each one. Of course right now we have no kids or pets around to spread the sticky all over, but the contest is done. 😄

reviewTitle: Great product but they shorted me a bottle.,
reviewDescription: This product works. I have ordered it before. But this time, even though the product is sold as a set, they only sent me one bottle.

reviewTitle: You can easily make this yourself..
reviewDescription: Vinegar with a squirt of dish soap in a small bowl works just as well. We did a side-by-side test. Use this only if you don’t have any vinegar and dish soap.
Didn’t really work, was trying to find way to refill Taro containers for fruit flies but all this did was make kitchen smell of vinegar.

The product attracted a few drain flys put didn't reduce their life cycle as advertised. It has the same vinegar smell that attracts the pests and a few will go in. Not worth the money. Won't buy again.

reviewTitle: Barely works, stick with vinegar and plastic wrap
reviewDescription: Apple cider vinegar in a small glass, Saran Wrap over the top, rubber band keeping the wrap on the glass. Wrap taut like a drum, poke three holes about 3mm long; long enough for the little so-and-so’s to crawl in. They can’t crawl out, and will eventually pass out and drown. Two or three of these glasses around the kitchen took care of everything. This product, by comparison, smelled worse and grabbed maybe 1/10th the amount of each wrapped glass.

Not sure if this is typical of product, but really fairly useless compared to a fly swatter. Had hoped these would attract the flies, but the only get an occasional fly that happens to land on it, which is very rare.

reviewTitle: Do what it says but kind of made cheaper.
reviewDescription: THey worked but you can get better price on 40 pack.
not really working. I can see flies passing by it but didn't get attracted at all.
It work, for what I have is fruit fly, 3 star because nothing is perfect, it did the job tho. Don't know how long it last when is open so yea.

reviewTitle: Flies weren't iterested in this tape, despite several tries in several different areas
reviewDescription: Tried putting these in several places around the house and, despite there being multiple flies around, they did not seem to be attracted to the sticky tape, at all. I have used these in the past (not this particular brand, but other brands, and the ones I've used in the past would collect flies very quickly), so it's hard to say if the issue is with this product, or just insect evolution.
Hard to put up without a ladder and stick to everything. Need a special cleaner to take the sticky remnants off walls and cabinets. Did not catch most of the flys and nats.

reviewTitle: They Work, But Hard to Pull Out
reviewDescription: hese fly traps are okay — they do the job and help catch flies, but they’re a bit tricky to pull out. Sometimes they work perfectly, and other times they don’t come out as easily as expected. Still, for the price, they’re worth it and get the job done.

reviewTitle: Depends on where you hang it
reviewDescription: I can't explain how they will work in one area of my house but not in others. I put these in my kitchen where I get most flies but the one I hung in the den window gets more on it. You will get flies on it but if you don't see results after 2 weeks move it

reviewTitle: Doesn’t unravel well - even when warm
reviewDescription: Wasn’t easy to unravel - and I made sure they were warm before opening. They would’ve caught more insects, quicker, if the tape unraveled better.

Minus 1 star because they don't unfurl like they are supposed to, no matter how carefully, slowly, and gently you twist and pull. They just rip instead because they are so sticky. Minus a second star because it can be very stressful to remove the stickum from yourself if you accidentally come into contact with it--it is stubborn stuff if you don't have the right solutions. Keep three stars because this stuff is more than sticky enough to catch flies. It can catch a lot of flies, and it can even catch people lol. If you buy this, proceed with patience to unfurl it, and be sure to have a variety of de-gunkifying solutions on standby in case you accidentally get some of the stickum on you. Seriously. I tried dish soap, oil, nail polish remover, rubbing alcohol, and pomice scrub and still had difficulty getting it all off of my hand. Maybe goo-gone could do it but I couldn't find mine.
I’ve had 2 of these right next to a bunch of bananas for a week now Still have a lot of fruit flies on/around the bananas, but only two flies on the trap

I hate fruit flies. But apparently they love me and my house. Sigh. I bought these thinking they would help control the fruit fly population. Alas, they don't. Yes, they are sticky. And yes, when the ocasional fruit fly decides to land on them they do get stuck. However, these sticky pads do not appear to be attracting the flies. I often find myself holding one of the sticky pads on a stick and trying to smoosh the flies against the window or against the fruit they are sitting on. Or heck sometimes I"m even waving the sticky thing in the air trying to catch the flies in mid-flight. I wish they had some sort of attraction scent or some other way to lure the flies onto the sticky pad.

reviewTitle: I made a better gnat trap
reviewDescription: Bought this as I had some gnats get into the house and were driving me nuts so I tried this. Not sure if I did something wrong, but not impressed. Then I remembered the traps that I have seen that involve creating a small opening just big enough for the pests to get in and then they never figure out how to get back out so I took an empty Gatorade bottle, poured a little Gatorade at the bottom, got some paper and made the paper into a cone shape with a opening and inserted it into the opening, added some tape around the edges to make it stay in place and also seal any gaps and BAM! Works perfectly. The pests fly into the bottle and cant figure out how to get back out. Since I drink Gatorade and have it always around I will be using this little DIY trick and the bottle/liquid just need to be sweet/tempting to the bugs, it does not have to be a Gatorade bottle. It could be a water bottle that you add some sugar and water to.

reviewTitle: The paper strips do not come off the glue backed board
reviewDescription: The idea was good but this firm did not spend enough on performance. I ended up with half of the butterfly and half of the tree and the other half was covered in strips of paper because I couldn't get it off. Lots of glue all over my hands I spoke to other people who had had a different manufacturer and they had not had any problem but they were a lot more expensive! I had to throw most of mine out and I was covered in glue!

reviewTitle: Get the ones that are just one piece, unless your only hanging them.
reviewDescription: Do they work? Well, yes. The gnats do stick to them, the traps just don't stick well to anything else... These are my issues. The white paper is hard to get off. Especially if you don't have finger nails. The traps simply fall off of the sticks they comes with. Yes you can use the twist ties to hang them, but I like them closer to the soil in the pot. I even tried sticking them to the side of the pot, and they just fall off. I did have some luck punching an extra hole in the bottom to slide the stake through both holes. That's the only thing that worked so far. And I may need to use dowel rods instead as these stakes are very small trying to use this way. I prefer the ones that are sticky trap with the stake all as 1 piece so there no chance.

reviewTitle: Not at all what I was expecting
reviewDescription: I purchased this item hoping to capture flies around my patio area. Took 2 days to catch 2 flies. Mean while flies are buzzing all around us as we sit outside. Did some research and found out YELLOW is one of the least attractive colors for flies....why make a product with a color that flies are least likely to be attracted to? Misleading advertising, shame on the mfg for stating it will attract flies. Do some research, you will find that blue is the color of choice. I actually watched flies land on the blue section of a beach towel, avoiding the yellow, further backing the science. Shame on me, got me with this one.

reviewTitle: Works some times, more or less...
reviewDescription: This definitely won't solve your fruit fly problem. It only works if you place it right next to a bait (ripe fruit, for example). Otherwise no fly will even come close to it. Even when they do land on it, it usually only takes them a little extra effort and they fly off, because the glue is not good enough. Major disappointment.

These are great! Only issue i have is when I opened the package is sticky tags and started using found a stack already used and covered in dirt, missing everything else but the sticky tags. Kinda disappointed but can’t send back since i already opened and had used some before I found the ones that had already been used in the package!

It works great as a sticky trap but it is difficult to peel in one piece. Especially the butterfly design. When it doesn't peel on one go and you try to remove more, your fingers get sticky and stuck as well which gets even more frustrating. Wish they thought of a better design. I don't need cute designs. A circle or square will work just fine.

Product description says these can be used indoors or outdoors, and they work really well; however, they should only be used indoors because lizards get stuck on them and die (also dragonflies and ladybugs - both of which help to keep plant eating pests under control outside). They are also a risk to honeybees and hummingbirds. I bought them for use outdoors and will not be able to use them

I looked at all the DIY suggestions on how to get rid of my fruit flies that seem to be multiplying exponentially. None of them really worked well and so I bought this product. Frankly, for me it didn't work any better than what I had tried and the formula seems to be relatively the same as the DIY

I have had much better success using apple cider vinegar and dish soap. Pour about a half inch of apple cider vinegar into a coffee cup and add a drizzle of dish soap. No need to mix, just leave it wherever you've seen the fruit flies. This product costs $8 and the only thing it was good for is the plastic container in my case. I have seen others having a lot of success with this and had hi hopes. I have also seen that others have said that they may not have fruits flies, but I'm pretty sure the little buggars are just that. No worries though, just trying different options.
For the price point I really expected it to do a way better job. It helped a little but definetly not the fix for tge problem .

Unfortunately, this didn’t work for me. I live in an apartment of less than 600 sq feet and the fruit flies did not take. I kept it lying around to keep re-trying it but instead it eventually ended up spilling and making my apartment smell like vinegar. Don’t recommend you wasting your $ for a fancily-packaged vinegar.
Bought these a few times now and they’ve always worked great! Recently tho they have not been catching anything at all!

this pretty much smells like apple cider vinegar. not sure if it has other things in it. Did not work as i hoped. have had it for about 3 weeks. there are a few flies in it but co side ring how many i have it is about 5% the others just swarm around the top. Will not be buying again
This is just a very expensive vinegar trap. I placed it next to a homemade trap with red wine, and another with vermouth. The vermouth trapped 90% of the flies. Not worth the cost
Saved my product defective with leaking. Returned.

reviewTitle: Did not work at all - it caught nothing! lol
reviewDescription: I've had it open for a week and caught no fruit flies. The homemade "traps" that i made (apple cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap covered with plastic with a few holes in the top with a toothpick) placed not too far away caught 5 fruit flies within 2 days.
This product (Annie's Fruit Fly) somewhat worked for my niece but it didn't work well for me.

reviewTitle: Caught a few, but have many, many more that are still flying around.
reviewDescription: Fall brings these little fruit fly, gnat things around my area. They are really problematic. This product was suggested to me and was said to be a miracle that would solve my problem. NOT!! While a few of these annoying little critters were attracted to the open bottle, the majority were not and kept annoying the household. Wouldn't waste my money again, would try the home remedy I was told about with apple vinegar and dish soap. Don't think it would catch any fewer. Good luck, buyer beware!

reviewTitle: You catch more flies with vinegar...
reviewDescription: After reading reviews and press for this product, I had high hopes. Unfortunately, the product didn’t quite deliver on my expectations. When the fruit flies first became an issue in my apartment, I tried out a DIY concoction of apple cider vinegar, water, sugar and dish soap. It caught a lot of flies, but not all of them. I ordered this product and put it out in the same room as the DIY solution. Unfortunately, it only caught one fly while my mixture caught 15-20 flies in the same time span. I have continued to leave Fanny’s out, but although the other mix continues to drown flies, this one still only has that one corpse in it. Save your money.

I’ve cleaned. My drains closed off. My drains done everything that I can to mitigate the problem, but I just cannot get rid of the fruit flies. This catches some of them, but from time to time you’ll see fruit flies, flying around it, or landing on the mirror above it, but never going into the bottle. It does catch some. It’s just not luring all of them. Ive had it for several weeks. has strong smell initially.

reviewTitle: Didn’t work as well as the first time
reviewDescription: It didn’t work as well as the first time I had it. Maybe the first week I didn’t see fruit flies but started again after that. Last year they were gone for good when I opened the bottle.

I've had the product open for two days now and it doesn't work as well as promised. It has attracted some of the fruit flies, but not nearly enough. I got this because I wanted something that was no mess and no touch. I definitely would try a different product if I need help again, which I probably will given the flies I still see.

I bought this after red wine vinegar with a drop of dish soap and apple cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap wasnt really working. I wanted something to help with my issue in a different way for my compost bucket. This product is the same that I was using, and shock… it still doesn’t work. They sit on the rim of the container. Save your money google the recipe with vinegar and do it yourself in a glass.
So I initially thought it would work because the fruit flies were all hovering around it but they never did drink it. Still have fruit flies and need to try something else. Bummer
Upon delivery one bottle was completely empty bc it leaked all in the packing . However , the other bottle worked fairly well so I bought 2 more. With a total of 4 of these it helped quite a bit.

It did work for a few fruit flies, but it makes the entire room reek of vinegar!!! If you don't mind having the entire room smelling like vinegar, then this may work for you. We used this along with other fly traps and the other traps worked more efficiently than this. Maybe because of the overwhelming smell.
Wasn’t nearly as effective as the terro traps. Dont waste your money. Left it out for 2 weeks and caught maybe 5 flys
Did not work as claimed. Simple vinegar recipe worked better

I had previously ordered this product and was very happy with the results. I reordered and when the product arrived today the seals on both of the traps had leaked into the packaging. This may have been caused by the high heat in the Phoenix area, but I won't be ordering this product again.

reviewTitle: I could have done this myself
reviewDescription: I bought this rather than doing a “do it yourself” remedy to get rid of fruit flies. I could have saved $$ and done it myself. You open the top of the bottle and it’s liquid- and easy to spill. While it is helping with the fruit flies, it took a few days. Save your $ and buy a cheap brand of apple cider vinegar. I think that’s all that’s in the jar!
This did work as well as other brands I tried. I won't buy it again.

reviewTitle: This product doesn’t work as well As it’s described
reviewDescription: It only caught a few fruit flies and then nothing

This used to work great. I used to just open it and watch as all of my fruit flies descended to their doom. My place would be rid of fruit flies within a few days. However, this last time it didn’t seem to work at all. No matter where this was put or how long it was left there, it didn’t seem to trap any of the fruit flies. I’m sad I’ll need to find a new solution to my fruit fly woes as this no longer cuts it

I was expecting that for the amount of liquid and compared to the other options in the market this product was going to catch many more flies and it turns out it didn’t. In fact I placed this product right next to the product that comes in the shape of an apple and the other one outlasted this one 2:1

There may be a bit of something else but it's mostly vinegar. It smells up the house but I will have to say I've only seen one pesky fruit fly since I opened the bottles, set one in kitchen and another near cat litter box. So I guess it's working...?

reviewTitle: Flies buzz around it, container can spill easily
reviewDescription: I haven’t seen any fruit flies actually get trapped by it yet. One was happily buzzing around it. The container itself has a wide mouth and this makes it prone to being spilled. I should have just made my own mixture - I thought this would be a more reliable alternative and a better container than mixing apple cider vinegar in a bowl.

reviewTitle: You're better off making your own solution and placing it in a bowl.
reviewDescription: Don't waste your money. You can easily make a fly trap out of vinegar from your pantry and put it in a cup. That is all this was. You rip off the seal and "pop" the top open and it lets the flies in. Over a few days I only noticed 3. However, this being cheap little bottle of plastic, having it open on your counters is not advised. I tipped this over several times causing the terrible vinegar smell to linger longer than desired! This tipped due to a gust of wind from my kitchen window or my maneuvering thru the kitchen just trying to make dinner.

For the cost, this is really ineffective. It works slightly better than trying to get apple cider vinegar and dish soap mixed properly but not by much. Smells about the same. It's not even worth the risk of knocking it over. I'll leave it out longer to see if I can change my mind but I don't recommend it.

reviewTitle: I give it a poor rating simply because it didnt work for me
reviewDescription: I give it a poor rating simply because it didnt work for me. Been sitting out in open areas near the flies for a week (two bottle traps even) and not a single fly has landed in there, or at least died in there. I tried leaving it cracked open, and fully opened as well. One night I accidentally left a ramekin of uncooked egg on the counter ( I was making pie or something) and the next morning I noticed the ramekin had about 20 flies over just one night and the traps still had zero. It clearly works for some people, but for me it didnt. If it didnt work for you, try the egg trick.

It catches enough flies (I guess?) but honestly like others are saying - you may as well have a home remedy if you’re just going to have an open jar of vinegar around. Also, one small movement and the entire bottle can be emptied since there is absolutely no form of filter/screen etc. on the top. Not a total waste of money but other products or a home remedy work better in my opinion.
save money. pour apple cider vinegar in a glass add 2 drops dish soap. that's all this is

I have a small enclosed apt. kitchen 8x8. It has been swarming with fruit flies, due to the extra fruit I've added to my nutrition. I bought this product and put it on my counter, opened it as instructed, and left it for 5 days. It has only captured 8 fruitfulness, no more. I'm disappointed.

reviewTitle: Doesn’t work like I hoped it would.
reviewDescription: I read reviews before I bought these. Some weren’t pleased with the product, but most said it was amazing!! I bought 3 bottles and spent $25 hoping this would be the end of my fruit fly problems, but it doesn’t seem to be working. Each bottle has caught about 2 flies and that’s it. They’ve been out for almost a week and they aren’t really attracting the flies. The smell isnt overpowering, so that’s a plus, but aside from that, I feel like I wasted $25 The seller is great; this is in NO WAY a bad review for them as a seller. ONLY the product is being reviewed here.

Didn't really work that well for me. Only a few fruit flies were caught using these. Not sure why. Tried to make sure there was nothing else they would be attracted to like bananas on the counter, but still only caught a few.

reviewTitle: This product didn’t work for me.
reviewDescription: Although highly rated, this product didn’t work well for me. I managed to kill about 6 bugs in a week. I closed the lid and waited a day then tried again. After opening the lid I noted the bug problem got even worse than before.
When I received the items one jar had totally leaked out and soaked the package.

reviewTitle: Not effective enough to justify the cost
reviewDescription: The bait is semi-effective. There are better baits out there. But the main fault is in the shape of the trap, which isn’t much of one. The opening is too wide. It’s as if you dumped the bait into a mug. The flies mostly sit on the rim then fly off. Perhaps if one could use the bait in a more effective trap, it’d be better, but one generally doesn’t expect to have to buy separate traps at this price. At least it doesn’t smell horrible or like a chem lab; just the homey tang of apple cider vinegar.

reviewTitle: Didn’t work at all…. Perhaps it was the bug not the product?
reviewDescription: This is a long story. I bought canned cat food from Amazon, as usual I put it in the cupboard and went on with life. Fast forward, a few days later little bugs started flying around my kitchen, I thought they were fruit flies. For two days I put up with them then bought this. Basically this is apple cider vinegar mixed with sugar. You can make the recipe yourself, which I would do instead of buying this. Now, upon a week after the food was put in the cupboard I opened it and was attacked by these little bombers with wings. The gray of cat food was infested, hundreds with eggs. The can of food in the second tray which was covered with the standard cardboard and outer plastic was split which made a great environment for these little jerks. It took me a week to hand kill these things- thankfully they were slow. Perhaps they were not fruit flies and that is why this product didn’t work. This is not why I am giving it two stars. I am giving it 2 starts because of its application. Once the top is flipped open it is easy to tip over, it is not balanced. The top is completely open because the goal is for the flies to go in for a drink and drown. Well, unless you put this inside a mug it is very easy to accidentally tip over. The smell is nauseating, but that is not the fault of the product, sometime the “medicine” is just awful. I also think it is a little overpriced. $8. For ingredients that would cost at most $.75. I know it is the ease of use and when you are being overtaken by bugs you are willing to pay anything. I am giving it 3 stars because I am indifferent of its effectiveness. I am not sure if these were fruit flies- if they were, then this product didn’t work at all, if they weren’t then I am judging the other aspects of the product: ease of use, quality, cost. Etc. Quality: Nice looking label, The high pungent smell of vinegar is enough to make you gag- so, it is the real deal and not watered down. Ease of use: just peel off the thin protective film and flip open the lid- voila. Cost: I think it’s a little pricey for the ingredients which you could easily make yourself. (They give the recipe online) But, isn’t it easy to press the “buy now” button and have the delivery guy drop at your door 48 hrs later?

This stuff worked okay. Maybe some batches are better than others. I bought the two-pack and one seemed to attract a lot of gnats while the other caught very few gnats. I tried switching the placement to see if that helped but it didn’t seem to make much difference. It’s very easy to use-just open the package, flip open the lid and it’s ready to go. I’ve made a similar mixture before with comparable results so I probably won’t buy this again.

The product did not work for me, so I originally posted as one-star. Also, it is not eligible for return, so I was stuck with three jars. I am giving three stars now, because of the communication with Aunt Fannie's. I wrote to them hoping they could help with a return. Kelly wrote back promptly and said the return could be done only through Amazon. But, she was very friendly in e-mail and was sorry the product had not worked. She sent some questions and pictures, wondering if what I have is truly fruit flies. As it turned out and upon further research by me, what I seem to have are drain flies. I have eliminated them with baking soda/vinegar and also baking soda/essential oils. I would recommend to anyone that if FlyPunch is not working, consider another fly besides fruit flies. Also, it is worth writing to the company. I found Aunt Fannie's to be very helpful. Update 12/14/17: I have phorid flies, and they are very difficult to get rid of. My baking soda/vinegar/essential oils invention kept them at bay but didn't truly eliminate them. However, I have found a "trap" by happy accident: ruby red grapefruit juice. The little suckers are actually attracted to an open plastic glass filled with juice. It takes a while for them to figure out that they should jump right in, but they are. I am joyfully seeing dead bodies float. This, after going through frustration trying to kill them. I tried adding a drop or two of dishwashing liquid but am finding they are more attracted to just the juice alone. No ACV, orange juice, beer, or red wine for my visitors. Ruby red grapefruit juice. Who knew.

I really like this product when it works but it seems inconsistent. Some bottles I have purchased catch a zillion fruit flies and other bottles seem to be totally ineffective. Not sure what's happening there. Appreciate that there is no awful smell and the design doesn't both me sitting out in my kitchen.
I must have very smart fruit flies because I don't see this product to be very effective as of yet and it's been a couple of days

reviewTitle: Didn't seem to work as effective?
reviewDescription: I didn't feel that this fly trap got rid of all the flies. There were a few at the top, but noticed a lot of flies that managed to avoid being near this item. I have a second one that came with the purchase and will try again next summer if it works. The strong is very pungent fyi.

reviewTitle: Has an effect over time, just not immediately
reviewDescription: Smells like an open jar of red vinegar. No film to trap flying insects in the solution, They basically sit on top of the open jar & nothing precludes the insect from flying away. Maybe it intoxicates them & they fall in & drown. Eventually some bugs do wind up dead at the bottom of in the solution. I did notice a significant decrease in the number of small flying insects in 7-10 days; just not an immediate impact. Cats do not like the vinegar smell & leave it alone wherever you put it (off the floor).

Does it work? Yes. Do I still have the same type of flies that I had before? Yes, yes I do. Have I tried moving it around where I see flies? Yes, I have. If I had to guess I have 6 flies/gnats, and the bottle has 3 of them. So I gave it a 3/5 rating for getting me 50% of the flies.

Effective, but stinky! Got all the critters quickly, but it stinks up the kitchen with strong apple vinegar smell. I have used others before that did not make such a strong smell. Next time I will just buy the little apple shaped ones. Not a great choice in a house with sensitive nosed people, and the container is quite large, always afraid it will get knocked over.

reviewTitle: It has a smell that is a bit off-putting
reviewDescription: I only gave this a three because of the aroma. It does work.

reviewTitle: A WASTE OF MONEY! DOES NOT WORK!!!!
reviewDescription: Horrible product!!!!!! Don't waste your money. It has not caught not 1 fruit fly. They are not attracted to the yellow color.

reviewTitle: Useless and a waste of money
reviewDescription: This did not attract a single fly or other flying insect! Do not purchase this.
Doesn’t work. Fruit flies ignored these.
Did not catch even one.

These are useless. They might work if you have an infestation but I literally caught a fruit fly walking over the adhesive. Put a few out for 2 weeks and have only caught one. Still see several flying around my home.

reviewTitle: Not a single fruit fly caught for weeks.
reviewDescription: Trash!!! Save your money staying away from this item!!!

reviewTitle: These don't work on fruit flies
reviewDescription: These don't work. I am literally watching fruit flies walk across them and then fly away.

reviewTitle: Unfortunately these have not worked for our fruit fly problem.
reviewDescription: Unfortunately these have not worked for our fruit fly problem.
Put 5 in area around kitchen. Not a single catch in 2 weeks.
Did not capture 1 gnat. Do not buy.

These feel sticky to me, but I've watched enough gnats land on them and then fly away to say they're definitely not sticky enough. Not sure what that's about, but I'll be switching back to the brand I used to use instead of these. Love the mini shovel though!

reviewTitle: The paper will not come OFF!!!!
reviewDescription: I’m so incredibly frustrated and angry. I type this while my fingers are sticking to my phone screen even after washing with hot water and soap and using acetone just to try and get off what got on my fingers trying to pull the impossible to remove paper. What’s the point if you can’t get the paper off!!!

reviewTitle: Defective batch not as sticky as before
reviewDescription: This is my second purchase and I may have gotten a defective batch. My first set worked well and captured many fungus gnats. This one I saw some fungus gnats but others also landed and fly away like it was not as sticky. I don’t know what it is, maybe weather is colder now and makes the things less sticky? But disappointed with the last batch of traps from this seller. I may have to go hunt for another supplier of sticky traps.
These don’t work. I still have tons of gnats on my plants and not a single one has been stuck.

reviewTitle: Does not peel off due to terrible design.
reviewDescription: Impossible to peel the paper on the flower pattern design. The paper pull tab is not designed to peel the entire paper. Once it fails, there is no clear way to pick the other jagged edges of paper off of the glue. And if you are successful with one side, you’re gifted with an opportunity to then flip it and try the same crazy process on the other side but have to fight with with exposed glue on the peeled side. Sticky fingers and frustration made me quit this failed experiment.

reviewTitle: Purchased these for fruit flies but they only caught 2 with 8 of them put out.
reviewDescription: Not effective for fruit flies. Found that putting vinegar in a cup with a dropper to a soap stir it in and let it sit on the countertop works the best. You have to add a couple drops of soap to change the surface tension of the water because a fruit fly can land on the apple cider of vinegar. When you throw the soap in a little bit stir it up it changes the surface tension and when you go to try to land they drowned.
Not one bug in 2 days!!

There is NO tab or strip to facilitate getting the paper off. This means that after the first side is off, it’s almost impossible to get the other side off. Yeah, they’re cheap, but I’d have been happy to pay more for a usable product.
Does not attract bugs. They will stick if they land on it, but it doesn't do anything to attract them. Other, better products are out there.

Wasn't sure if I had drain gnats or fungus gnats from the plants, but these things refused to do anything about either of them. In 10 days, not one insect was on the sticky part. In addition, the ones I placed near my plants would always catch any leaf that was near it, and it can damage the leaves when you try to remove them. Stick to other methods.
This is the worst product ever! It definitely doesn't work, if it wasn't a hassle to return I would definitely do it. Waste of your money! It making me give 1 star. Doesn't deserve it

Does not work at all. Did not catch one gnat or fly or anything. It’s also just an ugly, open jar on the counter. I feel like a DIYourself would work much better. Do not recommend. Waste of money. Not sticky, just a liquid. I don’t know about safety, but it it is just an open jar which I would not be comfortable with if my children were little still… that does not seem safe to me.

reviewTitle: Don’t waste your money or time
reviewDescription: Either I have the worlds smartest fruit flies or this product just does not work. They sit on the edge of the jar but don’t go in… lol It has a stong apple cider vinegar smell too. I would not recommend this product. The homemade type works much better. Disappointed 🙁
So useless,I want to cancel my subscription.it just didn’t work
Not effective - disappointed in Raid on this one. There are other reflective products available on Amazon.

If your buying this product because you have a fruit fly problem, save your money. I bought 3 of these because I tried alot of do DIY things and it didn't help. But these raids traps do not work at all, plus it makes your whole house smell like apple cider vinegar!

Thanks to a big local grocery store and their delivery of fresh fruit every order I was plagued with fruit flies from damaged orange and mandarin oranges; not many but enough to annoy. Purchased this product in hopes of resolving this issue but unfortunately the fruit flies just fly around this container and nothing dies! I actually sprayed these fruit flies with kitchen bleach spray and killed most that way. I’m not sure what Raid expects these critters to do but they certainly are not attracted to the container or smell Sorry, this is one of those “don’t waste your money “ products

Be careful before opening. When seal is taken off and spills, jar is wide open! How is this safe for children and pets? Of course the ordor is so effensive anyone stays away while the room has the aroma

reviewTitle: It doesn't work at all, not even a little. Not one fly caught!
reviewDescription: This thing doesn't work at all. Just makes your house smell like vinegar. I left it out for over 24 hours and even put it in the swarm spot they like. They won't touch the stuff. I wouldn't buy this product ever again. And wouldn't recommend to anyone. Unless you don't like them and want their house to stink. This product is trash

This product is not what they are advertising I purchased 2 of them, and I followed the directions and placed them, it suppose to start working immediately, but did not 3-4 hours went by, I still see fruit flies flying around!! …..smh this a ripoff…runnnn

reviewTitle: Not many gnats are being caught
reviewDescription: I was very disappointed.. i had hope these would work somewhat...BUT not a solution at all to my gnat problem....I placed in 4-5 places - more than one in each region and caught hardly anything...darn!!! They are flying around my head as i write this....Oh well

These sticky traps were placed all over kitchen, bathrooms and upstairs office but didn’t catch but one or two gnats. So frustrating but maybe they were more sewer gnats than fruit flies. Waste of our $$ and time. Almost too sticky to attach on walls
FRUITS FLY DANCING AROUND IT , NOT AFFECTED BY IT, I USED THE PATCHES TO SWAT THEM, CAN BE USED AS DOUBLE FACE STICKERS:))
This product did not work at all for me. It’s a shame since it seemed to make sense
These are excellent for arts and craft... A nice summer fun project for the kids or elderly to decorate with glitter and bedazzle. The did not trap anything.
Not one fruit fly stuck to these. Dozens in the apple shaped traps but not a one on these things.

It is easy to use. Adhesive is sticky. But the product does not work. I used tablespoon of brags apple cider vinegar, tablespoon of sugar, six drops of dish detergent, half cup of warm water, Saran wrap, a toothpick to put holes in the saran wrap. Glass bowl or a coffee cup in each room and it caught all of my phats. Be blessed it worked

reviewTitle: AVOID BUYING!!! The protective film does not line up with the adhesive backing...
reviewDescription: AVOID BUYING!!! The protective film does not line up with the adhesive backing causing a bunch of the traps to stick together. What a waste especially if you have a ton of plants to care for.

I’ve used yellow sticky traps in the past for fungus gnats with really good results. The brand I’ve used before wasn’t available so I bought these instead. They are cheap, poorly made and ineffective. Don’t waste your money!

reviewTitle: The item is useless! Waste of money!
reviewDescription: The item is of no use at all! They do not attract gnats or any other flying insects! Wasted money!
Don't waste your money. Gnats land on them and then fly off. They feel tacky to the touch, but still somehow they are not trapping the gnats.
Didn't stick! Waste of money.
I used the product. and fould by watching that the gnats walked on and over the sticky part without sticking. they did't even slow down. this product didn't work for me.

This product was a total waste of money. I read the directions, opened the package and placed one card in a potted plant. Shortly after I saw a gnat get caught on the card. I came back about ten minutes later -- just in time to see it walk off the yellow card and fly away!!
Not very sticky. Barely catches anything. Might catch baby gnats but that's about it
By the time I was able to get the white paper off each one I had planned to use, my fingers a ND nails were covered in thick glue residue. Waste of money, I threw them away.

We placed multiple traps and they didn’t work. We previously purchased a more expensive version but didn’t like the shapes they offered. So when this brand wasn’t working we thought that maybe the gnats and flies got smarter. We found a trap from the more expensive brand and over night it caught 2 flies and 4 gnats. The other traps were still bare! Check the other brands!
Didn't work on our gnats. Put out several through out the house and not a single gnat the next day

reviewTitle: Didn’t kill a SINGLE gnat or fly!
reviewDescription: This product did not work as advertised, at all, unfortunately. I returned it for a full refund. I’m not sure how so many reviewers rated these so highly. I had them in plants all throughout my house for weeks, without a single gnat caught, but they were still flying around the house.

The product works great, everything sticks. BEWARE.. so upset to see this product got stuck on a small bird birched on my pot. Unable to fly, product stuck to the feathers, bird ran on ground, everything stuck to the sticky paper as the bird ran through the yard. leaves, mulch.. SOOO upset that we couldn't help the bird, it escaped through the fence and we couldn't find it.

reviewTitle: We are still being plagued by fruit flys
reviewDescription: We are being plagued by fruit flys and considering that we have so many the traps have barely caught 3 or 4 fruit flys. I tried to hang it at the top of the garbage can and still only a few flys caught. I suppose it does nothing to attract them. I do better just leaving a small bag with garbage in in on the counter and snagging it up whan there is a bunch settled in. I can't imagine how these got any good reviews at all. I can see when a fly touched it the fly seemed to struggle a bit and i wondered if it would get free but eventyually it stuck. I don't think they are sticky enough and with out attracting them they are pretty useless.
Don't work.

I've had sticky stakes like this before and it attracted a gazillion of these fruit flies within a day. Not on single bug is stuck to these. Not a single one. And my house is still full of gnats. My theory is these are not neon yellow like the other stakes I've used in the past. This is like a muted banana yellow versus the neon yellow the other ones are. The bugs are not attracted to these. Do not waste your money.
I put them all over my kitchen. I caught 1 gnat.

reviewTitle: The 96 pack not a great value
reviewDescription: So I bought the 96 pack and I hate it. The 48 pack so much better because the bottom is not sticky and you can press it down in the dirt. But the 96 pack the whole thing is sticky so you need to use something with it to stick it on! And they only give you couple of maybe 10-15 to hold the sticky yellow sticky trap on it. So I tried to stick ( I have more than 100 plants) the trap on the pots but they fall off after a while. I wish the 96 pack was like the 48 pack where the bottom is not sticky !!!!
Waste of money! Not one of the fifty fruit flies stuck to it.

reviewTitle: Not as good as other brands
reviewDescription: I don’t know what it is about this particular brand, but it doesn’t work compared to other sticky traps that look exactly the same. My only guess is these are not as sticky as others. I have seen gnats land on them and then fly away. I will not repurchase this brand again.

I set up a gnat trap using these and I can watch the gnats land on them, struggle a little bit, and then break free and fly off. After 3 days they have not been able to keep a single gnat trapped in the glue. I'm not sure what else there is to say, they completely fail to do their job.

reviewTitle: Trying to get the paper off is awful!!
reviewDescription: These are awful!! It takes forever to get the white paper off each side!! After struggling with them I ended up putting them in the garbage. !

Got these with high hopes. First, the flies never landed on it for the few minutes it did stick.. and I set up MULTIPLE where they usually land. They avoided it, and then each one I stuck fell to the ground, not even 8min later, just to get stuck to my cat. What a waste of money!!

reviewTitle: This product doesn't work at all!
reviewDescription: All the fruit flies hung around the rim and NOT one fell in. I was surprised that it was a Raid product, it was as if I made it at home myself and forgot an ingredient. This truly doesn't work at all, it didn't catch or trap or kill NOT ONE fruit fly. And yes I did shake it before use as it says, and yes they are fruit flies.

reviewTitle: It's just Apple Cider Vinegar in a Cup?
reviewDescription: Well, I can honestly say I'm shocked at this one. I'm pretty sure this is just Apple Cider Vinegar in a cup. I could've bought an entire quart at the store and made my own traps with 20oz bottles for a fraction of the cost, but here we are. Pros: - well I guess I didn't have to build traps Cons: - There's no lid. Unlike other traditional traps, this is just wide open, so don't knock it over. - Pretty sure it's just ACV in a container, so for the price, it's not really worth it - Haven't caught any fruit flies, which are coming out of my drains. My cup is right on the sink, so I'd think there would be something. We'll see how this goes, but so far, I have to be careful not to spill it, it's just ACV, and I haven't caught any fruit flies, so it's not working. I'll update if something changes.
It trapped one. The little red traps worked better. Best overall: apple cider vinegar in a wine glass with plastic wrap, 8 holes created by fork, river band holding wrap in place 🍷

reviewTitle: Run don’t walk the other way
reviewDescription: This product was a total waste of my money again. I bought it and still today not one bug has gotten into this waste of a jar. I thought because it’s raid it would be a better product well not for me. The flying bugs are still flying and annoying me and I’m afraid to purchase another item that doesn’t do the job I really hate bugs and there winning the battle I know the picture is terrible describes my experience with the product just the same

...if I had my way, Raid would have received 0 stars. I wasted approximately $13 on 2 of these. The fruit flies look like they were invited to the club and couldn't get in. So they decided to hang out at the front door and harass the other customers. On another note, just go buy some red vinegar, take a cup/bowl, cover it with Saran wrap, toothpick some holes in the Saran wrap and your done. Don't waste your money...
Just smells like ACV. Attracts fruit flies but doesn’t actually kill them. Waste of money.
Used it over 2 months, never made a difference!

I rate this a -10! This trap is horribly rotten-smelling! No amount of room deodorizer can cover the rancid smell. I put up with it for 2 days, hoping the scent would fade, but it didn't! I requested a refund from Customer Service, and they kindly processed it. After that was finished I asked if I could write a review--then I saw many others had complained about the awful smell! Don't waste your money or time on this product.
It came without the cover that has the holes in it for the fruit flies to go into. So it just has to sit there open! So the fruit flies love to sit on the edge, but it hasn’t caught a single one.
Wth!?... This barely caught anything just bad whiff of apple cider vinegar

reviewTitle: So difficult, barely able to peel
reviewDescription: I have bought many a gnat trap from Amazon and these do not have any part that doesn’t have the sticky stuff so to peel them, it requires you to get your hands covered in sticky stuff and then forever to scrub from your hands. Absolutely horrible, do not buy!!
No son tan efectivas estas trampas. La mayoría de las Ud instalamos, no pegó ni una mosca.

My gnats look at these and laugh. They fly into them and they can jump right off. At first I thought some breed of Teflon coated super gnats formed, but no it was just normal gnats and non stick yellow post it notes.

i put a few out in the yard... because i have a bad gnat problem... but when I went out there , a bird was stuck to it... I felt so bad, i was able to free him, not sure how much damage he had to his feathers... now I'm afraid to put them out...

As is common this time of the year fruit flies have taken over the kitchen and I hoped to find a solution to lessen if not solve our problem, but after 3 days no flies have been caught, these don't work at all.

Bought singing similar to those two years ago and worked great. Overnight, all the fruit flies were gone. I installed this sticky trap and it hasn’t caught a thing the past 24 hours. This sticky trap was a total waste.

reviewTitle: Sticky but collects dirt not bugs
reviewDescription: To put the triangle into the soil is not strong enough. It bends, but even if you do get it in to the pot. Once you start watering and soil hits the sticky. It's game over for any bugs.

They are hard to put in the dirt since they’re flimsy, they give you a little shovel to help but it’s a joke. They are so low once you put them in that they get covered in dirt easily and the paper is hard to take off the sticky shapes without ripping

reviewTitle: Did not work for my gnats.
reviewDescription: These did not work for me at all. I placed them in several locations to help with fungas gnats and I literally watched them fly right by them instead of landing on them to get trapped. Waste of money.
I’m now looking into something else. These do not attract gnats.

reviewTitle: Does not work/GARLIC SAUSE AT LITTLE CEASERS WORKED
reviewDescription: Maybe other people have open windows or dishes sitting in the sink and it worked but for me it did Nothing at all. I still find fruit flies in my coffee. PLEASE READ FOR HELP! I ORDERED PIZZA WITH GARLIC SAUCE, ONE NIGHT. I LEFT THE GARLIC SAUCE ON THE COUNTER OPEN THE NEXT DAY. IT WAS FILLED WITH FRUIT FLIES. MY PROMISE TO YOU IS DOLLAR GARLIC SAUCE WORKS SPECTACULAR AND TRUST ME YOU WILL THANK ME IF YOU REALLY HAVE A PROBLEM. GOOD LUCK!! By the way it was little ceases pizza.
Wasn’t great the fruit flies just hung out outside of it. When I dumped it none of them were dead but a bunch of alive ones near it very annoying
I catch more flys with straight apple cider vinegar with a drop of dawn and a plastic cover with holes poked into it . This product caught 3 fruit flies in 3 weeks 😂😂😂😂😂

This is literally just a bunch of stank apple cider vinegar in an open container. No top to prevent bad spills and no top to keep the fruit flies IN the container. It attracts fruit flies, but they rarely get trapped in the solution. They just hang out around the sides which is an even bigger problem because luring bugs without killing them just makes them more of a nuisance. And all for almost $10. A WASTE! Try Terro traps instead.
Do not waste your money it does not work

I paid 7.00 for a small jar of apple cider vinegar. Needless to say I'm unhappy, do not buy. It's been opened on my counter for 24 hrs with not one gnat. Update 48 hrs 1 gnat and the rest are hanging out around the jar laughing at me.
Smell is awful. Smells like vinegar. Was hard to open which caused me to spill it. It’s in the trash now.
Absolutely the worst product I ever purchased on Amazon and we buy a lot. It does not work at all.
Didnt work well
It does not work. Fruit flies dancing around the rim.

reviewTitle: doesn't catch gnats like I thought it would
reviewDescription: I bought 2 of these, so I used the first pack right away to test as we had a serious gnat issue. It was about a week before I saw any gnats on it, and they were few. Don't think these are effective or work as quickly as I would've like to. I used a at home method (some redwine vinegar and dawn dish soap in bowl method) and that seemed to catch 2x more in 1 day.

reviewTitle: I would like a better product
reviewDescription: Its not catching many fruit flies They are still flying about

reviewTitle: Doesn't trap houseflies, and I have 2 units. Amazon copy says it catches houseflies.
reviewDescription: I bought 2 of these. I trapped one fruit fly, but they do nothing about houseflies. I catch and eat a lot of (non-Salmonidae) wild fish in the summer. Fish guts=houseflies. I have a plentitude of houseflies, but no trapped ones. I gave it two stars for the fruit fly, but that seems overly generous. Kinda disgusted.
Seems like they both stopped working after 4 days…..ordered another set 2 try them again but not happy w current new traps

reviewTitle: An honest review after a week
reviewDescription: I wanted to give my honest review of the product from my usage with it. *Easy to put together. Slide a piece of sticky cardboard into the slot. * The light is as bright as a nightlight and is an actually pretty deep blue. *I plugged in product in problem areas and let them exist for a week before writing this review. *It has caught a house fly. *It has NOT caught any of the small annoying little flyers. *No noticeable smell to product. *In my opinion the product has not delivered desired results. *However I am keeping it bc I like the blue light and it did catch a house fly so that's something *Would not buy again. *Wish I had not bought 2 Compared to a small bottle w vinegar in it. Topped with plastic wrap with little holes cut into it which has caught more than a dozen little flying annoying things.
So far it has not helped at all!

I bought a two pack and put them in opposite corners of our kitchen. One week has gone by and not a single house fly has been attracted to the sticky surface. So basically a fancy night light. Don't expect it to stop the common house flies. I've killed 5 house flies with diluted vinegar.

reviewTitle: Better night lights than fly traps
reviewDescription: I have had the on for over a week and not a single fly has been caught. The do make half way decent night lights though

Have had it next to our kitchen sink for over a week and it's caught just a few bugs, less than what I catch with Apple cider vinegar and dish soap and nats are still coming around. Was really hopeless this would work better.

Reviews on this product show that this thing is phenomenal. However, for us…. We live in Kansas - get heavy flies and fruit flies in the summer…. This was on for 4-5 days and caught ONE. We went back to the ugly OG fly strips….. caught 6 in an hour …. IMO not worth the money.

Este producto es muy fuerte el olor no se puede asta ni respirar a mi medio dolor de cabeza mareos y náuseas y que solo lo puse unas horas y mejor lo quité y lo tiré no lo recomiendo es demasiado fuerte el olor

reviewTitle: Just an open jar of apple cider vinegar
reviewDescription: These make your house smell like vinegar! And they can spill very easily because there’s no protection over the top. None! Not effective, no difference sitting putting these out.

reviewTitle: It's just a small cup of diluted Apple Cider Vinegar. Not very effective.
reviewDescription: It's just a small cup of diluted Apple Cider Vinegar. Not very effective. Quite a bit of a rip-off.
This does not work bugs are all around it with no effect.

I bought both this and the yellow, sticky things that you can put in a plant. I had high hopes because I really wanted to get rid of those annoying little flys. I followed the directions and shook it well. Be careful when you open it because it spits at you like a yogurt. It wasn’t a pleasant smell, very similar to apple cider vinegar. All of that, I was willing to work with. I had it open next to where we have the most flys and after a week, it had only caught maybe 7 of those little pests. The yellow sticky card caught that many in an hour! It was absolutely not worth it and I wouldn’t waste my time again.

This product is complete garbage! It has absolutely No effect on getting rid of fruit flies! I purchased this and got a refund. Do not waste your money because this has absolutely no effect on getting rid of fruit fly flies. After the jar has evaporated to the end I still have them flying around in my apartment.
