Do not buy this!! It does not work!! Complete garbage!! After using this to the bottom of the jar not a Single fruit fly had been drawn to the liquid. NOT ONE! This should be taken off the market!!

Product Quality: Product does attract Knats immediately but sure does not kill them immediately . Knats sit on the rim of jar for days and house smells of vinegar. I’d rather use the RAID spray. This RAID product is a disappointment. I would not recommend.
I was so hoping for success. Unfortunately it doesn't work at all
Save your money. This is essentially nothing more than apple cider vinegar. Minimally effective and extremely expensive for the value of.
Worthless!

This trap may work with American mosquitoes - it is no match for Jamaican mosquitoes! I have one plugged in right at my desk and mosquitoes still eat me alive. 3 mosquitoes are flying around my elbow as I type and others are under my desk. The trap is plugged in under my desk! It only catches the little moths that are attracted to light and not the mosquitoes I had hoped it would help with.

reviewTitle: Wish I could’ve gave it five stars
reviewDescription: Like I said, I wish I could give it five stars they were great. They actually do the job they supposed to, but they have a serious floor. I bought a similar with a different company I live in the mountains where we have some fluctuation and power even just a little bit of power fluctuation not even to hurt the clocks would shut this unit off to me that’s a serious fault and needs to be corrected to Make it The serious product

reviewTitle: You get what you pay for
reviewDescription: Why did you pick this product vs others?: This is refills only

I had high hopes for these traps to rid our home of the tiny irritating flying bugs but unfortunately the traps are empty and the bugs are still flying around. We had wonderful success with the Zevo trapping flys that I was sure these would be as helpful. I’ve had them plugged in for about a week and a half and not one bug in the trap.
Didn’t work for me. Not one fly was trapped. Wouldn’t buy again.

We had these in place for two weeks in each end of the kitchen. After about a week we still noticed fruit flies in the kitchen - no improvement since using the traps. This picture is after two weeks of use. About 4 fruit flies caught. Not impressed for the price. I have made homemade traps that are much more effective.
Not very effective. Been 5 days and only 2 flys between 2 plugins.

It is very easy to set up, makes no noise. It did not really do a great job at catching fruit flies, it only caught one or two in the span of a couple of weeks. Thankfully it was almost wintertime and they disappeared, but this thing was not really that helpful.

We had an infestation. Fruit flies everywhere. Over the course of several days, these traps only caught a few small flying insects but no fruit flies. I've located the source of the bugs so the problem has been eliminated.

Cool and modern looking, does not a thing to reduce fruit flies. Enclosed literature even states that fruit flies are barely attracted to light. Catches moths and a few gnats . Instructions tell you to give it a few weeks , then it's too late for a return.......
After many attempts to catch fruit flies, I gave up. Definitely did not work. They land on the lip of the container and fly away.

Absolutely horrible. I bought this to put around a fruit bowl to deal with a small fruit fly problem. It only made the problem worse, and also made the area smell strongly of vinegar. Now, I have an even bigger fruit fly problem to deal with. Because of the product’s poor physical design. The fruit flies just sit on the rim of the container and run in circles around it. Only a a few if it all actually go in and drown.

reviewTitle: Smells like I’d rather have flies.
reviewDescription: This smells like I’d rather keep the flies… and it didn’t even attract any. For what it is, pour some cider vinegar or leftover wine in a cup with a drop of soap. I figured since it said pet safe it would be enclosed. It’s an open plastic jar full of “here kitty, come knock me over”

Bought some smaller traps from a different brand, and when they filled up, decided to try this bigger trap from Raid. Big mistake. Huge. First, most other traps include some sort of funnel or cap to let the flies in, but keep them from getting out. This one is essentially just a big open jar of apple cider vinegar. Nothing to keep the flies from escaping. Also, because it's just a big jar of vinegar, whatever room you put this in will smell like a rotten egg fart. And it will still be full of fruit flies. Do yourself a favor, get the smaller ones from a brand that works.
Strong odor, easy to spill liquid , that you have to keep open. Poorly made and ineffective, and smells up your room. Don’t attract or catch anything . Would not recommend
Just made my house smell like vinegar and didn’t caught like 3 fruit flies in a month.
Did not work and created a terrible smell through my house. Felt unsafe to use.
Terrible. Don't waste your money. It attracts them alright but it doesn't kill them. Waste of money and it stinks horribly.

Total scam. This product has not killed one fruitfly. It attracts them to the container and they all land on it but none go in the solution so it does absolutely nothing to get rid of the flies just attracts more to come out. .do not buy!! It's a scam!! Just another waste of money on a product that swears it solves the problem.

Set it out. Checked it after 24 hours. Caught zero gnats or fruit flies. Checked 24 hours later, same result. Forgot about it for about 3 days, checked it and finally caught one gnat. One gnat, one star. Gnats still buzzing my face just laughing at me for buying this junk.

reviewTitle: Does not work, the fruit flies walk on it, then fly off
reviewDescription: The fruit flies walk on it, then fly off. Total waste of time and money. I do not understand all the high reviews. Maybe I got a defective batch.

The stake to insert into soil should have been made with a hard plastic! Trying to insert the flimsy stake while trying not to touch the sticky areas or have the sticky area not touch the plant is a joke. I would not recommend nor would I purchase these again. Not happy with my purchase :(
We are having a huge problem with fruit flys so I bought these! They’ve been all over my house in every plant and I think maybe 2 got stuck on them 🤦🏻♀️ don’t waste your money

I got these along with a sticky strip that attaches directly to the window. After two weeks the window strip which was right above the pot had ~two dozen flies stuck on it. This had two. I’ll stick with the window strips. Pun intended.
I loved the shapes and thought so decorative. I had previously gotten stick stuff from ace but loved the cuts Totally useless. I have a whole pack left and not worth using

I wish I would of gotten the smaller package because now I have a bunch of them and they are useless. Does not work at all. The fruit flies are still around doesn't trap anything or grabs their attention at all. Save your money. I wish I could get a refund!!
I've had them in every plant and in my kitchen for 3 days. I've caught ONE GNAT. I gave the rest away to friends on hopes they have more luck.

The fruit flies are never attracted to the sticky part by themselves. In fact, the very few that ARE attracted land on the non sticky part instead! I literally had to shake an enclosed basket for the flies to get hurled into the sticky part. Expected better seeing the positive reviews here.
As far as I can tell this product serves no purpose. I thought it might capture some of the small gnats in my house plants…. It didn’t appear to have a single one stick to it after several days.

Fruit flies all over the counter and veggies. The trap set in the middle of the activity. The next morning, not one fly was trapped. I've placed the traps in different areas where the flys are a nuisance and again, not one fly was trapped. These are useless.
So far it's not working

So in one month we have caught a single house flys and a half dozen gnats/fruit flys! Very disappointed with this product! I would not recommend it if you have more than a flys in your home as it simply does not work well!

The original bug trapper with the curved round front is SOOO much better. Had these it for 2 months and nothing was caught in them despite so many fruit flies everywhere. My lil bowl of vinegar did better than these.

So basically the technology of light to attract is smart, the only thing is it’s not attracting for them to stick. The sticky part needs some type of fruit smell to make them want to land.. I really thought they would work…

Product was supposed to attract fruit flies and they stick to sticky paper. they only attracted about 10 after two weeks. The light burned out on one of them in 10 days. For $22.00 they were pretty much useless.

reviewTitle: Worthless as a fruit fly trap
reviewDescription: This so-called fruit-fly trap was just a container of liquid that smelled like vinegar. It is more of a fruit fly feeding station. It did provide inspiration for a trap I made myself. I used an old spice bottle with a perforated top. I filled it half-way with a mixture of cider vinegar and dishwashing liquid. The fruit flies crawled into the bottle and were unable to get back out.

reviewDescription: Do not buy this! I repeat! Do not buy this! Just let the fruit flies dance!

reviewTitle: Total waste of money in my case
reviewDescription: I have been battling fruit flies in my kitchen for a while. I've tried several methods including fly strips as well as multiple homemade traps using differing methods (mason jar with apple cider vinegar & dish soap, wine bottles with a bit of wine left, mason jars with apple cider vinegar *and* rotting fruit with saran wrap & holes poked, jars with paper funnel, etc.) I've even heated the apple cider vinegar before setting out the traps to produce an aroma of the fermenting vinegar/fruit. I had high hopes for this product but it literally only caught 1 or 2 fruit flies. I caught many more with just regular fly strips that catch them by chance. I left this product in two separate places in my kitchen for over a week and the fruit flies ignored it completely. Total waste of money in my case. I got rid of my fruit flies by actively spraying them with a spray bottle filled with dish soap & water, fly strips, and pouring boiling water down the drain every day (and being diligent about not leaving food crumbs out). Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch did not help at all and was a waste of money in my case. The fruit flies I had completely ignored this product (which smells like it is simply apple cider vinegar anyway).

reviewTitle: An expensive version of a cheap home remedy that doesn't work
reviewDescription: I probably should have researched the product a bit more before buying it. After opening it it became very obvious from the smell and look what it was. It's literally just a bottle of apple cider vinegar with a few drops of soap. It even says so in the ingredient list "vinegar, water, ethyl lactate(aka soap)". To be fair it does kind of say that in the description so that's on me. Does it work? - About as well as apple cider vin and soap does (because that's what this product is). I've heard of this working for others so that's what I tried before buying this. For me I caught about 5 flies with my home made stuff and 3 with this product. My other home made trap was a bottle with a small amount of fruit juice and sealed with some plastic wrap with a few small holes. That has caught around 50. Still have an infestation. This, along with the home made stuff, will make the whole area smell of vinegar. If you really want to try this method then just make it yourself for half the cost. Most people have acv and soap laying around anyways. And if it doesn't work then at least you'll have left over soap you can use to clean and acv you can cook with.

I did not expect to open the bottle and find that it was a wide-open design and thus easily spillable. I have pets (including a very inquisitive and ungraceful cat), so I can't put it in certain places. I did try to put one in the kitchen but a tiny spider on the windowsill nearby is catching more fruit flies than this trap. The smell of vinegar is quite strong and not pleasant, and so far the fly trap hasn't caught a single obnoxious flying anything.
This has been put on my counter for over a day. There is not a single fruit fly in it. Definitely would not recommend. Terro traps are much more effective.

I finally received this item after a lengthy shipping period. Both bottles had the caps on wrong and all of the liquid was gone, the packaging is wet and smells like the product. Probably left a trail of fruit fly fluid the whole way.

reviewTitle: Didn't work for me AT ALL
reviewDescription: I have several fruit flies around the spyder plants in my kitchen (and near a counter at the other end of the kitchen where I have bananas hanging). I bought this with high hopes... but no go. Initially I opened the bottle, placed it between two plants and gave it a few days. Nothing. I then poured it into four small shallow open containers and placed three around all the plants and one near the bananas. Five days later, only 1 fruit fly ended up in any of the containers (I think it got in there by accident & drowned). I can say that after all this time, the smell of apple cider is still very strong. From reading other reviews, it seems that this is either wildly successful or an absolute miss. I'll leave them out for a while longer, but for me, this was a wasted purchase. I had hoped this would have some sort attractant I didn't know about and would work, but could have saved my money and gone back to a plain old cider vinegar & dishsoap mix (which gave me the same result but was much cheaper).

reviewTitle: Caught three flies only. Put your investment elsewhere.
reviewDescription: I paid almost $8 to catch three flies. My cat would have done a better job. Basically useless did absolutely nothing to solve my problem. I would be investing in something else.

sadly they do not work - don't know why but I was hoping this took care of my problem but not happening - going back to my local hardware store to get the ones that have worked in the past and these are going back to Amazon

Don’t waste your money! Not only is this nothing more than vinegar, it is a poor design. The top is just wide open. You have to have small holes so the fruit flies can get in but not out! They were all just hanging out on the popped top diving in for a slurp of vinegar and then resting back on the top. I had more success with a small jar of vinegar with cling wrap poked with a toothpick! China is dumping their junk and Amazon is selling it to us!

Initially I had high hope for this based on what others were saying. Unfortunately these were a huge let down for me. The design is clumsy at best and is a spill waiting to happen, It’s just a wide open bottle. I have left one out for over a week now where I have an abundance of fruit flies. Not a single fly in it, they’re not even interested in these. Save your money and find a working product.

reviewTitle: Save Your $ & Make A Trap From A Old Water Bottle & Vinegar!
reviewDescription: A homemade trap from an old water bottle & vinegar with a few drops of soap works better than this any day! This simply doesn't work. The reviewers that say it does must have some bad infestations or something as I've had this in various locations for several weeks and not ONE fruit fly was captured! I even added some foil over the top and poked a few holes as some suggested and it still is a miserable failure!
The bottle is odd designed, unprofessional, unpractical.
Ineffective, and will not get the job done. You could probably make something better on your own.
Do not waste your time nor your money. Zero effectiveness. Nothing else needs to be stated. Have a nice day.

reviewTitle: Just ACV! Biggest waste of money
reviewDescription: Biggest rip off ever. This item is literally a plastic spice jar of apple cider vinegar, you could make this for 50 cents in less time it would take to order this with items in your kitchen. Actually unreal that i just paid $8 for this, should have read the ingredients. Was specifically trying to not use apple cider vinegar because it stinks. DO NOT BUY! It doesn’t work very well.

I tried this product Because i was desperate to get rid of fruit flies. It’s my nderstanding that the that the flies would be attracted to it, enter the bottle and die. If I’m correct, the product was useless,as it did not draw a single fly into it, despite my having left it open on my kitchen counter for 4 days. It just so happens that the infestation had significantly improved by the time I received and set the bottle out, and the flies were actually almost gone by the end of the 4 days, so IF the product works just by sitting open and on the counter, it MAY be that the product did work. At this time I’m just trying to be fair and offer the seller the benefit of the doubt; however, I cannot, now give the product more than one star. I invite the seller to clarify the situation, especially if the product does not need to draw the flies into it for it to work. Also, however, the odor emitted from the jar is so strong and pervasive that it made me nauseous and I doubt I could have tolerated it much longer.
Left out for about a month and caught 0 flies in my kitchen. Just dried up. Waste of money…Avoid.
Not worth the money. This does nothing. Its vinger and that's what I've been using.

High priced vinegar. Didn't work for me. Very dissapointed. I usually never take the time to write a review, but this product was so bad. A week went by and just one fruit fly. Another week and still nothing. They don't even seem to go near it. Not worth the risk of accidently spilling the contents, due to open container. Unbelievable how some people love it.

reviewTitle: I put out 2 traps & watched cloud's of fruit flies surrounding it
reviewDescription: I had an area where some fruits and vegetables had gone bad. I sat both of the items with their tops opened ( sure smelled like Apple Cider Vinegar to me) and for 3 days watched cloud's of fruit flies flying in clouds around it. I saw them land in the bottle but not a single one went into the liquid and died. I've had better luck using plain white vinegar.

You can achieve the same results BETTER by simply putting some apple cider vinegar in a small dish. This is overpriced for a container that isn’t special. And a solution that is nothing special. Ol Aunt Fannie scammed us all. Aunt Fannie… more like Aunt Farty. Don’t waste your money.
This product arrived half-empty and the little that remained did not attract any fruit flies. Zero. Don't waste your money.

This didn’t work at all. I left it out for several weeks and it didn’t do anything. There isn’t really a trap, it’s just a bottle of vinegar, with a fully open flip top lid, that the gnats can fly in and out of. I feel like this was a complete waste of money and time. It needs a better design as there isn’t a way to actually trap the bugs.

I bought this brought June 2025. I opened the lid and placed it on the kitchen counter next to the fruit bowl. I let it sit there in disturbed until September 3, 2025, absolutely nothing happened to the flying fruit flies. The fruit flies ignored the bottle all together! Do not waste your money !

reviewTitle: No longer effective, change in formula?
reviewDescription: UPDATED REVIEW: Two years ago my apartment has very annoying fruit flies - I lived in a house of four girls, with all of our bananas and avocados and tomatoes, etc, that made it impossible to live produce free for long enough to make the bugs go away. I bought this product, and within days there were over 15 fruit flies that it had gotten rid of. I bought more, and our fruit fly problem went away and stayed away. This year, with the summer heat and plethora of summer fruit, the fruit flies are back. And this time, after buying 5 of these liquid traps, none of them are working. I even see fruit flies flying immediately next to the traps, but after 4 weeks of hoping they would start working again they are not, and I am thoroughly disappointed. The company must have changed their formula for this drastic of a decline in its effectiveness.
While I do like these and they work okay… They don’t stop the source of the problem which is likely the garbage disposal or a drain. They kill off a fair amount but they keep coming.
This works okay, but not as good as store products or homemade recipes unless you change it every 3 days.

The first package was poorly wrapped and arrived with one broken bottle of liquid all over everything. Amazon replaced this with no issue. But, it now sits next to my old jar with homemade stuff in it (red wine, and a bit of dish detergent) and the flies have not used this stuff any more than the homemade. They keep flocking to my homemade stuff. So save your money and look up a nice recipe.

It caught a couple of fruit flies so that’s good. I was surprised to see a completely open top. If it gets knocked over, it will be a huge headache to clean up never mind disgusting. I’ll probably use it to refill the little apple/strawberry-shaped containers that are less likely to leak if tipped over.

The trap smelled like other vinegar based traps which I've used before. This is essentially a spice container filled with vinegar/soap solution. There is no inverted bit to keep insects from flying back out. Unfortunately they fly in but don't get caught in the trap unlike others I've used. This didn't work at all. I ended up pouring the solution into a glass with a plastic bag over the top, cut a smaller hole, and it worked better than what I was provided.

reviewTitle: Works but so does Apple Cider Vinager
reviewDescription: Too expensivet. The cap is not the best for containing the bugs.

Its just ACV in an container. I thought it was more of a trap that i could refill. I'll still need to add cling wrap to the top so flies dont make their way back out. Creating your own trap with some dollar store cling wrap is a lot cheaper and more cost effective.

reviewTitle: Loved it when I bought it previously but it doesn’t seem to be working as well this time.
reviewDescription: I had purchased this product a year or two ago and I was very impressed with how well it worked as it seemed to alleviate all of the fruit flies in our kitchen within ~24 hours after putting it out. When I went to reorder a few weeks ago, I noticed that it had different packaging but didn’t think much of it. However, after having it out for several days now, there are still fruit flies flying around. Not sure if it is a different formula than what was used in the past but it doesn’t seem to be working as well this time. A bit disappointed.

This stuff smells just like vinegar. The label says there are other ingredients, but I feel like I just paid way too much for a little bottle of something I already had in my cupboard. More importantly, it didn’t really work that well. As you can see in the picture, there is a fruit fly perched on the outside of the bottle at the top of the label. I managed to catch a few of them, but it wasn’t enough to make a difference.

reviewTitle: Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch! Fruit Fly Trap ...
reviewDescription: I have been battling fruit flies for 2 weeks! Between the produce I buy and my fruit flavored vape juice they have been relentless. I just received one of these today along with a 2 pack of Terro brand "Peaches". I set all three out in my house. One Terro and this product in kitchen and one Terro in my den. The Aunt Fannie's product has eliminated more flies than the two Terro peaches combined so far (less than 24 hours). Update: You can lead a fly to a lure, but you can't make the fly jump in. I ended up finding a chemical product called PT Alpine that eliminated all the flies in my home in 48 hours.

We all of a sudden had a huge amount of fruit flys in our kitchen and bathroom areas. Tried all the home made remedies we could think of and nothing seemed to work. I bought Aunt Fannies Fruit Fly Killer from Amazon and was hoping to get rid of them all. Much to our disappointment, it did not work as well as we had hoped. It did trap a few flies, but not any better than the home made remedies we tried. We are still battling with them. We even bough a spray by RAID it does kill them but not all of them. Don't know where they are coming from or how to get rid of them
But only for about a week. Honestly this is just apple cider vinegar in a spice container. You could make it yourself if you just buy vinegar and dawn soap.

reviewTitle: There are better products on the market.
reviewDescription: Okay so I managed to get some fruit flies the first few days, but not all. Obviously the more you have, the better chance you will get at getting rid of fruit flies. I would consider it mildly effective at best though. And because the lid opens up wide without any sort of barrier, I have unfortunately knocked it over several times and had to clean up a stinky mix of smelly vinegar and dead bugs. After the initial effectiveness wore off, no more flies are taking the bait--I still have tons of flies everywhere. I think you're better off investing in a better product.

reviewTitle: Works as well as vinegar and dish soap.
reviewDescription: Works as well as dish soap and apple cidar vinegar or regular white vinegar. More expensive than the mentioned method. The container is cool but you could buy one for cheap. Good option is you do not wish to out the ingredients together yourself and pour into a container. I caught two fruit flies with it, my vinegar and dish soap caught three over a 24 hour period. Could be location of containers. I ended up swatting about 10 of them myself. So get a gooe fly swatter. The flies will land in areas and you can see them and crush their souls.

reviewTitle: Didn’t Work as fast as I thought it would
reviewDescription: I liked how easy it was to use, but it took awhile for the flies to get attracted to it, and the smell was a very strong vinegar smell.

reviewTitle: Works great but wasn’t packaged well
reviewDescription: These have historically worked great for me, but this is my first time ordering them from Amazon. Both had burst open and leaked all throughout the packaging causing a sticky mess.

Bought this for some fruit flies we had, had it opened in my room for about 4 days and in that time it caught 2 flies. It's nice because it requires very zero effort but in the same amount of time, I manually squashed maybe 2 or 3 by myself. I don't think the flies were especially attracted to it, but perhaps the unlucky ones who happen to stumble across will get caught. I ended up closing the jar and putting it away because the smell was bothering me more than it was attracting/catching the flies.

My office has a problem with drain gnats. I thought I would give this a try even though the description says it's specifically designed only for fruit flies. Well they weren't lying. Not a single gnat went in this bottle. They circle it and land right next to it but do not go in. Also, this thing just contains apple cider vinegar and a little soap. You could use an empty spice container and make the same thing at home.

reviewTitle: Impressed by the Reviews but Disappointed by the Results
reviewDescription: I have been needing a fruit fly trap desperately and had seen this was one of the top rated products. I had been hoping it’s simple design would be all there was so I left it adjacent to our sink and garbage can (a hotspot) and let it be. Two weeks later, there are only 3 measly flies down at the bottom compared to other pictures I’ve seen where there are dozens gathered down there. I’ve noticed the flies don’t actually go down in the bottle and mostly walk around the outer rim before flying off. Maybe mine are just too smart...
It’s an ok product caught some fruit flys not all of them and it just smells like vinegar and could be made at home

Omg, this stuff smells so bad! I mean maybe that’s the point to attract the bugs, but I couldn’t stand to be in the same room as an open container of this. For the most part I left it open at night and then lit a candle to get rid of the smell the next morning. To make matters worse, I ended up accidentally knocking over mine.. dead bug juice everywhere. It does work, but not enough for me to want to buy this product again.

Like a lot of reviews have mentioned, I opened this thing up with a decent number of fruit flies around and was having seemingly no success. I went ahead and left it in position near my trash can and after a few days it finally started to catch them. Not sure if they needed time to warm up to it or what, but it works decent these days for a relatively cheap product that doesn't stink up the place TOO bad.

reviewTitle: Doesn’t seem to attract fruit flies
reviewDescription: Love ease of use, but out of all the different types of traps we set out, these were the only ones that barely attracted a single fruit fly. The ones that work the best are the ones shaped like little red apples with lots of holes in the top. Those are now full of dead fruit flies, but the Aunt fannies fly punch traps have only one fruit fly trapped out of all four of the traps that we set out.
It as effective as I would have liked!
The product works okay but not as good as I expected.

Liked the dead fruit flies! Prefer not having another open liquid container on my kitchen sink, but workable if careful. Has time-limited effectiveness. DOES attract the little buggers but dont expect immediate results. Not the wayvm it's intended to work.

So I will say the product works because it did capture a lot of flies after a while. I guess my main concern was that It had to sit on the counter for a long time before anything really started to happen. Im kind of on the fence about ordering again because of that fact. other than that, the product was great, didn't have a unpleasant smell and arrived very quickly.

reviewTitle: Worked better when there were A LOT of fruit flies
reviewDescription: Originally this product worked as well as if I'd just put a cup of water on the counter. In over a couple of days since opening it, it was put to use near the source of the problem, and it lured/killed 2 fruit flies while the rest continued to fly around freely in the kitchen. Disappointed 😕 but once I put it around a higher concentration of flies, it lured more. It didn't work well when there were only 5-10 flies.

reviewTitle: Not The Best Product For Catching Fruit Flies
reviewDescription: Having a Bearded Dragon and Guinea Pigs as well as breeding my own meal worms and super worms for my Bearded Dragon and to sell we tend to get a lot of fruit flies. I bought a 6-pack but unfortunately they have not really been successful in catching the fruit flies. There have been an average of about five in each bottle. It is easy and convenient but not doing the job as we had hoped.

reviewTitle: Not effective. the "apple" fruit fly traps work better
reviewDescription: thought they were expensive apple cider vinegar..

So so. It smells like vinegar mostly and there’s no way to prevent a spill if it’s open. The flies land on the side a lot. The second one I ordered didn’t work at all. Like they got immune to it or something. Orange juice did the trick in a fraction of the time so save your money.

reviewTitle: Doesn’t kill them, only attracts them.
reviewDescription: This doesn't work, it attracts more fruit flies but does not kill them. I used it for more then 2 months and hasn't killed one… I have a lot more flies then before actually.
Ineffective -- we recently had an issue with fruit flies and this product didn't do its job. Better luck with homemade solution of water, vinegar, and dish soap. Also found sticky traps worked.

reviewTitle: Fruit Flies Roundly Ignored this substance.
reviewDescription: Have many fruit flies. Only one was drawn to this solution. Also, one of two bottles leaked all over the packaging. Returned after let it sit there on my counter tempting none of the fruit flies.

reviewTitle: Don’t buy if you love your life!
reviewDescription: Do not buy this! I repeat! Do not buy this! Within 10 minutes your house will be full of something that you cannot put your finger on other than grasping for fresh air and trying not to meet your maker. Someone decided that putting some sort of mix of ether, apple cider vinegar and some concoction of liquid from the pits of hell together and put it in a bottle said to be safe for home use and thought that was a good idea. 10/10 do not recommend! Just let the fruit flies dance!

reviewTitle: Total waste of money in my case
reviewDescription: I have been battling fruit flies in my kitchen for a while. I've tried several methods including fly strips as well as multiple homemade traps using differing methods (mason jar with apple cider vinegar & dish soap, wine bottles with a bit of wine left, mason jars with apple cider vinegar *and* rotting fruit with saran wrap & holes poked, jars with paper funnel, etc.) I've even heated the apple cider vinegar before setting out the traps to produce an aroma of the fermenting vinegar/fruit. I had high hopes for this product but it literally only caught 1 or 2 fruit flies. I caught many more with just regular fly strips that catch them by chance. I left this product in two separate places in my kitchen for over a week and the fruit flies ignored it completely. Total waste of money in my case. I got rid of my fruit flies by actively spraying them with a spray bottle filled with dish soap & water, fly strips, and pouring boiling water down the drain every day (and being diligent about not leaving food crumbs out). Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch did not help at all and was a waste of money in my case. The fruit flies I had completely ignored this product (which smells like it is simply apple cider vinegar anyway).

reviewTitle: An expensive version of a cheap home remedy that doesn't work
reviewDescription: I probably should have researched the product a bit more before buying it. After opening it it became very obvious from the smell and look what it was. It's literally just a bottle of apple cider vinegar with a few drops of soap. It even says so in the ingredient list "vinegar, water, ethyl lactate(aka soap)". To be fair it does kind of say that in the description so that's on me. Does it work? - About as well as apple cider vin and soap does (because that's what this product is). I've heard of this working for others so that's what I tried before buying this. For me I caught about 5 flies with my home made stuff and 3 with this product. My other home made trap was a bottle with a small amount of fruit juice and sealed with some plastic wrap with a few small holes. That has caught around 50. Still have an infestation. This, along with the home made stuff, will make the whole area smell of vinegar. If you really want to try this method then just make it yourself for half the cost. Most people have acv and soap laying around anyways. And if it doesn't work then at least you'll have left over soap you can use to clean and acv you can cook with.

I did not expect to open the bottle and find that it was a wide-open design and thus easily spillable. I have pets (including a very inquisitive and ungraceful cat), so I can't put it in certain places. I did try to put one in the kitchen but a tiny spider on the windowsill nearby is catching more fruit flies than this trap. The smell of vinegar is quite strong and not pleasant, and so far the fly trap hasn't caught a single obnoxious flying anything.
This has been put on my counter for over a day. There is not a single fruit fly in it. Definitely would not recommend. Terro traps are much more effective.

I finally received this item after a lengthy shipping period. Both bottles had the caps on wrong and all of the liquid was gone, the packaging is wet and smells like the product. Probably left a trail of fruit fly fluid the whole way.

reviewTitle: Well it didn’t work, also leaked out before I got it…
reviewDescription: When I say this was the biggest disappointment I have come across in along time, I mean it. Now this worked for my friend and was recommended to me so I thought, trusted review from a trusted person so why not. Yeah no, smell is much stronger than regular soap and apple cider vinegar but I was willing to smell( it in a completely different room mind you) as long as it worked. Received the product and both bottles were leaking all over the Amazon package… oh well moving on. Left it out for a couple of days and didn’t catch one bug, not a gnat, not a fruit fly, only attention it caught was mine cause that smell was STRONG. You would have better luck just making a concoction yourself or buying a big light for gnats and fruit flies- that’s what I did when I returned this. Would not recommend to anyone but my enemy.

reviewTitle: Not worth it for the price
reviewDescription: I just don’t like it

reviewTitle: Good concept but not great results
reviewDescription: Still waiting. One fruit fly caught. My resolution - take caution using - pouring hot water from the tea kettle down all drains - it eradicates living fruit flies and the eggs if they have laid any in your drains. Haven't seen a bug since! To be sure, I have treated every night before bedtime for 4 days. Total success!

One can came empty. Amazon won’t respond I can’t get the seller to respond. So I pours half the liquid from one into the other and set them out. It’s been two days not a single fruit fly. :( I had high hopes but it really doesn’t work for me.
Stinks and leaking. I don’t know why I expected it to have a way too keep the bugs in, like you do when you set up a home made one. But there’s no way to trap them in.

reviewTitle: Works BUT see the photos attached and you decide
reviewDescription: The FlyPunch works, BUT,... I had much better luck with a simple household product that didn’t cost me but a few pennies. Both traps were set at the same time 48-52 hours ago. FlyPunch and a bowl of apple cider Venegar with 2-3 drops of dish soap stirred in. Traps were set 3-4 inches apart on the kitchen window. Pictures attached that shows a massive difference between the 2 traps. I was able to count 8 fruit flies in the FlyPunch and far too many to count in the apple cider vinegar mix Can’t say if the FlyPunch would have worked better with the cling wrap, often saw fruit flies sitting on the rim but not too many died. I simply followed the directions on the bottle and did the same with the apple cider trap I googled. 🤷🏽♂️ Left for Thanksgiving and forgot to throw out the bananas, and Shazam,... lots of fruit flies when we returned. 48-52 hours later 99% are gone. You decide which trap worked better.
Had high hopes this product would work… But… Not one fruit fly took the bate.. very disappointed!

I bought this product because I began to have fruit flies in my apartment. I wanted something easy, something that didn't require much work, so I thought this would be perfect. Just open and forget it. Well after almost two weeks it only caught two fruit flies, just TWO. I was pretty disappointed. I ended up just buying one of those electrical fan things that have a light that are made to trap fruit flies via the fan down onto a glue board. I ran this for two weeks and caught almost 60! So this fan thing worked way better than this liquid. And to top it off, no refunds are allowed. Very shady in my opinion. If my fan trap caught 60 and this liquid caught 2, then it obviously if faulty and I should have the option to get my money back. Do not waste your money on this product.

Sorry I ordered this and it will be going down the drain. The description says it’s “plant-based” and “safe around food”. But the label on the product contains several warnings, including to wear rubber gloves when handling and what to do if it gets on your skin. When it’s set up for use it’s an open bottle, just waiting to tip over. It contains Ethyl Lactate: “Ethyl lactate can be harmful if inhaled or absorbed through the skin. Prolonged contact can irritate the skin and eyes, and breathing it in can cause dizziness, lightheadedness, and passing out. Ethyl lactate is flammable and can cause serious or permanent injury”
Completely useless against gnats (fruit flies)...or any other fly infestation. A total BS product. Don't be fooled!

A black capped chickadee got stuck in my trap and it breaks my heart! I was standing near and heard the poor bird screeching and moving wildly. We had to rescue it from the glue while watching it suffer as it stuck flailing. We removed it, and placed it in a cardboard box. Miraculously, it flew off as we spent a few hours trying to find a vet that could care for birds in our area. It truly was the most heart wrenching thing to watch and I seriously have trauma from this incident. Me causing this ordeal by placing this trap outside makes me feel even more horrible. Please hear my warning, if you love your neighborhood birds, don't get this trap! 😭
These are useless. Put them in my garage to try catching the flys that enter, in 2 summers, it hasn't caught 1 fly. In the trash it goes

I am not one who normally does reviews especially negative ones but I just had to on this. It came is the box and the sticky part of the trap was stuck up the side of the box instead of me exposing the sticky part after removing from packaging. The next issue I had was I hung it up and within 3 hours the stick on both of the ones I bought came apart from the top that it was hanging from and fell on my desk and got that sticky glue all over my work. I mean I didn’t expect it to be the best quality because of the price but I at least expected it to do what it was advertised to achieve.

Do not recommend. I live in the Central Valley of CA where the flies are an absolute nightmare in the summer. They like to hang out by my back door and despite having multiple of these products hung in clear sight with honey and overripe fruit to try to attract the flies, there have been zero flies caught. Just a couple gnats.

reviewTitle: Sticks more to your hands than the flies
reviewDescription: Immediately when I opened it up the red pull string was covered in the glue, which had apparently leaked out, which was pretty annoying to clean off, which was much like sap. Placed it in a fly heavy area both indoors and out for a total of about 48 hours, the first thing it caught was a moth, and then two carpenter bees (both of the bees managed to get unstuck after about 5 hours), and only after it being strung up for maybe 12 hours in a fly infested area did it manage to catch 2 real flies, but that didn't last long because when I woke up the next day they both had managed to get unstuck and fly away. I don't know how this is supposed to work, because besides the fact that it has nothing to entice a fly to actually land on it, such as a faint smell, everything except one tiny moth managed to break free within a few hours of being caught.

reviewTitle: The box I got either was very poor quality, or very old.
reviewDescription: Little unidentified little black bugs was the objective. The 1st roll pulled down, but the adhesive strip remained in a tightly wound spiral; did not open up. Second roll opened in same way, but the cardboard tube container pulled off when the roll was partway open. Tried pulling down by hand and unwinding the tight spiral, but as soon as I let go of the bottom of the paper, the bottom sprung back up and ended in a tangled mess. The adhesive is very sticky; more so than a good roll. The entire experience I had with a few containers purchased ca. 5 yrs. ago. When the package was new I used one of the four strips and it worked ok. 4-5 yrs. later I went to use the remaining 3 strips, but with similar results as described above.

These were a complete waste of my money, set up 6 of these inside & outside. Hung up, stood up, laid sideways, added a little honey. With all of this in over a week, caught 1 fly & 2 gnats. The flies won’t go near the traps, better off using a fly swatter.

Purchased thinking this product had to be GREAT.. it is a Great company. NOPE it is GARBAGE!!! Imagine. You pull this out of the container. First off it doesn't pull out easily.. so the sides stick together... Okay you deal with that part. Then you try and put the tack through the hole to hang it up. Thinking there is more dang glue on the loop then anywhere else. You get the tack in finally.. then you attempt to push the tack into surface. SHOCK.. it breaks. The fly strip falls and lands right on you. Now you are wearing the fly paper. Now you are thinking.. really? Yeah.. I even tried it again with another one in the box. Same thing. The tacks are GARBAGE and I could get nothing else to hang them.. so they all went in the GARBAGE. Please do not waste your money. Buy a more expensive brand. Some things are worth a little more. TRUST me.

reviewTitle: Messy not great at attracting flies
reviewDescription: Messy. Have to put honey or syrup in bottom cup without touching sticky area. Flies all over with product hanging at edge of area. One or two on trap 20 flying around right by it. Like super glue if touched. Only hung 1 out of six. Going back to fly bags.

reviewTitle: IT CATCHES NOTHING.....DON'T BUY THIS USELESS JUNK!....
reviewDescription: THIS STUFF IS JUST JUNK.....IT CATCHES NOTHING.....AFTER HANGING FOR OVER A MONTH.....THEY CAUGHT ABSOLUTELY NO BUGS.....I HAD TO SPEND MY TIME SWATTING THE BUGS.....IF I COULD GIVE ZERO STARS I WOULD HAVE..... DON'T BUY THIS USELESS JUNK!....
Would leave 0 stars if I could. Bought 6 of them and caught 2 flies. We are have a plague of flies in Arkansas and these do not catch flies. I even baited them with syrup like it said to. Nothing.

Yet another item wherein some spreadsheet doofus figured out a way to save 7 cents per unit by making the item an insult to the customer. First you stick it to you window, then you remove the backing to the side that's gonna catch flys. But there is no tab or extension that enables you get hold of the backing. It is exactly the same size and shape of the sticky surface you are supposed to separate it from. Make sure the kids are in the other room, because there will be bad language.
variant: Size: 1 pack, Style: Fly Window Strip

reviewTitle: USELESS PRODUCT: Buy a couple fly swatters. They work better.
reviewDescription: I bought a pack of these as we are having renovations done on our home and lots of doors are open this summer which makes for an increase in flies. Well after playing "wack a mole" with my fly swatter for a day, I decided I needed something with less human intervention. So I purchased a pack of these, hung them up, followed the directions and waited... And waited... And waited... And then realized the flies laugh at this thing and don't pay any attention to it. I've killed more flies in a week with my fly swatter than these have caught. Giant waste of money. Waste of pouring syrup into the things to "attract" the flies too. You're better off buying a bunch of fly swatters and keeping them in each room than owning these. I used two of them and threw the rest out as they are ABSOLUTELY USELESS. 0 STARS IF I COULD!

“Fly paper” is a good name for this product but not mosquito. I placed it in the bedroom, bathroom, back porch, and garage. It catches small beetles, moths, flies, etc. and since it’s passive, I assume I will see a mosquito eventually. I went back to my fly swatter.

I bought two of these. Seems like for some reason or another awhile swarm of flies hatched inside my house recently. I put one in the kitchen and one where most of them hang out in my living room. Been there for over a week and I caught one fly on each trap only. You have to add honey or syrup on the stick which I did one of each. I am wondering if the fact that they were left in the rain when delivered had an effect on them. They were packaged in a brown paper envelope and we're not sealed inside a plastic bag inside so the packaging was wet and soggy. Either way I was very disappointed in this purchase.

Hung this on my ventahood as I've been having a severe fly problem. Poured syrup in the bottom cup as instructed. A couple of flies went to drink the syrup and drowned. Flies were not attracted to the sticky column. I stuck some cat food kibble on it (as that's what they seem to love when it's in a bowl). I checked later and there were about ten flies stuck. I left it overnight and the next morning, the top cup (attached to the hook) had evidently let go of the column. Luckily I had an old pizza pan underneath it. See photos.

reviewTitle: Waste of money it's a yellow surface not shiny silver to attract the Flies
reviewDescription: I have purchased this in the store but it had a silver shiny surface these particular ones have yellow I have a serious fly problem and my neighborhood and they always get in my house when I had the silver surface ones they would be filled up within a couple of days and I would just throw them away I've had these out with the yellow surface 4 a week now and not one fly has landed on it I have several out in different locations I think the yellow surface is the problem because flies go towards shiny thinking it's outside and the yellow does not reflect any light so they're not attracted to it I put 5 stars on the value price because one of these will cost you $3 in the store however if they're not working I guess they're not a good value at all why did they make them with a yellow surface if I had known that I wouldn't have gotten them

reviewTitle: Has attracted tiny fruit flies not flies
reviewDescription: I bought this to trap house flies. They fly all around it and in 13 days I have only caught 1 fly. I am getting pretty good with my fly swattter though. However if you want to get rid of those pesky tiny fruit flies it seems to work for that. Just very little sticky. No scent that i could detect. The box says that the product is guaranteed. But when you order off Amazon you don’t have a receipt and they want you to mail the receipt for a refund. I would only recommend for fruit flies.
Flies dont stick And the loop to hang it is so small you cant hang in in a good place

This should never be used outdoors. I would say it should be banned. Yesterday evening I put one outdoors to try it out, near where we have a gazebo. This morning I noticed it was on the ground. I thought may be kids dropped it on the ground. When I got closer to it I realized to my absolute horror it had caught a bird! It was still trying to get away from the trap, the whole thing was covered with the poor birds feathers. I helped the poor creature, I freed the bird, but it is no longer able to fly because it has lost so much of it's feathers and parts of it's wings are all stuck together because of the glue from the trap. I doubt that bird will survive without being able to fly. I cannot use this product outdoors. I do not mind killing a few annoying flies, but there is no way I can allow other animals being harmed. How can this product be legal outdoors? I would like to return these, including the one covered with bird fathers.
Didn’t work at all. Got for a fruit fly problem, the fruit flies cared less about these supposed traps. A sticky mess that all went out to the garbage without a single fly on them.
The fly trap will not streak out like it should. It broke apart instead of rolling out.

reviewTitle: Sticky not always a good thing!
reviewDescription: Why did you pick this product vs others?: Price. Bad choice. The sticks themselves are skinny and do not have a secure base, thus the sticks fall over getting sticky all over anything in it's way. Including your hands. And it doesn't wash off as easily as they imply. Hot water, soap and a scrub brush are needed. I'm sure any fly would get stuck to these sticks if you can keep them upright. I had previously bought another brand of fly stick - much easier to set out, didn't tip or catch unwanted, miscellaneous items (an empty soda can and the cat included). I don't recommend Black Flag.

reviewTitle: Fly trap, just a money trap!
reviewDescription: We frequently have flies in and around our carport and front door. Which then in turn the flies enter our house. Since there were 6 in this package, I figured we could protect the inside and the outside. I have had the fly traps set up inside and outside of our home. Still the same amount of flies around and not ONE is stuck on the sticky traps! SIX sticky traps around and not one darn fly has been "caught." I do know that the sticky stuff works as the first one I opened my thumb hit the sticky stuff. So, if I got sticky, the flies should darn well get stuck!! I am usually not one to come down so hard on products, but with this one they failed! Can't return because they are opened. Big disappointment! Waste of money!

I put these where I had tons of flies in 3 different places. Added honey like instructions said. Which is ridiculous as they should be ready to use. And they didn't catch one fly. Absolutely worthless

Worthless, really hard to pull off the backer for the adhesive. And tiny square, does not hold on to window. So if you push it hard to window it sticks to selt and pretty much seals all closed. Mine fell of in 5 minutes, when I had it on a south window on a 90 degree day. Tried two, don’t stick!
variant: Size: 1 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewDescription: No issue with the product; I use them every year along with fly predators and they work great. The issue is Black Flag, and their inability to correct their own shipping errors. I ordered a case of 6 fly strips and the same afternoon, received a box containing a single fly strip. Amazon instructed me to resolve with Black Flag directly. After waiting on hold approximately 25 minutes I finally spoke to a very nice and apologetic representative. However, the remedy she offered less than met expectations. She was not able to simply ship out the missing five pieces, or even a replacement case of 6 - they do not have have the ability to take consumer orders at all. They can only issue a refund check which will take 4-6 WEEKS to arrive after they receive a copy of the invoice via email, fax, or mail. I would give the product 5 stars and Black Flag 0 if I could since 1 is the minimum. It’s a big company that’s been around a long time and they should have their act together.

reviewTitle: I can't recommend this to anyone serious about dealing with a fly ...
reviewDescription: I watched as two flies landed on one of these sticks and . . . flapped their wings and pulled their feet out and flew away. Shouldn't happen, ever. You should know that this fly stick does not attract flies. It's purely an "accident" when one lands on it. The instructions say to put a teaspoon of honey in the bottom which helps a little, but not much. Maybe the problem is that these were "made in China." I'm getting the impression that Made in China is a code phrase meaning "inferior, don't expect much." If you want a fly stick that really works, try a STARBAR fly stik. There is one that is about two feet long and orange (and Made in the USA). It really works! Here's an observation about this Black Flag Fly Stick: Ninety percent of the flies it catches are caught on their backs or sides, as if in their struggles to free a leg or legs they managed to hit the stick with a wing. The Starbar fly stik (their spelling) catches any fly that touches it with even one "foot/leg," but this Black Flag stick isn't nearly as sticky. Interesting to observe two flies just buzz their way off and not stay caught. All in all, I can't recommend this to anyone serious about dealing with a fly problem.

reviewTitle: Clear 3x12 window sheets are FAR SUPERIOR
reviewDescription: This is the 2nd black flag product (the other was the sticky hanging pipes, theyre okay) that ive bought in bulk and was expecting to get the 3x12 clear sticky window sheets. Now i understand i should have zoomed in closer on my phone to look at the teeny tiny little picture of the product but when i read sticky fly window trap I thought i had it right. Anyways i got home very excited knowing the package had arrived. Ive got about 40 flies buzzing around my apartment right now and when i opened the box i got some little triangle box thing with sticky paper inside it. I was a little upset but said "hey, maybe theyll work pretty well. Might as well try right?" Well ive placed 10 in my apartment. Its been a couple days and ive caught 3 flies... 3 flies!! Thats it! Not only that but the tiny strip of tape doesnt hold the weight of the cardboard and they keep falling off the windows. The clear strips will catch at least 10 flies in under an hour and in a matter of days are covered in flies. Now im not trying to be rude here black flag but this specific product sucks in comparison. It is a waste of money in my opinion.
variant: Size: 12 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewTitle: i thought the black flag name would mean a good product, WRONG
reviewDescription: Why did you pick this product vs others?: i thought the black flag name would mean a good product, WRONG. i added honey to the bottom cup per instructions and hung 1 on the front porch and a second in the kitchen. there were house flies buzzing in both locations. its been 3 days now and not a single fly has been caught by these traps. a waste of money.
Does not work. The flies aren’t attracted to the trap.

I bought two of these and left them out for three weeks. Neither caught a single fruit fly/gnat. I cannot believe I wasted $16 on these. I do not understand how anyone gave these good reviews! If I could give 5 negative stars, I would.

I have been struggling with fruit flies for a few weeks now and I have been trying every trick in the book. I did some research and heard about these and thought oh finally something easy to help with these annoying things. I have had them out for two days now and nothing….it attracts the fruit flys but they don’t go inside and if they do they come right back out. I thought I would try dumping some of it into a shallow plate but that did not work, I tried putting it in a bowl and that does not work either. I had got these little apple looking things prior and they worked pretty well so I just ordered a bunch of those alone with a drain killer to pour down my kitchen drain and hopefully soon I won’t have an issue anymore. But all in all I would not recommend these.

Purchased this product, and was very excited to use it since I have a pesky fruit fly problem. The bottles came broken. Some of the contents spilled into the packaging. Contacted customer support from the dealer and they do not do refunds since I bought through Amazon. Decided to use the product anyway, from what I had. I’ve had it open for 3 days now. It has caught one fly. All the other flies just fly around it. Product did not work for me. Don’t waste your money.

reviewTitle: It hasn't caught a single fly...
reviewDescription: When we open the back door, we've discovered flies congregating by the door, and invariably, at least a couple of flies fly in the door. (We have to go in and out a lot to walk dogs). I got one of those battery-operated fly eliminators that look like a tennis racket, thinking I could get them right at the door, but I'm not fast enough. We tried sticky fly paper, which works quite well, but it isn't attractive. I thought we'd give this a try. In four days, it hasn't attracted a single fly. There is nothing in containers. I got six. I put two out. I'm very disappointed because these containers sure look better than fly paper. I will update if there's any change but so far, not one fly...

This product doesn’t do much if you’re looking at drastically improving a gnat infestation. Get a drain cleaner instead. This is too gentle and doesn’t do much. Save your money. I wish I could return this lol

We had a lot of fruit flies upon returning from vacation. We tried these traps and caught only two flies with them in two weeks! The jars do not have any way to trap the flies, the lid is wide open, and this is a flaw of design. If the lid had smaller holes in it, the flies would get trapped in there. The flies fly into these traps and do not stay there--whatever is in it does not attract or kill them. We got rid of our fruit flies using yellow sticky glue traps and other liquid fruit fly traps that have a better attractant and design to keep the flies trapped. And also good old-fashioned home-made vinegar, water, dish soap traps helped--with a dash of apple-cider vinegar as attractant or small piece of fruit in it.
Strong vinegar smell, but tolerable. After 2 months, only 6 fruit flies. My leftover glass of wine works much better. Leave a small bowl of wine or juice overnight. Much more effective.
Literally did nothing. Left it out for 5 days and 0 flies went in. Waste of money

reviewTitle: It didn't work this time, formula change?
reviewDescription: Unlike the same product we purchased previously that worked really well, these two bottles were completely ineffective. Not a single fruit fly was caught, while my used wine glasses attracted many. I am not sure if there was any formula changes, but these simply didn’t work at all.

reviewTitle: Didn't work for me AT ALL
reviewDescription: I have several fruit flies around the spyder plants in my kitchen (and near a counter at the other end of the kitchen where I have bananas hanging). I bought this with high hopes... but no go. Initially I opened the bottle, placed it between two plants and gave it a few days. Nothing. I then poured it into four small shallow open containers and placed three around all the plants and one near the bananas. Five days later, only 1 fruit fly ended up in any of the containers (I think it got in there by accident & drowned). I can say that after all this time, the smell of apple cider is still very strong. From reading other reviews, it seems that this is either wildly successful or an absolute miss. I'll leave them out for a while longer, but for me, this was a wasted purchase. I had hoped this would have some sort attractant I didn't know about and would work, but could have saved my money and gone back to a plain old cider vinegar & dishsoap mix (which gave me the same result but was much cheaper).

reviewTitle: Caught three flies only. Put your investment elsewhere.
reviewDescription: I paid almost $8 to catch three flies. My cat would have done a better job. Basically useless did absolutely nothing to solve my problem. I would be investing in something else.

sadly they do not work - don't know why but I was hoping this took care of my problem but not happening - going back to my local hardware store to get the ones that have worked in the past and these are going back to Amazon

Don’t waste your money! Not only is this nothing more than vinegar, it is a poor design. The top is just wide open. You have to have small holes so the fruit flies can get in but not out! They were all just hanging out on the popped top diving in for a slurp of vinegar and then resting back on the top. I had more success with a small jar of vinegar with cling wrap poked with a toothpick! China is dumping their junk and Amazon is selling it to us!

Initially I had high hope for this based on what others were saying. Unfortunately these were a huge let down for me. The design is clumsy at best and is a spill waiting to happen, It’s just a wide open bottle. I have left one out for over a week now where I have an abundance of fruit flies. Not a single fly in it, they’re not even interested in these. Save your money and find a working product.

reviewTitle: Save Your $ & Make A Trap From A Old Water Bottle & Vinegar!
reviewDescription: A homemade trap from an old water bottle & vinegar with a few drops of soap works better than this any day! This simply doesn't work. The reviewers that say it does must have some bad infestations or something as I've had this in various locations for several weeks and not ONE fruit fly was captured! I even added some foil over the top and poked a few holes as some suggested and it still is a miserable failure!
The bottle is odd designed, unprofessional, unpractical.
Ineffective, and will not get the job done. You could probably make something better on your own.
Do not waste your time nor your money. Zero effectiveness. Nothing else needs to be stated. Have a nice day.

reviewTitle: Just ACV! Biggest waste of money
reviewDescription: Biggest rip off ever. This item is literally a plastic spice jar of apple cider vinegar, you could make this for 50 cents in less time it would take to order this with items in your kitchen. Actually unreal that i just paid $8 for this, should have read the ingredients. Was specifically trying to not use apple cider vinegar because it stinks. DO NOT BUY! It doesn’t work very well.

reviewTitle: Didn't work- not a single fly
reviewDescription: We've been having a massive Fruit Fly issue and been trying everything we could to get it under control. I had high hopes for these but we've had two of them open for a week now and not a single Fruit fly has gone for these. We've moved them around to different areas of our pantry and kitchen and nothing. We've had better luck with homemade traps using dish soap w/ red wine or cider vinegar. They still aren't managing the situation but at least SOME dead flies have resulted from those.
I bought a two pack of these….they both collectively caught three gnats. Mind you, I’m having an infestation right now. My own home made ACV and sugar water with dawn dish soap worked way better.

reviewTitle: Charging for 12 bottles and sending just 1 bottle
reviewDescription: Ordered the 12 pack for $80 and received just one bottle. Requested a replacement and received one bottle instead of 12 again despite a charge for a 12 pack

reviewTitle: Don’t waste your time or money!!
reviewDescription: This was an absolute freaking waste of money. I am so irritated because it was one of those products that really stood out to me, like no way thousands of people are being dishonest and rating it highly consistently. Boy was I wrong. So, I have a summer time issue with fruit flies (due to fruit). Could not get rid of them for anything. I used the katchy, stem spray, and this product. I had my home PROFESSIONALLY cleaned, opened one of these jars, and left for a 2-week vacation. I just knew I would be coming back to a fruit fly free home thanks to this product working it’s magic. No such luck. I’ll stop there, but just take it from me. It is a waste of money, so don’t get your hopes up.

If I can give zero stars, I would, but it makes me pick at least one. Total waste of money. I’ve worked in the bar industry for years, I’ve seen barflies congregate around the alcohol for years. I bought this product because it said it was specifically formulated for barflies. At the three days only two barflies were caught in the syrup. I decided to buy another product and filled the bottom cup portion with Jim Bean. Within an hour, there was over a dozen flies caught in the trap. Don’t waste your money. This product does not work.
we were sent a product that was open, spilled all over the bag and one of the containers was missing half the liquid

I was having an awful problem with fruit flies in the kitchen and dining room. I read about using apple cider vinegar and water or, even better, Flypunch. I had apple cider vinegar on hand so I tried it first and ordered Flypunch on the same day. After two days of using the apple cider vinegar 95% of the fruit flies were gone. The Flypunch arrived and I figured it would clear up the remaining 5%. That was not the case. After three days of using only the Flypunch, two bottles, the fruit flies were back. I pulled out the apple cider vinegar and the next morning the flies were gone. If you want to get rid of fruit flies first try the apple cider vinegar.

This product did not work for me! I have had it out for about a week. There were only two fruit flies captured. I purchased another product which seems to have caught the ones that Aunt Fannies fly punch didn't catch. I absolutely wasted my money.

I purchased Aunt Fannie's fly punch fruit fly trap based on all of the encouraging reviews. More wasted money! It smelled just like vinegar when I opened it and left it out for quite a few days with quite a few fruit flies .They were not interested! I have a picture of the open container of the fly punch next to a tuna fish can filled with regular apple cider vinegar and a few pieces of old fruit. Regular apple cider vinegar caught more fruit flies compared to the fly punch which caught none.

Hasn't caught anything!! It ended up getting knocked over... what a mess!! It smells like vinegar. My son found a DIY recipe online and it's only caught a few but it was free!! I did a thorough cleaning with bleach on counters, floors, and cabinets and it's adequate a noticeable difference for today anyway!! Save your money... or throw it away but don't give it to these people!!

When I got my product one bottle the lid was loose and had leaked all out in package . So didn’t even get to use it. We shall see if it really works as of now not sure. Don’t like the lid has to be completely open should have some kind of guard where the liquids won’t leak out if tipped over

reviewTitle: seems my used wine glasses that sit on the counter overnite get more attention from the fruit flys
reviewDescription: not what i expected . takes quite a while to attract the fruit flys

reviewTitle: It works better for me than home-made.
reviewDescription: A lot of people are upset that this is basically just the home remedy of apple cider vinegar and soap. And yeah, it is, but I’m catching way more of the flies with this than the home brew, so Aunt Fannie’s doing something right. I wish it was a little more enclosed though, because the open mouth with the unwieldy flip top is begging for it to get tipped over and spill.
I love aunt fannies products, this one just did not work for the little flies in my house. I’ve done everything and they won’t go away, so it may not be this products fault.
Did not kill the fruit flys
Although it worked,it didn’t perform as I hoped
The flies don't go in. Doesn't work.

It attracts the flies, but the lip is perfect for them to have a little snack and not fall in. I watched one fly in yesterday so I know it works. I've removed the lid to see if that is the problem. You could probably make one of these yourself if you wanted to save like 4 bucks considering what you need to make this included.

reviewTitle: Not sure what it supposed to do 🤔
reviewDescription: So to be honest. I'm really not sure what it is supposed to do. According to the description it should trap the fruit fly but it didn't trap even one but it repelled them where I don't have any in my house anymore which the goal. So I'm happy with it. It did deliver what I paid for even though it is the opposite outcome🤪🤪

reviewTitle: cider vinegar works just as well and is cheaper
reviewDescription: We went on vacation and forgot to take out the trash so when we returned home the house was full of fruit flies. I set out little dishes of cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap to get rid of them but they were not disappearing fast enough so I purchased this to see if it would work quicker. It looks and smells just like the cider vinegar and only worked about as well. There were no more bugs in this than in the other home remedies I used so it was really a waste of money to buy it when it didn't work any better.

I’m sorry, I thought this cute little bottle with the peculiar odor would be the end of my fruit fly problem. Nope. A few of the little buggers have flown in, but not enough to make a difference. I’m also afraid I’m going to knock it over. Uggh. No re-buy for this product.
Did not work right away, over a few days noticed flys in liquid, but did not work quickly

Tt worked instantly, fruit flies hovering over it and dove into the liquid... but it slowed down considerably. It could be that I got a fair amount of them, not sure. I still see one or two/three, etc buzzing around. I've only had this for a week....so I'll try to update this review. Smells like apple cider vinegar, so if that's what is in this, you could make a home remedy
It does attract flies. The cost per volume seems high.

reviewTitle: It works but not for a large infestation
reviewDescription: This product is okay but I would highly recommend one of the products you pour into the sink and let sit overnight.

reviewTitle: These bugs are literally mocking me
reviewDescription: I have little knats in my house and I thought maybe I'd leave the formulating of these traps up to the pros. They have been out now for almost 24 hours, not 1 big captured. Update. I think I have a combined 18 dead. So... I take it back... It does work.

reviewTitle: Make Your Own For a Lower Price
reviewDescription: It's essentially apple cider vinegar with soap- you could make something like the product by yourself and for way less money. As another reviewer pointed out, the bait liquid itself will probably drown some fruit flies but to really maximize the number of fruit flies it kills, you need to stick some doubled sided sticky fly traps into the bottle. If you do this, you'll be amazed and disgusted by how much fruit flies have been lurking around your home.
Package leaks and the fluid was all over the inside of it and it smells pretty bad so that's my review

I didn't look closely at the photos and assumed the lid had small holes in it, but it's just a wide open lid. You're better off pouring some cheap apple cider vinegar and dish soap in an old cup of your own. I've noticed that this method of catching fruit flies works best when you use a paper funnel or poke holes in plastic wrap, to trap them once they're lured in. I caught more doing that than I did with this wide open on my counter.

Save yourself a few bucks and use apple cider vinegar, the cheap stuff, because that's basically all this product is, with a couple of other things thrown in that you don't really need. I've been using the same container and have just been adding some vinegar to it as needed, but you can use any container you have available. I haven't noticed any difference in their product vs. the apple cider vinegar. It still attracts them into the bottle and there they stay, in the liquid, until you dump it out and refresh it.

I tried this product Because i was desperate to get rid of fruit flies. It’s my nderstanding that the that the flies would be attracted to it, enter the bottle and die. If I’m correct, the product was useless,as it did not draw a single fly into it, despite my having left it open on my kitchen counter for 4 days. It just so happens that the infestation had significantly improved by the time I received and set the bottle out, and the flies were actually almost gone by the end of the 4 days, so IF the product works just by sitting open and on the counter, it MAY be that the product did work. At this time I’m just trying to be fair and offer the seller the benefit of the doubt; however, I cannot, now give the product more than one star. I invite the seller to clarify the situation, especially if the product does not need to draw the flies into it for it to work. Also, however, the odor emitted from the jar is so strong and pervasive that it made me nauseous and I doubt I could have tolerated it much longer.
Left out for about a month and caught 0 flies in my kitchen. Just dried up. Waste of money…Avoid.
Not worth the money. This does nothing. Its vinger and that's what I've been using.

High priced vinegar. Didn't work for me. Very dissapointed. I usually never take the time to write a review, but this product was so bad. A week went by and just one fruit fly. Another week and still nothing. They don't even seem to go near it. Not worth the risk of accidently spilling the contents, due to open container. Unbelievable how some people love it.

reviewTitle: I put out 2 traps & watched cloud's of fruit flies surrounding it
reviewDescription: I had an area where some fruits and vegetables had gone bad. I sat both of the items with their tops opened ( sure smelled like Apple Cider Vinegar to me) and for 3 days watched cloud's of fruit flies flying in clouds around it. I saw them land in the bottle but not a single one went into the liquid and died. I've had better luck using plain white vinegar.

You can achieve the same results BETTER by simply putting some apple cider vinegar in a small dish. This is overpriced for a container that isn’t special. And a solution that is nothing special. Ol Aunt Fannie scammed us all. Aunt Fannie… more like Aunt Farty. Don’t waste your money.
This product arrived half-empty and the little that remained did not attract any fruit flies. Zero. Don't waste your money.

This didn’t work at all. I left it out for several weeks and it didn’t do anything. There isn’t really a trap, it’s just a bottle of vinegar, with a fully open flip top lid, that the gnats can fly in and out of. I feel like this was a complete waste of money and time. It needs a better design as there isn’t a way to actually trap the bugs.

I bought this brought June 2025. I opened the lid and placed it on the kitchen counter next to the fruit bowl. I let it sit there in disturbed until September 3, 2025, absolutely nothing happened to the flying fruit flies. The fruit flies ignored the bottle all together! Do not waste your money !

reviewTitle: No longer effective, change in formula?
reviewDescription: UPDATED REVIEW: Two years ago my apartment has very annoying fruit flies - I lived in a house of four girls, with all of our bananas and avocados and tomatoes, etc, that made it impossible to live produce free for long enough to make the bugs go away. I bought this product, and within days there were over 15 fruit flies that it had gotten rid of. I bought more, and our fruit fly problem went away and stayed away. This year, with the summer heat and plethora of summer fruit, the fruit flies are back. And this time, after buying 5 of these liquid traps, none of them are working. I even see fruit flies flying immediately next to the traps, but after 4 weeks of hoping they would start working again they are not, and I am thoroughly disappointed. The company must have changed their formula for this drastic of a decline in its effectiveness.
Comes with nothing to attract flies… supposed to put syrup in the cup be none of my flies were interested in that…
must be old because does not pull out correctly. did catch flies tho.

I'm laughing because so far, no mosquitos have found their way to the trap so they can stick to it. I bought a six-pack two weeks ago to put inside a 12' x 20' outbuilding. I am besieged by mosquitos every time I'm in there, and every trap is as clean as when I unpackaged them. However I will say- if you have a can of Off to spray yourself with in addition to these strips- you'll be good to go.

reviewTitle: Cheap bc you have to add your own attraction
reviewDescription: Bought 2 bc they were cheap. Got what I paid for. 1 flimsy hat on top with a flimsy cheap store brand like hanging tag and a flimsy cup on the bottom.
