It's very sticky but doesn't have an attractant to the sticky roller. Per directions you must add honey or syrup. I added honey and flies are everywhere but near it. Theyre hung in open areas wear the Flys are and in 2 days not one fly landed on them. I've killed about 12 with they fly swatter.

reviewTitle: not very effective in my house
reviewDescription: I bought this product because we have a fly problem in our kitchen. Three dogs go to the back yard through the kitchen door for relief and running, and we leave the door open so they can return when they are ready. (No way we will stand around and wait--sometimes the dogs are out for half an hour or longer.) So we get a fair number of houseflies (and some other flying insects) in the kitchen. (If we put up a screen door, the dogs would destroy it. The smallest dog is 70 pounds, and the largest is 100 pounds and can be aggressive.) This fly paper was hung near the kitchen door but catches very few flies; we have to use a swatter on most flies in the kitchen.

This time of year we have lots of house flies, fruit flies & gnats. This was a test purchase before signing up for auto delivery. Baited with pancake syrup (as instructed) and hung from the ceiling in the middle of the shop. Six(6) days later and we have an entrapment of TWO(2) gnats. NO flies!!!, They are still happily buzzing around the shop pestering the good folks who are trying to work.

reviewTitle: A plain piece of paper would have worked better
reviewDescription: I've used these, different brand, years ago when I worked at a stable. Recently had a critter expire in the crawl space in my house. By the time we found the thing one of my kitchen windows had over a dozen flies around it. Hung a few of these up in the near the window. A week later, flies are still in the window and papers haven't been touched, not a single one. What a total waste.

So I've had these fly sticks in our home, for just over 3-weeks now, and this is all that it's attracted/caught (see image)! We live near the beach where a very fresh breeze blows almost daily; so we tend to open all the windows, and terrace doors wide open (this helps curb what's usually a super-high monthly electric bill). Anyways, that said, flies tend to come in and circle around the middle of our rooms (like 6-7 on average in each room) throughout the day. They pay absolutely no attention to these stick, even with fresh syrup poured in the reservoir as instructed. Three weeks in, and all it's caught is one fly and a couple gnats. No thanks!

the "other" name brand sticks work great these not so much. Def not enough sticky on them I watched a fly land on it then take right off again. The other sticks are white, these are yellow not sure if color matters, but the other ones my stick was completely covered with fly's and hardly any on these. I would not buy again

Not a good product. I literally followed and trapped a fly for about an hour and the fly would not go to this stick trap. Waited and waited and nothing. Also tried it on a large swarm of gnats that came in through our exhaust fan in an our door bathroom. Strange occurrence but I place 2 of these sticks and shut the door and only 2 gnats attached to it. There were thousands of gnats. My white doors and crown moulding were full of them so there was my chance to try this Black Flag insect trap. A bomb worked best. So don’t waste your money.

I think flies are entering my room through my poorly sealed air conditioner. I hung one fly paper on one window and one on another window, the flies like to congregate in the windows especially when i'm running around after 10 of them with a fly swatter. Never once did I have even one fly attach itself to these fly papers. The fly paper attached itself to my arm and fly swatter several times. My hair once... that was not fun. Vinegar didn't work to get it out, but shampoo fixed that mess. I threw them all away after that moment after having them up for 2 weeks and everyday killing all flies with the fly swatter. Since I fixed the air conditioner cracks with foam tape I've had no more trouble with flies.

We bought a package of six. We live in Florida and the flies are terrible this year. We placed them all around the house with the honey added to them. Three days later all they attracted was one gnat and one mosquito. What a waste of money!

reviewTitle: Completely useless and seems to be actually repelling flies
reviewDescription: As I write this, I'm watching a fly buzz around a window where I stuck one of these traps. It absolutely DOES NOT attract flies and in the couple of instance where the fly did happen to fly close to it, it quickly turned away as if the trap was actually repelling the fly. Yesterday there were two buzzing around the window and one of them would sometimes land a few inches away from the trap and walk around on the window as if no trap was there. I've also stuck several of these in various other areas of the house where the flies frequent and NONE of them have any flies on them. The dogs are better at catching flies than these traps are. Complete waste of time and money.

reviewTitle: Do NOT touch these with any part of your body.
reviewDescription: I do not really know if this product works for flies or not. BUT, the hook by which each piece is hung is totally inadequate. The first time, I accidentally bumped the bottom and it instantly fell. Reacting, I grabbed for it and caught it. Trust me. You do NOT want to touch this. The recommended cleanup with soap and water is total B.S. First soap, then alcohol, then GooGone, and finally the solvent Goof Off. And even that took repeated applications and my hands were STILL sticky, as were the various containers of clean-up liquids. Then today, I accidentally bumped it again...lightly. And down it came onto the back of my hands. As soon as I was finally able to get it off my hands, I sat down to arrange a return.
Didn’t work. I still have flies, and their offspring.

reviewTitle: My flies just laughed at it.
reviewDescription: I have regular house flies, but I think they are nose blind. They fly around and stop for a rest on top of this thing. Directions say to add honey or syrup to the base. Ok did that, set it in a closed up freshly cleaned room, just it and the flys. 24 hours later, not a single fly stuck to it. Actually watched a fly stop on top of it before buzzing off. Checked the internet for fly bait. Came up with apple cider vinegar. Added apple cider vinegar, closed up the room and waited. 24 hours later, 1 gnat, still 0 flies. Watched for a couple minutes, fly landed on top just like before. I don’t know what these things are good for, but it’s certainly not good at catching flies. TOTAL WASTE OF $18

Save your money and spend a little more for Raid fly sticks. They accidentally catch a fly or two but not gnatty fruit flies. I've had 2 out in 2 different areas for over 2 wks and still have fruit flies. Raid fly sticks would have been full after 3 days. Very disappointing.

I followed instructions and hung the fly paper inside the house, in the kitchen. I hung it on a Thursday, one week later, we had not caught one fly. It’s easier to spray window cleaner on the flies to eliminate them.

Hung these in my trailer to try to catch some of the horrible TX black flies that invaded our camp trailer this summer. I followed the directions and hung these (2 of them in a 26' trailer) and waited for the flies to start to be drawn in like steel to a magnet. Got up the next morning, and all we had caught was a single moth. Flies were still happily buzzing around the trailer and our heads as we tried to eat breakfast. I figured maybe they need a little attractant to bring 'em into the paper. I took a little piece of bacon that we had for breakfast and stuck it to the glue. Came back to the trailer later that afternoon and not a single fly was stuck. Don't know what's wrong with these, but they sure didn't work for me. Glad I didn't pay much for them! I hope you found this review to be helpful, please click below to indicate that you did. Thanks!

We host an annual 4th of July party and people are in and out of the house all day long. By the next morning, I always have a house full of flies. So I thought I’d be smart and have these on hand for the aftermath. Pfft. I set two out in the kitchen, poured my syrup as directed, even added a piece of hotdog to one to help entice the little buggers. After three days, I caught one fly on the trap! One! The rest went down by way of the almighty fly swatter. On the hopes of these working well, I foolishly bought a half dozen. Total waste of money!

Arrived in a paper envelope. The fly sticks are in a thin cardboard box. The fly sticks are very sticky, that is how they catch the flies. When the box crushed, it stuck against the fly stick, ruining it. Both of them had to be returned. I bought the same fly sticks locally and they work great!

The sticky ribbon does not open up wide for the flies to sit because you have to pull on it so hard to get it out of it's container that it all stretches and curls on to itself leaving a long skinny stick hoping the flies will land on it
Does NOT work. The Raid Fly Stick actually attract flies even without bait. This brand did not attract flies even with bait.

Very disappointed. I hung it up and now 3 days later there's only a single gnat and no flies or other flying insects. Flies are landing on the ends and flying all around it and not landing anywhere near the sticky strip. Also the tiny hook to hang them by is so small and cheap plastic it doesn't fit on anything.
Item did not attract insects
Did not work on TX house flies. I had about 15 flies near my pit and not got near the glue stick that was right above it.
These do not work at all. Flys just fly around it. I definitely don’t recommend at all

I hung this right above a trash can that has flies all over it. They never went to the fly paper. They just ignored it. After a week, the fly paper has not caught a single fly. I would have thought at least one would have tried to sit on it and go stuck, but they actively avoid it.

Like other negative reviews, I’ve had it out for about two months, flies flying around it all day long and none of them stick. Smaller bugs get stuck to it but it seems like it’s not strong enough for the flies. I’ve added various things to the bottom cup to try to attract flies and still have caught none.

Waste of $3. I had it in the house after about 10 flies got in from a BBQ party. Didn't xatch one! Lol I spent the whole day chasing and killing flies with a house shoe. So, I just hung it up outside....THREE DAYS and not one bug as touched it. Lol just get a fly swatter.

Based on what was advertised, it did none of that. And I didn’t read reviews where I saw some say nope and I still purchased it anyway. I was desperate. I had so many fruit flies in my house it was crazy. Placed both of them in designated areas and over 12 hours went by and it had only captured maybe 2 or 3. 24 hours maybe 6.
Bought this and the red terro traps and this. Placed them side by side for 2 weeks. Terro traps continually trapped fruit flies. This? Not even one. Complete waste of money.
DOES NOT WORK AT ALL! Did NOT capture 1 fly and there are plenty.

The container has no little holes for the fruit flies, its just open on top when you set it up. They don't list the ingredients but it's basically vinegar and some dish soap. You could make this yourself with an empty spice container. To add insult to injury it ABSOLUTELY DOES NOT WORK. Can fruit flies laugh? Because I'm pretty sure they're laughing as they swarm around our kitchen and completely ignore this "trap". Literally not even ONE fruit fly is stupid enough to fall for it. I however was dumb enough to purchase without checking all the reviews so Fruit Flies 1, stupid human 0.
Didn’t catch a single fruit fly.

I only gave this a one star because I couldn't give it a zero. When I read reviews I try to take all of them and try or not try based on what others say that is important. Please Don't waste your money on this product to me it doesn't smell like other say it does since I use apple cider vinegar at my house on a regular. But it doesn't help with the fruit gnats either. I placed a bottle right beside one of my plants and not one gnats went into the bottle. Even after two weeks when the smell was not strong it didn't have any gnats inside the bottle.

Product has been left out in kitchen counter for over a week, and just 1 little fly or gnat has been trapped in there. Definitely will not purchase again. I love AF products but not this one! Better stuff out there for sure
I was hopeful this would work based on the reviews. However, after two days, it has done nothing. I'm going to give it two more days. I will update my review if anything changes.

I purchased this item due to a recommendation by several websites. I had a MUCH better experience catching fruit flies with a home remedy than this product. I tried it in different areas of the kitchen and only caught one. The home remedy caught the large majority of the flies we were struggling with. The home remedy was apple cider vinegar and a drop of dish soap. I learned through trial-and-error that a shot glass works best instead of a bowl. I assume the steeper sides of the glass helped catch them.
Doesn't work
Blue UV light...how safe is that? Didn't effectively catch many of the adult phorid flies. Caught several baby or small ones. Product didn't do what was expected.
I bought this for a fly problem inside. It caught fruit flies and a mosquito but not a single fly. Not happy with the unit when it said it works for flies.
It fits the zevo light thing, that's the only plus. Mine arrived a week late, and damaged, so I won't be repurchasing.
I only seen one gnat on this thing and I could see other flying around and they would never go to this thing waste of money.
Didn’t attract any gnats for me
The plug is not lighting up after about one week of use.
Bugs really aren't very attracted to this. Will not order again.
Quality made device with cool LED blue light but only caught 1 fruit fly in each trap. They have become more of a night light

reviewTitle: Not doing a real good job for me
reviewDescription: It’s not doing a real good job for me
Traps work pretty well but don't attract all knats and small moths.
DOESN'T work as GOOD as I thought it would!!!
Good for fruit flys and gnats. Won’t work for bigger bugs like flys.
Ive had it pluged in four about four days now and it hasn't worked .
Will trap the occasional fly. Don't expect it to handle an infestation.
In 1 week only caught 1 fly
Did not work
this has been turned on since I received it 3 weeks ago and it may have 10 flies' on each one

reviewTitle: I really am not impressed with this product.
reviewDescription: We had a small fruit fly infestation, but I can't stand anything like that, so I saw this advertised on T.V. and ordered it. There are flies sticking to it but that are still a nuisance, so I really don't know how well it is working.
Gnats didn't seem to be drawn to it as much as I thought
These work great for little fruit flies however regular flies don’t seem to be fooled by this device.
I like the size and the effectiveness of the items, but one has stopped working already.

It’s easy to use and is a fine nightlight, but when it comes to dealing with bugs, it’s far from the best solution. It doesn’t attract that many bugs and doesn’t deal with a fly problem well. What bugs it does catch do stay stuck to the cardboard which is nice, but in general I’d say to try something better. Like a bug zapper.
Did not work!!
It was small and easy to use. It didn’t enough bugs

It did not attract flies but it did get small bugs. It was on constantly but only got one fly when there were a dozen or more. They came in while a party was going on. After the party the house was closed up for two days still only one fly caught.
Did NOT work for fruit flies at all. Totally sucks because I really had high hopes.

reviewTitle: Carnivorous plants doing a better job
reviewDescription: This product worked terribly for me. I have fruit flies all over my kitchen. I have tried everything. I've even bought carnivorous plants at this point, ya'll. This seemed like a much better way to handle it - so I thought. I've had this stuck in the wall turned on for over a week at the most active point in my kitchen. Not a single fly. I SAW ONE WALK ACROSS THE STICKY PART o.O. Not thrilled with this product. The plants are doing better at this point.

I bought this product because we have a gnat infestation in our kitchen. I was hopeful that it would work but after about 20 days and three traps we have captured 4 gnats! That’s about $8/ gnat. Worthless!

reviewTitle: Both units failed quickly after plug in.
reviewDescription: I purchased a two pack. The light on the first one died exactly 7 days after plugging it in. The second one died about 10 days later. I was given a full refund. Once Vevofly works out their QC issues, I might be interested again.

This did not work at all for us. Despite being within six inches of the fruit it caught a total of 2 flies . Left it up, but also put a cup of apple cider vinegar with a perforated top nearby . That worked pretty good and my Veyofly still only had 2. To me this item is useless. They did refund our money so there's that I guess

reviewTitle: May catch 5/100 in weeks. Another ripoff.
reviewDescription: Ah, the FlyTrap—my new favorite kitchen décor! I bought it in a moment of desperation, convinced that it would be my knight in shining armor against the relentless army of flies and maggots invading my space. Little did I know, the commercial was just a well-crafted fairy tale, complete with staged videos of the trap looking like a five-star buffet for flies. Weeks later, and not a single fly has even glanced at my sticky little contraption. It’s like the ultimate fly rejection therapy! I can practically hear them laughing as they buzz past, enjoying their freedom while my FlyTrap collects dust. So, if you're looking for a decorative piece that screams "I tried," then by all means, go ahead and purchase this glorified paperweight. Just don't expect any actual results—unless you count the satisfaction of watching it sit there, utterly useless. Cheers to false advertising!

The Veyofly trap is the worst product I have ever purchased. I bought 2 traps and placed them both in my fruit fly infested kitchen. After 5 days, 5 hole days not a single fruit fly or any flying insect at all has been captured and killed. Zero insects trapped, a total disgrace. Please do not waste your money. I give this product a -10 rating.
Didn’t do much. The light looked promising, but it barely caught anything and felt more like a night-light than a trap. Not impressed and wouldn’t rely on it.

This called every gnat in my county. My gnat problem became x4. I had one in the kitchen and one in the bathroom. A single gnat was stuck to the sticky side and now I have 4x more gnats. Do not buy this product.

I’m having an extreme influx of flies due to a sewage backup and overflow, and I purchased this item to help word off this pandemic but I’m not seeing any impact in these devices… Really disappointed.
The product did not catch flying insects are reported. I am very disappointed in this product.
kills maybe 10% of gnats. pretty blue glow.
This product is not as advertised. In 2 weeks we have only caught a handful of “fruit flies”. It states it catches house flies but the only way I’ve caught them is with a swatter. Very disappointing.
Works just as bad without the light. The light just wastes electricity.

reviewTitle: Not as good as the Zevo brand.
reviewDescription: These are cheaper than zevo screens for a reason. They stick really well to the plastic that comes on them - too well because it rips apart when you take it off (so you have to scrape at the remaining plastic to expose the sticky screen). However, it works less well for bugs. Woke up to a few having been caught, but watched at least 2-3 crawl onto it, just to fly back off after a bit of a fight. Depending on the size of the bug, these do not really trap them unless they get their wings stuck. The Zevo brand REALLY sticks to a human finger - this is just a bit tacky.
Not good. Caught gnats, but no flys. Had to use fly swatter . Moved to two different locations and still did not entice the fly.

reviewTitle: Not as good as name brand.
reviewDescription: Thought it would work like the name brand, but it didn’t. It caught one gnat in a week. Bought the name brand and my gnat problem is gone.
It wasn't what I expected. So far it hasn't caught a bug
Tried it outside didn’t work Then tried it in The garage no luck there either
Didn't zap any bugs.

We had a weird influx of flies that were driving us crazy! I ordered these and hung a couple up. Sadly, after being up over a week they’ve each caught one fly while I’ve killed at least ten. I’m not impressed and would not recommend.

So I bought these to catch a few flies flying around the basement. I left it downstairs for 3 days and it has not caught a single fly. Not sure if this is a user error or if the product simply doesn't work.

reviewTitle: Top to the Fly Stick Sucks
reviewDescription: Why is the top of the fly stick able to come off the entire stick? Works well for reducing my bad gnat population, but with the wind sometimes it falls on the ground because the top of it isn't attached securely to the stick. Design flaw?

It is an effective product; however, they are tricky, not to say frustrating to set up. The small strip on the back is too narrow. When both cover papers are removed and the you try to place it, it sticks to your fingers, making the process near impossible; and, when you remove the back piece, leaving the front police in place, it is easy to place, but then when you remove the front cover, quality control fails you, as the cover doesn't come off easily due to patches of "overstickiness" between the cover and the main glue surface, which rips the whole contraption off the surface you tried to put it on in the first place, bringing you back again to the frustrating task of placing it with both sticky sides exposed, usually at this time with dirt, dust, and/or lint on it from dropping it.
variant: Size: 2 Pack, Style: Fly Window Strip

reviewTitle: Not easy to use. Fly tape…. It’s supposed to be easy…
reviewDescription: We have had flies bad this year! We have never had flies before so I’m not sure how to get rid of them. Fly tape? I bought some at my local store (different brand) and it worked like a charm! So I ordered more and these did not do well. When I tried to take them out of the roll to hang them they stayed curly and then stuck to themselves. Even after 2+ weeks they hung super curly. They didn’t catch many flies as the other brand I bought. I didn’t think their would be any difference, but surly their was.

We hung this up following the instructions, including placing some syrup in the cup at the bottom. It failed to catch any flies. The flies would just land in the cup, eat the syrup, and fly away without ever touching the adhesive. I cheaped out instead of getting the Gold Stick brand, which has always worked in the past. Penny wise, pound foolish I guess.

This stuff is extremely sticky and has a tendency to stick to your hands, the wall and anything else that gets remotely close to it…however…in the week I’ve had them up it hasn’t caught a single fly. I have tried moving it to all the high traffic areas in the home and still nothing. Maybe it’s just the flies in Northern California but this product doesn’t work for me. +1 star for the stickiness.

Black Flag has a good reputation, so I purchased this product. This time of year, we in Arizona get lots of flies. Suffice it to say that they get into the house and become annoying buzzing around and landing on yours truly and become a pest. Enough's enough. I looked for a product that would remove these pesky critters. Naught. I followed the set up procedures on the box and with flies flying around, none, I repeat none, came in contact with the honey, let alone the sticky glue on the trap. The engineers who designed this product should go back to school. This may work in a test laboratory, but does not work in our home or perhaps, your home either. Trust, tried and true Black Flag, but verify first. This product doesn't cut it.

reviewTitle: Falls off days after I hang it
reviewDescription: Great sticky stuff however the top is not glued to the tube and out of 9 I’ve not been able to get one to stay up longer than a week. The top needs to be stabilized better
but I had mine up 2 days now 2 of them and hasn't cought 1 fly there in my house and the flies that get in aren't interested with this to bad had high hopes I used syrup maybe honey is better?

I'm opposed to killing anything. Be it a fly or a flea. These work as well as fly paper has since it's invention, so you will be happy with the results.. just as much trouble with it failing as generic strips. The plastic piece coming off to the paper ripping.

reviewTitle: They work but aren't easy to install.
reviewDescription: So far I have caught flies, fruit flies and even a wasp with this sticky trap. In order to stick it to the window you have to peel off the thin strip of paper to expose a line of adhesive that sticks to the window. This paper is easily removed. However, the paper that covers the glue is extremely difficult to remove. I don't know why they can't make these so that the paper removes easier from the glue side. I have wondered about how to go about removing one of these once it is filled with bugs. They should have put a little tab in the corner that isn't covered in glue so that you have something to grab to remove from the window. Cut them up into halves or thirds. You do not need to use them full size to catch bugs. They work well enough but could use some improvement in the design.
variant: Size: 1 pack, Style: Fly Window Strip

reviewDescription: I had a phorid fly infestation and these worked as well as traditional fly tape for phorid flies. They were more pleasing because the yellow sticky stuff really allows you to see the flies giving the impression it caught way more, but really looking its the same amount per inch. That being said, I would just get a good fly tape instead. A box of 10 fly tape costs the same as one of these and you are definitely going to catch more with 10 fly tapes. Note, this is only with Phorid flies, house flies or other flies may be more attracted to this. I also used honey as bait per the directions.

cost was good, and it attracted some of the flies. The sticky substance is a mess, and not useful in extremely warm climates. It is over 100 degrees here in summer, and its too messy, especially near humans.

The flys will get stuck to this but it doesn’t necessarily attract the flies. My kitchen had many flies and this only caught about a third of them. For whatever reason many flies didn’t land on it even though it was in a central location. Its better than having nothing but doesn’t fully solve a fly infestation.
For the price you can find much better. The sticky part is not big enough it's as simple as that. You will have to buy many of these fly sticks. I did not return it but I surely won't order again.

I deployed two of these traps in our window where we have flies, wasps, and other flying insects gather and we're still waiting to see a single fly in either of these traps. So, it's hard to say these traps are meeting my expectations. I'll be patient, but I would have thought within several days we'd see at least one or two flies captured.
variant: Size: 1 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewTitle: Ugly and sticky - you have to slowly unwind the paper from the roll
reviewDescription: We hung a couple of these fly paper traps and didn't catch any flies but the traps sure caught me (stuck to my shirt when I brushed up against it). Very sticky on the hands too. I think the flies decided to leave by the time we got these in place. I may put them back up later if the flies return. I found them very difficult to keep from getting sticky stuff on your hands when unwinding the paper from the roll. The piece of material for hanging the strips is too small making it hard to stay away from the sticky paper when hanging them.

reviewTitle: Didn't work at good as I thought it would
reviewDescription: Either I have flies with high IQ's, or this fly paper doesn't work so well. I had many flies in my small kitchen not only three were caught on the paper hanging in middle of the room. I had the same issue in a small bathroom where the glue and nowhere else to go, but managed to avoid the fly paper for days. The stuff is very very sticky as you will experience when you untwirl it. But the most of my flies ignored it.

reviewTitle: I have had a fly for a month.. doesn't seem to attract it
reviewDescription: I used sugar solution, protein in bottom... nothing attracts fly. I think this fly is a super fly. I can't believe it doesnt' just die. Can't get with fly swatter. Keep no food or garbage around and he keep living. People say get the thing with the light to attract the fly. Fly not worth 35 bucks to kill.

reviewTitle: Messy. Be careful where you locate it.
reviewDescription: The solution Black Flag uses to catch the insect with this product is a yellowish, slimey goo. IMO this would be great hung out in a barn. However, located anywhere that a person might accidentally brush against it will ruin clothing. Raid makes a similar product using a clear adhesive similar to plastic tape. A much better alternative for inside the house.
10/10 on stickiness, but didn't attract any flies. Stand is not sturdy and can fall easily.
The box was broken and open when I opened up the bag. Hope this does impact the integrity of the trap!

reviewTitle: Decent and helps but is not...
reviewDescription: the solution if you need to get rid of many flies, as many are smart these days and avoid landing on these. You will catch the dumb ones and the rest you have to kill with a zapper. My new neighbor raises dogs, chickens and has his trash bin outside hidden behind a fake half wall and the result is a swarm of flies. Made in China.

These are very sticky - so should catch a lot of flies. But the product is hard to get out of packaging without sticking to your hands. Once I hung it, it didn’t lose its spiral shape, so all the adhesion isn’t exposed to flying pests. See pic - This is how it looks after hanging for 1 month. Still in spiral shape. As a result it hasn’t “caught” many flies. Product or packaging needs redesigned….

reviewTitle: Good glue but unable to attract flies.
reviewDescription: I tried this product using maple syrup, honey and even small pieces of meat in the bottom cup but none worked to attract flies. The only way this was capturing flies was when I placed it by the window where they gather. In other words it seems as though you have to go after the flies and they’ll get caught, but it doesn’t attract them at all. This doesn’t work for me and won’t be buying it again.
works somewhat but not great

These did not work at all for me. I don’t think I caught a single fly. I hung them up in an entrance mud room and we get quite a few flies that sneak in there but none were attracted to the strip. It was an eye sore and I got something else stuck to it by accident and decided I was not using those anymore.
This product works good for flying insects. But it didn’t attract the swarm of flies.
I put it outside in the problem areas & it caught more gnats than flys.

I wouldn’t recommend this product to anyone. I put it outside by our trash cans. I have had it in up for about 5 days now. It has not attracted one single fly. I have caught a million mustard bugs and a couple of moths, but not one single fly. In fact, the flies continue to buzz around me, annoying me, but they have not been attracted to the strip at all.
The Flys can see this and avoid it. No Flys were caught. Just a couple gnats.

reviewTitle: everything sticks to this except the flies
reviewDescription: I hung it from a pendant light near the patio door and couldn't catch one fly for 5 days! we got new neighbors who have horses and with horses come flies. and it's too windy outside to hang these outside. last year was terrible for flies. I have more luck with a fly swatter but it scares the crap out of my dogs.

reviewTitle: Doesn't seem to attract the flys
reviewDescription: The flys just seem to be multiplying the trap isnt decreasing the number.
This was not what I was hoping for. Tough to find the right spot to place.
Did not catch a single fly. Not buying again. Would not recommend.

I hung this up near the end of last summer when the flies were relentless. I hung it about 5 feet from my sales counter. It caught zero flies. I then sprinkled some sugar on the adhesive hoping to attract more flies and ended up catching one. The rest of the time I used a fly swatter which was FAR more effective. Won't buy again.
It did not catch any fly and it’s been a month

reviewTitle: You get what you pay for
reviewDescription: There were 4 strips in the package. One of them was so sticky that it unwound into a tight spiral instead of a strip of fly paper and one of them snapped apart while unwinding it (and the glue definitely does not come off the hand using standard soap). I just put them up, so I can not yet determine if this will catch the bugs that came into the house.
Flies are not attracted to it and I have flies flying around and not a single one was caught by two of these strips. Don't waste your time and money on these.

These sticks are a good idea and I assume they would work if a fly was unlucky and just HAPPENED to land on it but there's nothing to attract them to it at all. It has no odor or anything so it's basically a crap shoot. I've had it up for over a month and it's only managed to snag a few gnats, which is better than nothing at least. -they did arrive quickly.
After a few weeks, got one fly and a lot of gnats. A for effort.
Not a fan. Wouldn't recommend. Very messy and didn't do much for house flys

Not bad for small flies, or if the fly just happens to get stuck on its back. But I watched a large fly land on the stick and then simply struggle a bit and fly away. Need bigger guns for bigger flies.

I found it did not stick to the windows well and needed reinforcement. I also put in on a window that had a strip of flat flypaper that. I was hoping this worked as well, but it does not. For every bug in the Black Flag window trap, there were 3 or 4 on the plain fly paper. Te Black Flag has the advantage of having the bugs hid away, but the regular transparent paper flat against the window seemed to attract more bugs (either gnats or fruit flies in my case)
variant: Size: 1 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewDescription: This caught a total of eight flies in 3 days
Didn't like .only thing in trap is few ants .

I have a feral cat colony and their food attracts swarms of flies un the summer. They eat under an open sided shed so I hung these from the rafters over the food Bowls and was lucky to see one or two flies stuck to it after weeks of hanging.

Okay, so problem number one was that I bought these based on a TikTok video. The adhesive is actually crap. They do not work and I literally seen a fly go in and fly out. I am so disappointment but only in myself for believing TikTok 😂 save your money.
variant: Size: 1 Pack, Style: Fly Window Trap

reviewDescription: This stuff seems to be good, easy to use, but flies conjugate all around it and don't land on it. Those very few that do definitely stick but they don't seem to be attracted to it. I've used flypaper before and I remember what I had before worked great, the strip was full of flies in no time but I don't remember what brand that was.

This product catches bugs real good. The product's positioning in "Patio, Lawn & Garden" suggests that it can be used outdoors. Yes, but it's light as a feather, so a strong wind will blow it around like crazy or even blow it away. And although it is advertised to last up to three months, if you put it outside near a light, its glue-paper surface can be completely covered with bugs in a single night. I suggest putting something in its cup-shaped base to weigh it down in the wind. Metal scrap such as discarded nails will provide good "ballast". Jam or honey will not be as heavy but will help attract flies. It hangs from a small hook that pulls out of its top, so you will need something to hang it from. It is well designed. Its protective top and bottom make it less likely than a fly strip to stick to somebody's hair, to the house, etc. See my "customer image" photo. I've rated it at only three stars because of the price. It's made (in China) out of cheap, lightweight materials. I bought it to lower the insect population on my front and back porches. It does that so well that it lasts only a night or two, then it's time to hang another one. Wow, what an expensive way to solve the problem. Any of the other Black Flag traps would be a better value, in my opinion. For indoor use it still seems a bit expensive, but I would rate it at four stars. 7/10/12 update: Good, the price here is starting to drop. When it gets down to the price in local stores (where it sells out quickly), I'll consider ordering it here.
Almost everyone of them broke while pulling out

During the summer, the abundance of flies outside, especially on the patio, drives us crazy. We’ve recently got 2 dogs, so that makes it even worse. In the week they’ve been outside, with honey added, per directions, I don’t think we’ve had even 1 fly stick. On the patio we’ve got several small gnats that have stuck and a few moths. I also hung one in the bushes, where my dogs do their “business”. I thought that one would catch lots of flies, but nope. In fact as I just did the video, there were flies all around! And for the record, I pick up dog poop every other day. I’m pretty disappointed with my purchase. I’ll probably end up getting an old fashioned fly swatter.

reviewTitle: Catches many flying bugs, but no house flies after 2 weeks
reviewDescription: This stuff is ugly, but if it catches flies and keeps them from getting inside then it is worth it. This would be very easy to install if the included thumb tack was more solid. I tried to place one inside my cellar entrance by pushing the thumbtack into a 2 x 8 beam. It kept bending under the pressure, so I tried another with a light hammer but it too was unsuccessful. I assumed the wood might be too tough so I proceeded to attempt to mount another one by my garbage pails. This is an area where I have observed many house flies, and a problem area as there is a door nearby that they use to sneak in. The thumbtack also bent while trying to push into a softer wooden railing. I gave up and used a finishing nail. After 1 week I did not catch any insects in my cellar, but I did catch a lot of small flying insects by the garbage pails. I have not seen any house flies stuck to the tape, but I have not seen any around the garbage pails either. I don't know where they have gone. After 2 weeks the wind blew the tape off the finishing nail, so I will have to supply and use a more sturdy tack to fashion a more effective mount.

So this product does work and catches some flies, but you will notice, as I have, that some flies approach the trap and get away from it. Research from the University of Florida (you can search it on youtube) has shown that flies are actually REPELLED by the color yellow. For the life of me, I don't know why these fly trap manufacturers keep using YELLOW instead of BLUE which has been found to attract flies the most (blue with black stripes actually work the best). What I did instead was buy blue sticky sheets off amazon, wrapped it around this fly trap (any freestanding tube will work but I had these traps on hand), and poured a little apple cider vinegar at the bottom cone to attract the flies. This has worked WONDERS and eliminated my fly problem. USE SCIENCE AND USE BLUE INSTEAD OF YELLOW!!

I've had fly strips like these in the past and have had mixed results. These seem a tiny bit worse than the others, but not by much. I took stars off for a couple reasons. First, when you pull the ribbon out of the cannister, the cannister is already covered in goop. I have yet to find a way not to get it on my hands. There just isn't a reason for that. Spend an extra nickel on each container and make the seal work. Second, the idea that flies are in any way drawn to this is silly. If you don't have lots of flies that are going to either land on this by accident or accidentally bump into it, it isn't going to do you a lot of good. We only ever have a couple flies at a time, but it's all summer because of where we live. I hung these and only caught one fly. The others just never quite felt the need to land on it. If you were somewhere that lots of flies were banging about they would work great, but the odds just aren't on your side with a couple of flies.

I was all of a sudden inundated with hundreds of flies for some reason so after swatting about 50, I ordered these sticky traps. I added some honey, as well as coffee, in the bottom as others suggested, but neither one worked. Flies would land on top of the plastic. So I drizzed some honey down the sides of the sticky trap and caught three flies in a day. Others seemed to have disappeared as soon as I set them out. Did not make sense.

reviewTitle: My fly stick does not attract flies
reviewDescription: My fly stick has been up with many flies flying around; but, they are not attracted to the stick. So disappointed. I don't know if the sticks get old and, eventually, become less effective or just what the problem is. I have used the Raid fly sticks in the past and they have worked really well. So this surprises me. Unfortunately, I was unable to give the rating of 5 or recommend them, since the sticks I received are not working.

reviewTitle: Bad for flies, great for gnats
reviewDescription: I've used one now for almost 2 weeks. They work good if you have a few gnats flying around. However, the random house fly is much too clever for these glue contraptions. If you put it outside, it works really well with both flies and gnats. Great for the back deck. Bad for 3-4 house flies inside.

This is a lot smaller than I thought it would be, which is actually a good thing. A smaller footprint means it doesn't look awful hanging in my kitchen. It is, however, not doing what I bought it for. I got a bunch of house flies come in from outside and I have been loosing my mind trying to get rid of them. I have had this hanging for 3 days and not a single house fly has landed on it or been affected by it. It got a bunch of house flies I didn't know I had, thank you bananas, but the house flies just laugh in my face instead.

reviewTitle: Not a solution for gnats in your apartment, maybe for plants
reviewDescription: this is supposed to be put into a pot of plants but it does not have any odor and does not specifically attract the gnats. if you do not have gnats in plants, do not buy this product, if your gnats are in potted plants then maybe this will be a good product for you. the description on this item should say for potted plants only.

I really like these gnat catchers. I bought a plant online that arrived infested. This didn’t cure the problem but it definitely helped. However, the price has gone up and up between my orders. I thought I ordered two packs but only received one. I came back to order the second one and the price was a dollar more. I have a problem writing good reviews on Amazon because the higher the ratings the higher the cost rises. So yes, these are cute and catch bugs. But no, I won’t order any more. I will buy the plain sticker sheets at the nursery.

The shape of the BOTTOM part of the sticky part has downward angles that rip the cover in the removal process. A better design could improve the release of the protective cover. The sticky part does its job VERY WELL with only the bright yellow color as an attractant (no odor) it works on the gnats presenting the problem.

reviewTitle: Work well on fungus gnats but don’t let your plants touch them!
reviewDescription: These work well to catch the fungus gnats in my house plants. I always have tons accumulate on it but I really struggle to maneuver these around my plants pots. You do not want your plant to touch these or get caught on the sticky parts! I had a leaf accidentally get caught and it died. So it’s really hard to find a place to sticky these where the plant won’t touch them if you have a plant that’s really full in it’s pot. I tend to like the sticky traps that are on a little stake better.

Unfortunately, it seems like it helped the production of bigger & more nats; than, what I had to begin with!/? Too add, I have not seen... Not one nat attached to any of the sticky butterfly traps? ... In any event, maybe I have a bad batch? Unsure. Nonetheless, I’m not sold by this product at this time. Perhaps, you try again freely? Let’s run another test... Or simply refund, much appreciative. Tks. Nevertheless, “The ball is in your court, are you going to dribble or shoot?”🤔 Again Tks., be safe & blessed, ttyl.

I bought these sticky traps for my houseplants after I noticed gnats hanging out around the area. The sticky traps themselves are exactly as advertised and the adhesive backing was easy to remove. The traps have already caught several gnats after only being out for less than 24 hours, so they definitely work! My only issue was that I ordered a 12 pack, but I only received 11 in the box. Just something to keep in mind if you are needing a certain amount.

The sticky factor is definitely a 12 out of 10, but be careful not to touch a plant leaf with it or that leaf is a goner. It will even stick pretty well to a person's fingers, so if you don't want to wear it out to dinner, take care to handle it around the edges. That being said, it sure will do the job of catching gnats. But I found the bright yellow color to be aestheticly unappealing.

Didn’t see a whole lot of anything gets stuck to the sticker but that could also be because I don’t have a lot of aphids inside of the house maybe I don’t know. The reason I bought this was because somebody at work was using them and they look to be working really well. for myself at home I haven’t had much success with bugs sticking to the sticker.

I bought these instead of the usual ones that have the triangle plastic that sticks in the ground, and I regret it. These things will not stay stuck to the bamboo sticks at all. I come back a couple hours later and they've fallen off and stuck to my plants. The paper is also difficult to get off. They work okay if you plan on just hanging them I guess.
As stated in reviews they are difficult to peel and so far I can still see gnats, they don't seen to post any attention to the sticky traps... we'll see.

reviewTitle: Great product, but with some cons
reviewDescription: This is a great product, but they are big, bulky, and constantly fall out of the pot. when handling your plants, I sometimes find the sticky stuck to my jeans, blouse, etc? Taking them and cutting them in half is a little bit more effective, but still a struggle. They get the job done though, so for that it is worth the headache.
I have bought these before this particular package, the paper tears making it extremely difficult and it should be easy

It gets a few fruit flies, but not nearly close to what I had expected.
I would recommend. They work as expected for indoor and outdoor plants. Wish they came in a variety of sizes tho

reviewTitle: Flimsy and difficult to peel the label off
reviewDescription: They’re very flimsy, so if you don’t have fairly aerated soil in your pot - time to aerate as they were difficult to slide into the potting soil. Also, the paper was difficult to get off due to the butterfly shape. I hadn’t thought of that. So ended up with sticky fingers. Not a big deal - just be prepared.

I’ve had it up for a couple weeks but it doesn’t seem to catch anything, HOWEVER, I don’t see fruit flies anymore and they say it’s odorless. It doesn’t bother me as long as they’re gone but there’s nothing on the traps!
The product delivered. The little gnats that were in my plants stuck immediately to traps. I was surprised at how well it worked.
Wish they would add a scent to attract the insect that would make this product 100x better,

They worked and catch insects, but the adhesive doesn’t stick well to the stand and half of the ones I put up, ended up falling off. If they made a better stand for these I’d give it a better review. If you use this inside without having wind, I’m sure it works great, and if you hang them from something outside, I’m sure they also work well.

reviewTitle: They Laugh As They Fly By
reviewDescription: Update: Ok. I put a little wine on these to attract the fruit flies and put it by the garbage can. It actually trapped a few of them. I think I’ll still find something else, but these are somewhat useful so I’ll give it 3 stars. I hate fruit flies. Their single purpose in life is to be on your fruit and aggravate people. So, I bought these. The fruit flies fly right around this thing and laugh as they fly by 😞

reviewTitle: They need to be a bit stickier
reviewDescription: I like them, they are cute in the pots but they need to be just a little bit gooier to get all the bugs that fly onto them. They have caught a few bugs but I watched a fruit fly hit one and then pull itself free. Otherwise they look much better than fly tape and I’m happier to have them out

reviewTitle: Got plants in soil? You’re gonna have fungus gnats!
reviewDescription: No way around it. I’ve tried everything & still have fungus gnats! I guess that’s just a drawback to being a houseplant enthusiast.

Not bad at all for the price... very very very sticky so use caution getting the paper off of them. Took care of my indoor plant gnat problem pretty fast. Also the designs on these make it not so bad to look at.

Its sticky—-me, dirt, other plants greening, but then it’s so sticky that will not take the paper off. My fingers with sticky and hard to get it off. Push it in the dirt and it goes with my fingers. Aggravating...
Works as described

Easy to peel and put into the pots. It doesn’t attract or catch as many gnats as I had hoped. It honestly sticks to my cats more than it sticks to the bugs but it’s caught some! Was just hoping for more

These work super well but do not use them outdoors, a big got its wing stuck to it and we had to wash him with dish soap to get it off his wing

reviewTitle: Does what it's supposed to do

Packaging left a lot to be desired. It came in a clear plastic bag. I had trouble removing the covering on some of the sticky traps. This was okay since I didn't want one of the sticky sides touching the plant leaves. Other than that, it works. Fungus gnats are under control.
You will need to create a trap in order for these to work. Even after leaving these out for a week along with vinegar trap these caught maybe 4-5 gnats.

reviewTitle: Does not seem to attract gnats/fruit flies.
reviewDescription: The product is cute and sticky but unless you have a large infestation it does not seem to work that well. An occasional gnat can be caught if you wave the product around and catch it that way.
Not better than the cheap fly strips
Did not work well!
También atrapa lagartijos y otros animalitos, lo devolví.

reviewTitle: paper is very difficult to remove. Other tacky strips have been more convenient to use.
reviewDescription: The paper tears easily and often doesn't come off in one piece. They are very sticky but hard not to get on your fingers. They are flimsy so difficult to properly use.

reviewTitle: Works in soil, not so much without plants
reviewDescription: I have fruit fly issue in the kitchen and put out vinegar trap, but it wasn’t working as fast as I would like. Bought this in hopes that I can just hang it around by itself to attract fruit flies. It caught ZERO (0) fruit flies! Meanwhile, my vinegar trap still slowly gets an few fruit flies everyday. I put 1 of this sticky thing into the soil of my plant and seems to attract flying bugs just fine! Not good for just hanging around by itself. It be best to be used with soil. I will upload pictures later.

These sticky traps are very very very sticky, to the point if they stick to something it's very difficult to get them off. If your plant leaves stick to this you can kiss them goodbye. When you get your fingers stuck to it, it gets the adhesive transferred to your fingers and is difficult to wash off. I think the set up and accessories are great, if they could tone down the amount of stick it would be rated much higher.

These will not get rid of gnats. A few have stuck but they lay hundred of eggs in the dirt and most fly past the yellow stickers. I have it 3 stars bc a few gnats did stick. If you really need to get rid of gnats do several things: this solution (1 cup), apple cider vinegar (1/2 cup), sugar (tbsp), and dish soap (7 drops). Put this in small dishes (I used bright colored solo cups, cutting the bottoms off into a small shallow dish) and put these around all of your plants. 2) Let the soil dry out as much as you can 3) get rocks to put over soil in pots * Also if they are flying in your face- peppermint oil, coconut oil, or olive oil dabbed on your face will keep them away.

Overall it did help my situation with fungus gnats. didn't completely eradicate them but I did notice a difference. Also came with a mini shovel, which came in handy for top covering small seedlings though lol.
Traps are sticky enough to trap bugs. Unfortunately this item does not contain a scent so you have to bait it over a jar of rotting garlic to trap the fruit flies.

The product works really well but it is impossible to peel away the outer paper. It would be better if this was a regular shape instead of these cute shapes. A regular shape would make it easier to pull the paper off. This is a REALLY sticky product so as soon as the outer layer is removed on one side, your hands will stick to it. Then you have to remove the other side. Way easier with a regular shape.

reviewTitle: They work...if the bug runs into them
reviewDescription: I have a fruit fly problem and these seemed to be a cheap solution to it. The description implies that bugs will be attracted to the stickies, but they aren't. It very much seems that they only trap a bug if the bug lands on them. Luckily, they aren't obviously but traps so I don't mind having them out, they just don't work as well as I'd like.

I have had issues with gnats in my plants for awhile. I got these to try to capture them before they infested the rest of my house. I still see gnats around but don't see any on these yet. They are quite sticky, even being careful I still got it stuck to my fingers so I know they should be working. Have to wait and see I guess.

I got these because I liked the shapes. These things are SUPER sticky, which in theory should be great. However, they’re so sticky that it’s impossible to get the paper off without your fingers ending up caked in the sticky stuff. Plus paper doesn’t come off in one sweep; you have to go at it in parts because it rips. They do their job otherwise.

reviewTitle: To pricey for such a small amount
reviewDescription: I have alot of plants and alot of fungus gnats, at one point i was changing these out every other day and using 3 total, so i went through this in a week, 12 is just not enough especially when you can find other sellers who also have free shipping but offer more product for the same price.

reviewTitle: Works but SO HARD to set up
reviewDescription: These worked well to catch little flies, but they are IMPOSSIBLE to set up. I’m not sure if I just got a bad batch or what, but the white protective layer rips when I try to remove it and it’s so hard to get it off so the adhesive is showing. Takes like 20 minutes. If the white cover came off easier, I’d change my review.

CAUTION: I was so excited to use these sticky traps until I found a little lizard stuck on it. I live in South florida, so these critters are everywhere. I was able to set the lizard free by applying avacado oil over on the trap and gently removing it with my nail. Not sure if I want to use these traps 🤔

these work pretty ok........but not the best option from my experimentation.........They did catch quite a few of the flies but it took days to do so ......would probably work better if they had some kind of attractant or bait scent added to them. I switched these for the apple shaped bait traps which caught most of the flies in 24 hours
Need to have more support I either attach it to a straw or a popsicle stick to put in the soil easier.

reviewTitle: Not worth the hassle with my cats
reviewDescription: I have bad kitties. I would find these stuck to them regularly. I should have anticipated this would happen because they harass my plants all of the time. It was a hassle getting these off of them. They are essentially fly paper on posts. You can imagine the fun I had.

Not the worst fly traps, but not nearly as effective as fly strips. I guess they're more discreet than fly strips, but not by much since it's a bright yellow butterfly. Also slightly frustrating to install because the traps have static cling which results in soil getting stuck to the trap.

reviewTitle: Disappointed that they were stuck together because the paper was siding off!
reviewDescription: Didn’t like the fact that they were stuck together. I use these to keep any flying insects or fungus gnats off my plants!
I dislike this product didn’t catch many gnats but what didn’t work for me may work for other ppl.

To actually use these... is difficult. The glue is too good. So good you can't even get the paper off covering the adhesive. They're so flimsy, which makes it hard to get a grip on them when pulling the adhesive off. And the whole piece is glue. Back and front. The stake part doesn't need glue.
yes they are simple but i guess the bugs have gotten wise. no bugs were stuck to the seven sticky cards mounted in my plants.
I fully understand these need to be sticky but they leave a sticky residue on your fingers. Better options out there.
Keep in mind there’s no bait you just have the hope they fly into it and get stuck.
These are very effective in limiting insects. The big issue is getting the protective paper off the sticky paper.
So far it is working to catch those dang gnats in my house plants just a FYI try to keep it out of the way of the cat lol 😹
Really hard to use. The protective sheets don't come off easily.

reviewTitle: Make sure you have the right flies
reviewDescription: These things are cute but haven’t attracted one of my flies. I think I may have drain flies and these do not work on them.

reviewTitle: Yes' but not as good as it should!
reviewDescription: It works slow and the gnats sorta know what's going on. I've seen a few on the sticky pad, but not enough yet! Still watching...
Had lots of fruit flies in my home. These didn't work for me. Bought a bug zapper lamp. That did the trick

reviewTitle: Fine but just remember no bug is going to actively walk onto it
reviewDescription: Unfortunately I thought this would attract the bugs we had but it didn’t.
This product is not as effective for fruit flies as fly paper. The color does not appear to be an attractant.
They are great but they need a stronger bottom to hold. It's hard to keep them standing, they get full of debris.
These are okay, but I’ve seen multiple gnats escape after only having these sit out for 12 hours. Ymmv.

reviewTitle: Does not work for fruit flies
reviewDescription: Its great for fungus gnats but fruits flies have zero interest. Will not work for them.
The adhesion is definitely there. I wish there was a better way to hide it or maneuver it because it sticks to everything.

reviewTitle: Sticky but the shapes make it difficult
reviewDescription: Very sticky but the paper on each side is very hard to remove. Tried to get it carefully but ripped a lot. Especially the butterfly shape.
It did caught a few of this pest, but not enough to solve the problem.
The paper sticks to the trap too well

reviewTitle: The flower shaped ones DO NOT WORK, ONLY THE BUTTERFLIES
reviewDescription: Only the butterfly’s work, save your money on the multi pack
I put several of these up where there were bugs and only one bug got caught, the rest ignore it.

I purchased these since my apartment has had a lot of gnats. As much as the reviews said these work, unfortunately they do not. However to be fair I did not use these in plants I propped them up in different locations of my apartment. I have had them up for about a week and half now and I have caught 0 gnats. Maybe these work better in plants but no luck for me using these around the house.

When they state sticky, believe me they are sticky. Very difficult to get the protective paper off the sticky part and very hard to insert into your plant without have the leaves of the plant stick to it. By time I got done I had sticky glue all over my fingers which only using acetone nail polish remover was I as to get the sticky off. Not to mention that took my nail polish off with it. I have had these up for one month and not caught one fruit fly and still have them in my kitchen. I am disappointed and will not be using the test.

reviewTitle: FRUIT FLIES TOTALLY IGNORE THIS PRODUCT - IT IS WORTHLESS
reviewDescription: I HAVE BEEN TRYING TO FIND A PRODUCT THAT WOULD RELIABLY KILL THE FRUIT FLIES THAT COME INTO MY HOUSE WITH PRODUCE. THEY MOSTLY IGNORE MY UV ZAPPER. SO I DECIDED TO TRY THIS PRODUCT RECOMMENDED BY AMAZON. IT HAS NO SCENT ATTRACTANT. SUPPOSEDLY THE FLIES ARE SUPPOSED TO BE ATTRACTED BY THE "BRIGHT YELLOW COLOR" I HAVE PUT 8 OF THEM ALL OVER THE HOUSE, TO NO AVAIL. THEY TOTALLY IGNORE THEM!!!

warning: do not buy this crap because they do not attract and trap any bugs!! They also are in a bizarre shape that cannot be easily placed to attract bugs anyway. I left one outside overnight just to test it trapping mosquitoes and gnats....it did not attract a single bug!!!!!

how did this junk get these high reviews? they are not worth anything. a stupid yellow is going to attract bugs/flies. i had them in a dry sink, the ants must come up from the drain, i dont see them anywhere else. not one ant wasn't attracted, not one ant stuck to this lousy c**p. i am trying for a refund. DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. i just cant believe the high reviews for this

I had an invasion of fruit flies so purchased this item. The packaging is lovely. The box is more attractive than most stationery but the product attracted only 2 flies over a period of two weeks. I cannot be bothered returning this item but is absolutely DOES NOT ATTRACT FRUIT FLIES. Look closely at other options....there is a much better option out there.

The trap are completely useless. We've located 5 sticky traps in plants and in the kitchen. Dozens of flies fly above them and non of them gets caught in the trap. It's been like that for more than a week, and all traps are still clean and empty while we suffer from the flies

reviewTitle: Hated these traps, because it took weeks to catch any decent amount of them
reviewDescription: These traps are not a very good solution for controlling fruit flies, They caught about 3 fruit flies in 3 days. It took weeks before any amount were caught. Eventually about 100 to 200 were in the traps. I still have quite a few flies in the kitchen though that ignore them. I guess if you don't mind waiting forever to catch the flies, you'll like these traps. However, I was extremely disappointed and Amazon gave me a refund.

reviewTitle: These did not work at all for me
reviewDescription: We have an infestation of fruit flies. I put many of these out. We attracted/caught 3 flies. Don’t waste your money. I would rate ZERO stars.

Save your money, these do not work at all! I several of these throughout my home. Not ONE FRUIT FLY on any of them. Made my own trap, A little red wine or fruit in the bottom of a glass, covered with plastic wrap and a paper funnel in the center so they fly in but not out. Dozens in the glass, none on these traps.

This product came in a zip lock baggie with no instructions. There was a plastic shovel included. I have no idea what I'm supposed to do with it. The only photo available online is one with the traps in a planter. These are supposed to be for indoor and outdoor use, but no indoor instructions, so I had to use my imagination. That would even be okay if they worked. I've had these up for a week, but the little buggers are still flying around and very much alive. They don't seem to be attracted. Except for a few unlucky ones who must have hit the one in the photo accidentally, these traps are not working.

Used as directed. Placed in kitchen and in a closed off room which contained house plants. After several weeks of use it did not catch 1 gnat in either area. They continue flying all over, never attracted to this item.

I had a serious problem with funges.gnats so o purchased thsse after reading all the positive reviews. They did not work at all! I didn't catch one single gnat! I placed them in and around my house plants. I also put several of them in my kitchen and also on my garbage cans in my kitchen and bathroom. I was very disappointed.

I had high hopes for these. As we have had tried multiple items to get rid of the ruthless fruit flies. There's always the 'tape' which looks gross and tacky so we decided to give these a try. they have a nice look to them. As soon as they arrived I put one out. It took a week to catch 2 houseflies and 1 fruit fly. When we have a ton of them. Either from the sink, or my compost pail. So, unfortunately I must return them because we still have the fruit fly problem and they simply aren't attracted to these.

I will be making my own effective fruit fly traps after using these MESSY stickums that don't work. Be prepared to remove the glue from your hands after trying to take off the coverup paper. WHATTA MESS it is. Hours later, I'm still removing glue from my fingernails.

These do not work whatsoever. I have a plant that is infested with fungus gnats and haven’t caught a single one with these. I used another brand of these when I had an issue 2 years ago and the sticks were coated in the gnats the next day. This time with this brand and it’s been 3 days and not one single gnat. They aren’t sticky enough I think.
Doesn’t work! Don’t waste your money!

It’s just a yellow sticker with no smell or anything, I have put 10 of them all over my house. I have fruit flies and none of them even give a damn about this yellow sticker 0 Result waste of my money

Purchased with high hope and followed instructions BUT never worked!!! I put 6 traps at different places at home including kitchen and other rooms but it’s just a waste of money and time!! DOES NOT WORK and DO NOT RECOMMEND!!
Useless!

I ordered two different fruit fly traps. For these made of yellow, sticky paper, I paid around $5. I also ordered an electronic Bugbow fruit fly eliminator with UV light , fan and sticky paper for around $30. After the first day, the electronic Bogbow caught about 12 fruit flies. This yellow sticky paper did not have a single fruit fly. I have since moved the traps around, put a shallow dish of red wine near the traps to attract the flies. The electronic trap continues to catch flies, and the yellow sticky paper does not. I was really hoping this yellow sticky paper would work as the other electronic device is quite pricey. But, this yellow sticky paper does not work and the electronic device does.

It's very difficult to keep them stuck to anything I don't care what the service is. I ended up using Scotch tape across the center of it just to keep it on the wall. But the nets never went to it. I put up probably 30 of them. I don't recommend this product.

reviewTitle: it worked for my nephew, but not for me
reviewDescription: I bought this because the fruit flies in my tiny apt kitchen were getting more and more annoying. My nephew said they worked pretty well for him, but I'm disappointed to say that even with 2 of them, it caught ZERO insects in 2 weeks.

reviewTitle: Not easy to use, not worth it
reviewDescription: Comes with several wood sticks and plastic sticks to hold the trap up if you want it into the soil. I like to put my traps inside the pot close to my plants. You have to be creative when you attach these to the traps without the trap sticking to your fingers. The paper adhering to the trap is not easy to remove, there is a very tiny part to pull the paper out but very tiny that your finger or fingers will end up sticking to the trap. Comes with a tiny plastic shovel, but a plastic spoon works just as well. If you use the green wire that came with the product to hang the trap, then the trap may stick to the leaves or whichever is close to where it is hung and then you have to break off the leaves to remove the trap. If it's a big leaf then you may not be able to use that trap or at least one side of it. Not practical for me. I don't recommend it.

Worthless. After two days and nights in my pantry and on a basket of tomatoes I've caught a total of two fruit flies and they are still swarming in the pantry and kitchen. These do nothing to attract flies.
Doesn't work at all don't waist your money on these.

reviewTitle: Please read this before buying! Bird hazard!
reviewDescription: These are a hazard to birds. I tried to see if any other reviews mentioned it and I found two that touched upon it, but I think it's important for everyone to know this when using this product. If you use it outdoors, small birds can get caught in them. I keep a few potted plants outside by apartment and while it only caught about two fruit flies (of hundreds currently on my plants) it did catch the wing of a small finch. Poor little guy was completely stuck. I used oil to get it off of him but I have no idea if his wing is broken or if he'll be able to get the oil off of him because it happened in the middle of the night and he scurried off. I feel terrible and really wish this product had a big red warning on the front for everyone to see. Obviously after this happened I threw them all out.
Not sticky. Doesn't work well for fungus gnats

I was excited to get these traps I read the reviews and was like immediately yes ! However I was very disappointed they did not work at all. I did not expect these to completely eliminate my gnats issue but I was very hopeful it would help. Not once but twice did I watch a gnat land on the trap walk across it then fly away. They were placed all over my home for over a month and not 1 had caught a fly. Not sure if I purchased a bad batch or what but would not recommend waste of money.

I had 10 flies cruising around my place. So I ordered these and put 3 of them hanging up. 2 inside and 1 outside. It has been 2 days and I still have flies cruising my place and none sticking on the fly traps.
I have had 2 up and there are so many flies!! One must have flown to close because it is actually stuck. Don't waste the money!
Why did you pick this product vs others?: I hoped to be able to catch some large house flies that were iny house. It didn't attract A SINGLE Fly though there were many flying around.
Zero stars would be best for this awful product! Sticks to you rather than catching flies or gnats!!!!! Had to use Goo Gone to get the sticky stuff off of me.

reviewTitle: nothing to draw flies to the glue
reviewDescription: Total waste of money. It's just sticky paper - no attractant. Had it up for 2 days absolutely 0 flies. Vinegar + dish soap in a small cup nearby did the trick.

reviewTitle: Easy to use but no flies caught
reviewDescription: Three sticky tape fly paper strips were unrolled & hung in an area with many flies visible within a few feet. Unfortunately, not a single fly was attracted to the strips. Not one was caught on the sticky strips after days of use.
It appeared to be like any of the others, but it’s not. After unraveling them holding them straight, they continue to wind back up in a tight twist, they won’t relax to hang properly.

I don't know if I just have have very crafty flies, but they had zero interest in killing themselves on one of these traps. I've had a handful of flies bugging me in the house so I bought these traps as a cheap and easy way to get rid of them. Hung up three of them, one by the window, one hanging from a light, and another in the threshold. They seem sticky enough to work...but watching a pair of flies circle around one of these traps for hours without ever wanting to land on it makes me think I may have wasted my money. Maybe they would work better in an massive infestation, but did nothing for my low-key household situation.

I've been buying these for years, and never had a problem. But this last shipment, every single roll was defective in some way. There were thumbtacks missing, or the thumbtacks broke as soon I tried to place them. Several of the rolls, the paper ripped apart as I was gently pulling them out. Now I see that I can't get a return because it's past the deadline. I'm sure this is a defective package. But I can't recommend them now because the quality was ridiculously poor.

reviewTitle: Flies are not attracted to the product. Useless unfortunately.
reviewDescription: Flies are not attracted to the product at all.

reviewTitle: Don’t waste your time and money.
reviewDescription: I don’t understand why some people say this product works. I had it hanging for three days about round an area with a bunch of flies and it didn’t catch a single fly in three days so this product is a waste of your time and money. The fact that I have to give it any stars at all is a shame. It shouldn’t even have the one.

reviewTitle: Do you really want to purchase faulty garbage?
reviewDescription: I purchased this product, really another terrible item. When I pulled lightly on the loop on top, it snapped off, flew up and this item got stuck in my hair! A 75.00 haircut later, I tried another one, loop was too flimsy container would not open. Consumers do not buy a sticky fly trap to wait another few days for another fly trap, be out 7.00, and a haircut later! I am very weary of Amazon and their continued profit from selling garbage!

Don't waste your money on this product. I hung all 8 tapes, and only 2 were effective in catching flies. I actually saw a fly go onto a hanging flytrap and flew away. They do not all have the same sticking power. We finally bought fly swatters and within 2 hours we rid our house of these pests.

Earlier this summer I had a housefly infestation in my kitchen. Had some old fly tapes of a different brand. Used all three which became covered in flies within a day of hanging them. Couple weeks later the flies returned so I bought these. Not a single fly has lasted on them in the days I’ve had them hanging. I think they repel flies rather than attracting them.
We set two up in different locations in the house and garage. Not one single fly went to them yet we swatted and killed 15-20 of them over a week thanks to a trashcan issue. Throwing the rest away.

reviewTitle: Total piece of crap!!! What a gimmick!!!
reviewDescription: Do not work at all! Flies all over my garage hung this thing for 3 days not one fly on it. These are a gimmick. I have no idea what they used to put in them back in the day but these things would attract a hundred flies. This thing is sticky but a fly would have to randomly land on there. I have no idea how there is even one decent review. These are trash and I want my money back.
Flys were all around it but not attracted to it. Pulled apart from the base too easily so didn’t hang properly

These are definitely not for inside the house, unless you don't mind looking at an already ugly thing, that eventually gets uglier when there are flailing, dying flies on it. It does not kill them quickly. I suspect they eventually die of thirst, in the next day or two. Maybe my flies are different, but they were not at all attracted to the Fly Magnets. They buzzed all around them, often landing close by, but not ON the product. I think the folks who get flies on theirs must also have a lot more flies that got away. The only way I could make the Fly Magnets enticing enough to land on, was to place them in front of the windows, so they were in the flies' way of where they were going anyway. But if I have to do that, I'd rather use the stick-on anti-fly products that are specifically made for use on windows. Because the Fly Magnet could more aptly be called the Curtain Magnet.
Dried out

1 stars for stickiness that's it!. Broke 2 while unraveling. Two others coiled right back up while extending. Threw the remaining ones in the trash. Re-ordered Raid fly strips which I had before, they worked great. Buy these at your own risk. Se photos. Longer strip is the Raid. Shorter curled is this item!

This week my house was overrun with flies so I decided to try one of these again, even though I had been disappointed the first time. There must have been two dozen flies in the house. Yikes! But in three days this thing caught TWO FLIES. I even witnessed a fly get caught and then ESCAPE. I ended up chasing them down with a vacuum.

We are in the middle of a kitchen remodel and have temporarily moved everything to the garage for a makeshift kitchen. With the weather, being what it is, we have had a ton of flies. I hung two of these strips in the garage to help combat. After a week they had not Attracted or captured one fly As much as I wanted these to work, they don’t.

reviewTitle: Absolute waste of money and time
reviewDescription: These things begin by being very difficult to open and use. They open by pulling the top off a small cylinder (requiring significant effort) which results in a tightly twisted strip of sticky paper which is impossible to untwist. It hangs by using a supplied thumbtack which leaves a hole in the attachment location. The sticky (twisted) strip doesn't seem to have any substance which might attract a fly, so that it simply hangs these looking ugly until you finally give up and discard the (flyless) "strip", while swatting flies which have ignored the useless flycatcher. Do not buy this stuff, or even use it if given to you.
This item sucks. The fly in my kitchen don't even go near this is product. WHATEVER YOU DO, DON'T BUY THIS PRODUCT, IT DOES NOT WORK.
Warning! This is advertised as a 4 pack but only one box is shipped. Do not buy!

I am not a fan of sticky fly strips to begin with but even less of a fan of these. I purchased these and another fly trap both made by Terro. Neither one is worth a dime. I've caught more flys on a dollar store fly strip. The have been hanging for a while and there is not one flying insect stuck to it. The only thing stuck to is was my hand while trying to hang them followed by my wall and cabinet. Save your hard earned money and go to the dollar store for this product. A lot cheaper and a much better product.
These are terrible. They do not u with d properly, or the tear. Waste of money...
The flys are not even remotely attracted to this. There's no smell, it's sticky but captures nothing.
