My flies love this tape! They take turns landing on it and seeing who can get unstuck the fastest! 🥰 I live in a livestock neighborhood and the flies can be intense in the summer. I hung this fly tape in my kitchen (a high traffic area for flies) and was confused after a whole day passed and not a single fly was stuck. I sat for only a minute to watch and see if the flies were just avoiding it, and watched a fly land on it, struggle for a second, and then fly back off. Needless to say, I will not be purchasing again.
Doesn't work as advertised. We have a fly infestation issue, and after 2 weeks and multiple placements, not one of these have not caught a single one.

I've had one of these fly strips hanging in my camper for over a week. I have killed many flies in that week with my fly swatter, but not one fly, has gone on to that fly trap. Don't waste your money.

First, I did read a number of the low rated reviews and just ignored them. Well, I have two strips hanging up for a few days now and not one fly caught yet. I'm pretty sure I used this same brand years ago when I had a problem and they worked great. Since that time they must have changed the formula. Just like so many others have noted they are almost impossible to straighten out to keep from spooling back up and THEY FAIL TO DO THE ONE THING THEY WERE MEANT TO DO-CATCH FLIES!

Our house is full of flies and we hoped this would solve that problem. Not one fly has been attracted to the the two we've set up yesterday in our kitchen/family room area where they've been a problem. Absolute waste of money.

Hung these up a few days ago, caught a lot of flies. Noticed my cat would not leave the window today and when I finally went to check it out there were 2 small birds caught in it. They were still alive and I was able to remove them but I know the glue stuck to their feathers and they probably won't survive long. I love animals so this is heartbreaking for me. Please do not buy, I'm sure honey bees can get caught in them too.
Strips were not coming apart. Not sticky

What a waste of money . Flies fly around the strip and none of them are attracted like it advertises. I will never buy this product again ..plz save your money and buy another product if you want to ..

reviewTitle: Waste of money for inside house flies.
reviewDescription: Seriously these did nothing, there's no pheromone or anything. It's purely sticky? We continued to kill all the house flies by hand and cleaned the kitchen daily. We also bought a UV fly trap, which worked amazing for the fruit flies/potentially the young flies...I don't know. Anyways, don't buy these.

Super sticky and I’m sure any flies that land on them will be there forever. Buuttttt. They must not have any form of attractant on them because the flies do not land on them. Had them up in my woodshop, metal shop and my house and after 3 days, lots of flies buzzing, none on the sticky traps.

reviewTitle: doesn't work unless they land on it.
reviewDescription: Have had a fly around now in my kitchen for 2 days. I hung one up over the counter and now I've put three by the garbage can. He flies around and pays them no mind at all. If he actually lands on them, they'll probably stick. But there's nothing drawing them to land on the product.
Horrible. The loop broke off so you couldn't expand the strip. I returned it.
TOTAL CRAP DO NOT BUY THIS JUNK

I've seen these things on the ceilings of "hoarders" on TV and they were always covered with flies. So I tried to set them up in my house. They were nearly impossible to unroll and wouldn't stick to an I tried to use to hang them. I''m sorry to say this, but it was a complete bust. I ended up throwing them away (waste of money)

When we disturbed a big trashcan on my patio we did'nt know it was loaded with flies and dozens and dozens of them got in my house. I bought this product thinking it would catch most of them. Well, it only caught 2 so I ended up buying a flyswatter which did a heck of a lot better job! I would not recommend this product. Get yourself a flyswatter instead.
Product was totally unusable items were like melted and all the glue was sticking them together. Not one was usable.

reviewTitle: DISAPPOINTED. Difficult to open and set up. DOES NOT attract flies.
reviewDescription: Difficult to unfurl and flies are not attracted to this product. Not a strong odor and NOT easy to use or it's a rotten product and not worth the cost.
Put these up in our RV because we had at least a dozen flies. Not one fly landed in them in 2 days so ended up using a fly seater.
I would give no stars if I could. After two weeks several flies killed by me with a FLY SWATTER. None by Fly Magnet!

We've had a handful of flies in our house, so I bought this product in hopes that it might catch them, sadly, the trap has been completely ineffective and hasn't caught a single fly for the week it has been up. While it is orderless and it seems the adhesive is intact, I am disappointed that we haven't been able to resolve our fly issue with this trap...

I ordered these because they promoted no smell/scent. But they absolutely have a smell and I was triggered with a massive migraine & itching/burning skin upon opening. 2 days later, the one I set up outdoors, still smells! We cannot use indoors due to severe health impacts. If you have MCAS, DO NOT ORDER! One star for having a smell when boasting “Unscented.” Also has only caught 4 flies, so not very effective. Flies still harass my poor dog and I’m constantly shooing them away so he can rest peacefully. Improvements desperately needed to be effective and unscented, as advertised.
Don't waste your money on this. It doesn’t work.
I live in Wisconsin we get massive flys in the spring n can't seem to get rid of them even in the winter

Our neighbors have problems with dogs on their balcony, and a huge number of flies are coming into the room and onto the neighboring balconies. We tried to combat this with these flypapers. It's impossible to catch anything except trash if they're hanging outside, and not a single insect wants to be indoors. A useless product.
Absolutely worthless! Strip hung for a week next to a window that always attracts flies. Not one fly caught. In fact at one point I thought I could hear the flies laughing at me.

As the flies buzzed around my kitchen, this product attracted not even one. Oh, it did catch 2 or 3, but I'm sure they just flew into the sticky strip. This stuff doesn't work at all. My fly remedy: I got a fly swatter. Works every time.

reviewTitle: Thank you fly magnet you changed my life
reviewDescription: Wow unrolling this product was an adventure. Before it even slid out of the tube like a nose nuddle it curled up on itself and was tubular and 4 inches long. That means only one side it is usable. I grabbed a handly dowl and piece of string to secure it. My hand was covered with dodgy smelling slime as I tried to hang it, the thumb tack broke and jambed into the flesh of my thumb. I tried to hang it with tape. When hung, I wasn't sure which side was tape and which side was fly slime. It hung for 5 minutes killed one fly and crashed to the ground and became tangled with my cat. This is a product in sore need of modernizing. Live the smell of fly slime after 10 hand washings.

i initially purchased this due to a fly infestation which drove me nuts. i read the reviews and saw some photos that convinced me in all my desperation to purchase this as it seemed to work well at the price point. i literally had a small swarm (appx 20 or more flies) mostly in the kitchen area so i put these up near and around the kitchen. for a week i had these things up- you pull this sticky tape out of the tube and you have to unravel the winded sticky tape which was a pain and then you have to use the enclosed thumb tack to hang it in place. getting it up on the ceiling to let the unraveled sticky tape hang down was such a burn- brought out a ladder to hang them. all this trouble would be worth it if it caught flies, but sadly after a WEEK of having these up they collectively caught ONE fly. freaking one. a fly swatter worked way better compared to these. i threw the rest of them away. waste of money and energy spent trying to get them into proper position.

WORST PRODUCT EVER!!! does NOT unroll. Turns into giant chunk of unrolled sticky garbage. Forcefully unrolled it, covering hands in glue, for it to spring back into a padded stuck together pile.of trash. Glue stuck to hands for over 24hrs after washing with about a dozen kinds of soap. Rather deal with 1 million flies than one roll of this trash.

Does not work. I have followed the directions, other advice, and tips, but fruit flies will not take the bait. I have placed the strip where they hang out, and have tried to draw them even closer with a bright light above it. Have tried to attract them by using a bright light above the strip, and have baited them to the area with a piece of fruit. They will come to the area, but completely ignore the strip.

We had a sudden fly infestation and ordered these to combat the problem. Unfortunately, of the three strips we deployed, we only captured one fly. We had manually eliminated over theee dozen ourselves. The product is extremely sticky and a bit of a hassle to deploy. If it worked, it might have been worth it, but it's not worth it for one fly.

Does not work! Flies have no interest in landing on this tape, they just fly all around it. I assumed there was something to attract flies to this paper, but I see no evidence of that.Fly strip has been hanging for a full week and there are three flies on the tape. Several times I have found flies sitting on the anchor tube and they fly away when I get close

reviewTitle: Product did not work for the flies in my attic
reviewDescription: I purchased these in the past and they worked great. This batch did not work. The flies that it caught ended up breaking free. I think this is designed more for fruit flies than the large flies we have. I tried returning to Amazon, but they only offer Staples drop off or $8 ups pickup. I live 1 hour from the nearest staples and I only spent $14 on the item. I hope this deters others from buying. Honestly I don’t buy much from Amazon anymore due to Staples only being the free option to return items. I guess it’s a good way to deter buying things that have a high return.

reviewTitle: Don't buy!!! Cheap even for sticky fly paper!!!!!!!
reviewDescription: What a waste of money!!!!!! Once unraveled it just curls back up onto itself. After the fourth one I threw them in the garbage. I should have bought the black flag brand again. It unraveled, remained straight and didn't stick to itself.

reviewTitle: Not sticky enough. Doesn't catch many
reviewDescription: Do not unfurl well even when twisting. Flys that are attracted to it, most of them escape . Tons of flies in the area and as you can see in the picture nothing hardly sticks when they do it's just one or two flies and then they disappear later.

reviewTitle: Weak feeble material keeps tearing apart
reviewDescription: Half of them tear apart when I try to extend the strips from tube. I've had 4 tear in order to get 4 that function. They are a very feeble and weak material.
I had a strip in each of my six rooms in my apartment. After a couple of weeks of not catching a single fly, I returned these. And, yes, there were flies in my apartment.

reviewTitle: 1 Fly in 10 Days. Not working for my garage.
reviewDescription: Flies all over my gararage for some reason this season. The catch rate is 1 fly for 2 traps in the last 10 days. Ended up going in there with afly swater instead. This is a great brand, but the functionality of this product is less than desireable.

reviewTitle: They somehow changed, and suck now
reviewDescription: Not sure what they did, but they no longer work like they used to. First, they won't fully "uncoil". IF you slowly pull the tube, get all the paper out, do a bunch a "wrist twists" so its a flat sheet of sticky paper about 2 feet long. Once you let go it snaps back like a Yo-Yo and turns into a 6" long pencil. Second, the wrapping tube is not an "anchor" or weight as the box says. Its so light a flimsy that once again, you let go and it coils back up like a Yo-Yo. They are very sticky, so the glue still works. But, whatever paper and tube they use are garbage now. Such garbage that the item is no longer useful. Gonna look for cheap brand ones to see if they work. Terro products are usually good and work great, this is one of their FAILS!

I attempted to unroll the first one very gently. It started to rip at the bottom, so I had to pull it apart. Once I got it to unravel, it twisted back up to a long, thin cone shape. I had to add something to the bottom to add weight so it would stay unrolled. The second one broke off at the top, wrapped around itself and my fingers and arm and had to be weighed down, as well. I don't know if they work for the fruit flies, yet, but setting them up is a sticky nightmare!

reviewTitle: I have no idea why this is highly reviewed
reviewDescription: Before I left for vacation, I had a fly infestation problem in the house. I texted my mom and asked her if she could put up some fly paper. When I came back, there were a few sheets of clear fly paper on the window and several flies that had gotten caught. The sheets were easy to remove and throw away. I tried using one of these fly paper rolls, and it is incredibly difficult to use. The sticky is all over and it's very difficult to manipulate without getting it on you. I got the glue on my hands and arms and someone on the window. And now I'm not sure how to clean off the glue that's on the window as it took me several hours to scrub the glue that was on my hands and arms. I wish i knew what brand my mom used as it was way better than this product.
I hung up 2 at a time and neither one of them got any. Then tried hanging some again once in awhile and I think it caught a total of 3 the whole entire time they were up. Not a good product at all

Over the course of three days, the multiple tape strands we hung up BOTH inside AND outside, where we had major fly infestations, trapped a grand total of THREE flies, while we watched dozens and dozens of flies dance around them. The tape is very sticky, no problem there, it just doesn't catch any flies! A complete waste of my money.

reviewTitle: They don't work! TERRO is terrible
reviewDescription: I was overrun with flies/ fruit flies. Came from my kitchen trash bins. Bought the fly traps to clean up the area. I hung 3 strips around the kitchen- above the sink, above the trash bin and in the pantry. After a week, i have captured fewer than a dozen flies. It's more effective to put out glasses with fruit juice and let them drown themselves. The flies are often in close proximity to the strips, but aren't attracted and at time stick briefly and then fly away. Altogether, a bad investment! AND this after a week in use.

DO NOT BUY. These are truly terrible. I have tried 3 strips, and they all have failed. The push pins are worthless and just bend. I couldn’t stick them in the drywall of my ceiling. I finally got one to go in, but as I gently twisted and pulled it down to unravel it, the strip ripped in half. The remaining half then sprung back into a tight coil close to the ceiling. It will not catch anything. These are trash.

TERRO usually does it’s job to kill, but these glue strips I tried were terrible to unwind/hang…and for whatever the reason, I’ve got 2 ugly strips hanging…..and not ONE fly was drawn to them…..strange, because they are there! I can’t hang them in just amy room…..they are hideous looking….only the basement and garage…..and I’ll never buy this product! And you can’t return opened sticky mess! (wasted money as one was thrown out!) Very sticky ugly brown strip, VERY annoying to unroll/unwind/open. NEVER will I get these again. Trusting TERRO product, I bought a 3 pack….one was so twisted and ruined trying to unwind the strip to hang, I had to throw it out. I’ll be looking for another type from another company for sure.

I would suggest indoor use only. I bought this for our patio area to keep flies from breeding near our outdoor areas. The glue strip itself works well, catches just about anything with wings, however, it is difficult to get it to hang from anything without falling off, blowing away, or blowing into something else and getting stuck. There is no weight to the roll so when the wind blows, even just a little, it falls off of hooks or nails and then gets stuck to the deck or plants or fence or whatever its around. The glue is NOT easy to remove which makes it a great trap but also a pain if it falls on the deck. I would suggest this for indoor use where no people or pets are around.
Do not waste your money on this garbage. The fly paper doesn't unwind like other fly papers making it almost pointless to hang up.
They don’t unfurl when you pull out the tape so they don’t hang like you would expect. They also don’t trap any flies. Don’t buy these, try another kind

reviewTitle: Read the 1 star reviews!!!! And AVOID
reviewDescription: The positive: Came on time and it’s sticky. The negative: Impossible to unroll and place it. Other fly papers roll out to provide a large surface for the flies to stick to; this one rolls back to its original shape after you open it and try to hang it (like a 35mm film). The first picture shows how much you can unroll it AFTER 10 MIN OF CLOSE COMBAT that requires another few minutes of washing up. The second encounter (pic #2) lasted only seconds as the paper snapped while I tried to unroll it. The verdict: whether it will catch flies or not (I don’t know yet), there are many other brands of fly paper that do the job and they do it without these unnecessary hassles, so LOOK ELSEWHERE.

I just received the item and tried to pull out the rolled paper. Each time it tore and then it got all over my hand and arm. It does not easily unroll as seen in the videos. And as other products have done that I used in years past. Quite the mess and now I have very little surface area for the fly to find. I am very disappointed. ZERO stars. Use the slightly more expensive known brand instead. I am ordering another brand NOW.
So far not a single fly and we hung up 2 of them according to instructions. Finally bought a couple of cheap fly swatters.

I live in the south where it gets super hot and flies. Are everywhere in the summer. I had bought these to put around the inside of my house and an attempt to keep flies to a minimum. Although I saw flies in the house, I didn’t see any of them on these paper strips, so I’m not really sure that they actually work.

Re: Sewer Flies (NOT) 20230503: No sewer or house flies. 20230428: The tape is so sticky every time I try to remove the tape the tape broke. The container directions do not indicate the best direction to turn the container s you remove the fly tape. I plan not to purchase this brand again. Down graded the stars from three to two. 20230322: No interior Sewer Flies caught in two weeks. Will try to catch exterior sewer flies beginning today.

reviewTitle: Don't buy if you have cats
reviewDescription: We got for a few fruit flies lol, cat liked it more. Got all over its fur when it rolled around. Don't know if they work but they sure are sticky

These might work for some folks but they absolutely did NOT work for me. I have a big fly problem around my chicken coop area and I put these out but caught zero flies in a week (despite flies being literally everywhere.) You'd think I'd have at least caught one by accident but nope. Maybe they are made for some other type of fly, but the ones around here couldn't care less about whatever sweet goo is on the tape. Best go with one of those fly trap with the liquid even though they stink.

reviewTitle: Flys don't seem attracted to the sticky trap. Then when they land on it they fly right off of it
reviewDescription: Flys don't seem attracted to the sticky trap at all. Then if they land on it they fly right off of it. I witnessed the flies landing on it and taking right off like it was nothing a few times today. Don't seem to work at all.

so this is suppose to be a fly magnet but I have caught gnats, couple of mosquitos, bugs and other small flying insect but I have only caught 3 flies! It has been out for 2 weeks now. I even place on in our kitchen window because flies were accumulating there, very annoying. nope no flies there either. they sill bump and buzz against the window and you can see them in the corners and the frame hinges but not on the fly magnet. so my review is if you have flying small insects you want to catch this will do the trick but not with flies.
this product is above average but for price it is listed for is recommended

It didn’t separate as directed, but that was just a little bit more difficult than necessary. The real problem is that in a small room with several flies, none of them ever got caught in it. I even found one dead on the floor right below it where presumably the fly chose to starve to death rather than touch it.
Never attracted to flies flies came near it and just flew away from it just was not reliable at all.
so much memory in the “paper” that it never relaxes or extends, even when weighted. yes, it’s very sticky, but that’s not very useful when the deployed length is 1/6th of what it should be.
Did not work the way I expected it to. It’s very sticky but flies were not drawn to it. Some flys that did get stuck would come off.

Super sticky but they come out too curly. That is, when you stretch it out and hang its a really tight spiral that sort of sticks to itself and also has reduced target area for the flies. Would rather have flypaper strips that hang more flat.
Placed in an area where flys constantly hang out and over several weeks caught a honey bee, a moth and 2 flys. Complete waste of money.
Not so great... the holding container ripped off of a few of these as I hung them up...never had that happen before with other flying insect lines I've used for the last 30 years....

I am sure these are effective at catching flies if you can get them hung. The sticky curl is very long. Maybe I am a klutz but I had trouble getting these hung, they stick hard to anything, even my hair! I found them very hard to use.

I bought these to put on porch by my cat's food to get the Flys, it caught every kind of flying bug you can think of except not ONE fly, they ignored it and kept after the cat food. Not one Fly was caught!

reviewTitle: Almost no flies stuck — waste of money
reviewDescription: The size is extremely small. It barely attaches to anything and is very inconvenient to install. I used three of them, and almost no flies got stuck. The rest went straight to the trash. Very disappointing and ineffective. Total waste of money 🫠
These do not work. My garage is FULL of black flys, the fly strip was up for 6-7 hours. Not a single fly went for it.

reviewTitle: My flies suffer from attention deficit 😒
reviewDescription: Yellow is a color that insects love, whis is why all these sticky traps are yellow; but ny flies do not pay attention to this items specifically, they fly around it and do not get trapped. I have added two lictures for comparison between this items, and another bigger item, and you can see the results.

reviewTitle: Doesn't do the job of fly catching!
reviewDescription: Flys NOT drawn to it. Waste of money.
Real sticky but does not attract the flies, they just fly around bugging me. I hung three around sink area one fly was caught in a full 24 hour test.

Since putting up 3 strips, I have killed about a dozen fruit flies on the wall immediately next to the strips. The strips have caught 0 flies. They seem to ignore it entirely, even when I placed it in common places and right next to the light. It just has no effect at all.

I had a small fly infestation and hung these up where the flies were hanging out and they didn’t catch any flies. If they did it wasn’t noticeable as it was maybe one or two. They were easy to unroll and set up but didn’t work for me.

reviewTitle: Nice deal for the price. Not sure if it works.
reviewDescription: Rated this a 2 Star under the assumption that it works for someone. Based on my personal experience I haven't caught a single fly on it. I have one in the kitchen, dinning room and a few hallway / entry ways at my work. I wouldn't say that we have a ton of Flys in those areas but we have enough that this should have caught one by now. 3 weeks after installation we still haven't caught anything.

These don’t really work that well. Sure, it has the ability to capture flies…. But I feel like they’re not really attracted to it. We just moved houses and with all the boxes/furniture etc, we left the doors open for a while, which came with flies in the house. I put these up and left them up for about a week. One of them captured a total of 2 whole flies… that’s it. The rest captured nothing. I’ve squashed like 25x as many in that time frame. Not an ideal product, even if it is pretty cheap

We had large black flies buzzing all around and we’ve tried several of these in different locations. We left them up for over a week and they caught ZERO flies. The flies are still driving us crazy. I moved it to 2 stars because one did catch a few fruit flies but that’s it.

I have 3 of them set in my shop, one near the entrance to my building which is always open and 2 more further in. So far after a month of setting them up they have not caught a single house fly or large fly. They catch an occasional really small fly but that is it. Not effective in my opinion.

I don't know why, but I ended up with a ton of flies in the bathroom, there was nothing for them to eat so they just kept buzzing around randomly. I set up a couple of the fly traps around the bathroom and got almost none of them on the paper. It seems like they only get trapped if they randomly decide to land on the paper, rather than being actually attracted to it. So I went to lowe's and got the same kind of trap from a different brand and as soon as I put it up they started landing on that trap's paper. It seems flies just aren't attracted to this brands paper. I wouldn't reccomend buying.

reviewTitle: Has not caught a single fly
reviewDescription: I got these because I have a bad fly problem in my apartment and thought this would solve it. It did not. Ive had them up for a week and they have not caught a single fly. And my fly problem is worse than ever. Gonna need a zapper or something else.

reviewTitle: Doesn't attract flies, so it can take a while to work (protip: put bait on it)
reviewDescription: Keep in mind that it doesn't actually attract flies, so it takes a while for the flies to find it, and they may get lucky and not find it at all. I had one up for a day, and it didn't catch a fly until I put on a few drops of honey and bits of fish to attracted the flies. I also tried a different method and stuck the paper line on some cardboard, and then added some bait (honey and meat/fish work well). It made it a little more manageable to add the bait and position it. However, after a few days, even with the bait, it wasn't that successful.

reviewTitle: A fly trap that doesn't catch flies.
reviewDescription: The flies in my house had a great time staring at this trap as they flew right by it for days. Somehow managed to not snag a single fly. In a very clean apartment.
I've seen these around absolutely covered in flies. I decided to try them and didn't stick a single fly on 4 traps. They are very sticky though.

reviewTitle: Day 2. No flies are attracted, we continue to wait..
reviewDescription: Actually, two have walked around on it. I don’t think they’re sticking? Wait, I think one just winked and did a moonwalk, what the !?
I had just one fly in the kitchen, hung this up for 2 days and the fly never went near it. Might work when you have swarms but not to catch one

Sticky it is but it gets all over you just trying to get it out of the package and it doesn’t wash off. Had to get alcohol to remove and it never hung flat. What a mess" - flys are almost better than this. Ugh!
Some reviews said it working good. Bought them and placed right in where the flies are … but only one fly was trapped in. So I returned the product and get money back.
We had a bunch of random flies so we put up 5 of these between our kitchen, living room, and dining area. We had these up for a week and not one of them caught a single fly.
Terro is ok with ant control, but these fly strips did not work for me. 2 stars cuz I caught a few moths.

Didn't really like this. Based on other reviews, I thought this would be a very efficient way to get rid of the flies inside the house. But no. Barely caught any. Flies indoors do not seem attracted to this adhesive... There were some flies, outside the house, which got stuck onto the adhesive strip that was placed outside. This adhesive may be a waste of money. Best to look for some other stronger adhesive options or even the electric indoor zappers.

Honestly I bought this due to my friend suggesting they are good. The flies don't even stick to it, they don't even get close to it, there was 1 in the kitchen and one in the living room. We had a couple of flies but none on this tape. Either the adhesive is not strong or the smell so the flies can go by is not strong one of those two. Pros: no smell, easy to use. Cons: flies do not stick nor fly by it. Would I buy it again: most definitely not.

I tried this product Because i was desperate to get rid of fruit flies. It’s my nderstanding that the that the flies would be attracted to it, enter the bottle and die. If I’m correct, the product was useless,as it did not draw a single fly into it, despite my having left it open on my kitchen counter for 4 days. It just so happens that the infestation had significantly improved by the time I received and set the bottle out, and the flies were actually almost gone by the end of the 4 days, so IF the product works just by sitting open and on the counter, it MAY be that the product did work. At this time I’m just trying to be fair and offer the seller the benefit of the doubt; however, I cannot, now give the product more than one star. I invite the seller to clarify the situation, especially if the product does not need to draw the flies into it for it to work. Also, however, the odor emitted from the jar is so strong and pervasive that it made me nauseous and I doubt I could have tolerated it much longer.
Left out for about a month and caught 0 flies in my kitchen. Just dried up. Waste of money…Avoid.
Not worth the money. This does nothing. Its vinger and that's what I've been using.

High priced vinegar. Didn't work for me. Very dissapointed. I usually never take the time to write a review, but this product was so bad. A week went by and just one fruit fly. Another week and still nothing. They don't even seem to go near it. Not worth the risk of accidently spilling the contents, due to open container. Unbelievable how some people love it.

reviewTitle: I put out 2 traps & watched cloud's of fruit flies surrounding it
reviewDescription: I had an area where some fruits and vegetables had gone bad. I sat both of the items with their tops opened ( sure smelled like Apple Cider Vinegar to me) and for 3 days watched cloud's of fruit flies flying in clouds around it. I saw them land in the bottle but not a single one went into the liquid and died. I've had better luck using plain white vinegar.

You can achieve the same results BETTER by simply putting some apple cider vinegar in a small dish. This is overpriced for a container that isn’t special. And a solution that is nothing special. Ol Aunt Fannie scammed us all. Aunt Fannie… more like Aunt Farty. Don’t waste your money.
This product arrived half-empty and the little that remained did not attract any fruit flies. Zero. Don't waste your money.

This didn’t work at all. I left it out for several weeks and it didn’t do anything. There isn’t really a trap, it’s just a bottle of vinegar, with a fully open flip top lid, that the gnats can fly in and out of. I feel like this was a complete waste of money and time. It needs a better design as there isn’t a way to actually trap the bugs.

I bought this brought June 2025. I opened the lid and placed it on the kitchen counter next to the fruit bowl. I let it sit there in disturbed until September 3, 2025, absolutely nothing happened to the flying fruit flies. The fruit flies ignored the bottle all together! Do not waste your money !

reviewTitle: No longer effective, change in formula?
reviewDescription: UPDATED REVIEW: Two years ago my apartment has very annoying fruit flies - I lived in a house of four girls, with all of our bananas and avocados and tomatoes, etc, that made it impossible to live produce free for long enough to make the bugs go away. I bought this product, and within days there were over 15 fruit flies that it had gotten rid of. I bought more, and our fruit fly problem went away and stayed away. This year, with the summer heat and plethora of summer fruit, the fruit flies are back. And this time, after buying 5 of these liquid traps, none of them are working. I even see fruit flies flying immediately next to the traps, but after 4 weeks of hoping they would start working again they are not, and I am thoroughly disappointed. The company must have changed their formula for this drastic of a decline in its effectiveness.

So so. It smells like vinegar mostly and there’s no way to prevent a spill if it’s open. The flies land on the side a lot. The second one I ordered didn’t work at all. Like they got immune to it or something. Orange juice did the trick in a fraction of the time so save your money.

reviewTitle: Doesn’t kill them, only attracts them.
reviewDescription: This doesn't work, it attracts more fruit flies but does not kill them. I used it for more then 2 months and hasn't killed one… I have a lot more flies then before actually.
Ineffective -- we recently had an issue with fruit flies and this product didn't do its job. Better luck with homemade solution of water, vinegar, and dish soap. Also found sticky traps worked.

reviewTitle: Fruit Flies Roundly Ignored this substance.
reviewDescription: Have many fruit flies. Only one was drawn to this solution. Also, one of two bottles leaked all over the packaging. Returned after let it sit there on my counter tempting none of the fruit flies.

reviewTitle: Don’t buy if you love your life!
reviewDescription: Do not buy this! I repeat! Do not buy this! Within 10 minutes your house will be full of something that you cannot put your finger on other than grasping for fresh air and trying not to meet your maker. Someone decided that putting some sort of mix of ether, apple cider vinegar and some concoction of liquid from the pits of hell together and put it in a bottle said to be safe for home use and thought that was a good idea. 10/10 do not recommend! Just let the fruit flies dance!

reviewTitle: Total waste of money in my case
reviewDescription: I have been battling fruit flies in my kitchen for a while. I've tried several methods including fly strips as well as multiple homemade traps using differing methods (mason jar with apple cider vinegar & dish soap, wine bottles with a bit of wine left, mason jars with apple cider vinegar *and* rotting fruit with saran wrap & holes poked, jars with paper funnel, etc.) I've even heated the apple cider vinegar before setting out the traps to produce an aroma of the fermenting vinegar/fruit. I had high hopes for this product but it literally only caught 1 or 2 fruit flies. I caught many more with just regular fly strips that catch them by chance. I left this product in two separate places in my kitchen for over a week and the fruit flies ignored it completely. Total waste of money in my case. I got rid of my fruit flies by actively spraying them with a spray bottle filled with dish soap & water, fly strips, and pouring boiling water down the drain every day (and being diligent about not leaving food crumbs out). Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch did not help at all and was a waste of money in my case. The fruit flies I had completely ignored this product (which smells like it is simply apple cider vinegar anyway).

reviewTitle: An expensive version of a cheap home remedy that doesn't work
reviewDescription: I probably should have researched the product a bit more before buying it. After opening it it became very obvious from the smell and look what it was. It's literally just a bottle of apple cider vinegar with a few drops of soap. It even says so in the ingredient list "vinegar, water, ethyl lactate(aka soap)". To be fair it does kind of say that in the description so that's on me. Does it work? - About as well as apple cider vin and soap does (because that's what this product is). I've heard of this working for others so that's what I tried before buying this. For me I caught about 5 flies with my home made stuff and 3 with this product. My other home made trap was a bottle with a small amount of fruit juice and sealed with some plastic wrap with a few small holes. That has caught around 50. Still have an infestation. This, along with the home made stuff, will make the whole area smell of vinegar. If you really want to try this method then just make it yourself for half the cost. Most people have acv and soap laying around anyways. And if it doesn't work then at least you'll have left over soap you can use to clean and acv you can cook with.

I did not expect to open the bottle and find that it was a wide-open design and thus easily spillable. I have pets (including a very inquisitive and ungraceful cat), so I can't put it in certain places. I did try to put one in the kitchen but a tiny spider on the windowsill nearby is catching more fruit flies than this trap. The smell of vinegar is quite strong and not pleasant, and so far the fly trap hasn't caught a single obnoxious flying anything.
This has been put on my counter for over a day. There is not a single fruit fly in it. Definitely would not recommend. Terro traps are much more effective.

I finally received this item after a lengthy shipping period. Both bottles had the caps on wrong and all of the liquid was gone, the packaging is wet and smells like the product. Probably left a trail of fruit fly fluid the whole way.

reviewTitle: Didn't work for me AT ALL
reviewDescription: I have several fruit flies around the spyder plants in my kitchen (and near a counter at the other end of the kitchen where I have bananas hanging). I bought this with high hopes... but no go. Initially I opened the bottle, placed it between two plants and gave it a few days. Nothing. I then poured it into four small shallow open containers and placed three around all the plants and one near the bananas. Five days later, only 1 fruit fly ended up in any of the containers (I think it got in there by accident & drowned). I can say that after all this time, the smell of apple cider is still very strong. From reading other reviews, it seems that this is either wildly successful or an absolute miss. I'll leave them out for a while longer, but for me, this was a wasted purchase. I had hoped this would have some sort attractant I didn't know about and would work, but could have saved my money and gone back to a plain old cider vinegar & dishsoap mix (which gave me the same result but was much cheaper).

reviewTitle: Caught three flies only. Put your investment elsewhere.
reviewDescription: I paid almost $8 to catch three flies. My cat would have done a better job. Basically useless did absolutely nothing to solve my problem. I would be investing in something else.

sadly they do not work - don't know why but I was hoping this took care of my problem but not happening - going back to my local hardware store to get the ones that have worked in the past and these are going back to Amazon

Don’t waste your money! Not only is this nothing more than vinegar, it is a poor design. The top is just wide open. You have to have small holes so the fruit flies can get in but not out! They were all just hanging out on the popped top diving in for a slurp of vinegar and then resting back on the top. I had more success with a small jar of vinegar with cling wrap poked with a toothpick! China is dumping their junk and Amazon is selling it to us!

Initially I had high hope for this based on what others were saying. Unfortunately these were a huge let down for me. The design is clumsy at best and is a spill waiting to happen, It’s just a wide open bottle. I have left one out for over a week now where I have an abundance of fruit flies. Not a single fly in it, they’re not even interested in these. Save your money and find a working product.

reviewTitle: Save Your $ & Make A Trap From A Old Water Bottle & Vinegar!
reviewDescription: A homemade trap from an old water bottle & vinegar with a few drops of soap works better than this any day! This simply doesn't work. The reviewers that say it does must have some bad infestations or something as I've had this in various locations for several weeks and not ONE fruit fly was captured! I even added some foil over the top and poked a few holes as some suggested and it still is a miserable failure!
The bottle is odd designed, unprofessional, unpractical.
Ineffective, and will not get the job done. You could probably make something better on your own.
Do not waste your time nor your money. Zero effectiveness. Nothing else needs to be stated. Have a nice day.

reviewTitle: Just ACV! Biggest waste of money
reviewDescription: Biggest rip off ever. This item is literally a plastic spice jar of apple cider vinegar, you could make this for 50 cents in less time it would take to order this with items in your kitchen. Actually unreal that i just paid $8 for this, should have read the ingredients. Was specifically trying to not use apple cider vinegar because it stinks. DO NOT BUY! It doesn’t work very well.

reviewTitle: Didn't work- not a single fly
reviewDescription: We've been having a massive Fruit Fly issue and been trying everything we could to get it under control. I had high hopes for these but we've had two of them open for a week now and not a single Fruit fly has gone for these. We've moved them around to different areas of our pantry and kitchen and nothing. We've had better luck with homemade traps using dish soap w/ red wine or cider vinegar. They still aren't managing the situation but at least SOME dead flies have resulted from those.
I bought a two pack of these….they both collectively caught three gnats. Mind you, I’m having an infestation right now. My own home made ACV and sugar water with dawn dish soap worked way better.

reviewTitle: Charging for 12 bottles and sending just 1 bottle
reviewDescription: Ordered the 12 pack for $80 and received just one bottle. Requested a replacement and received one bottle instead of 12 again despite a charge for a 12 pack

reviewTitle: Don’t waste your time or money!!
reviewDescription: This was an absolute freaking waste of money. I am so irritated because it was one of those products that really stood out to me, like no way thousands of people are being dishonest and rating it highly consistently. Boy was I wrong. So, I have a summer time issue with fruit flies (due to fruit). Could not get rid of them for anything. I used the katchy, stem spray, and this product. I had my home PROFESSIONALLY cleaned, opened one of these jars, and left for a 2-week vacation. I just knew I would be coming back to a fruit fly free home thanks to this product working it’s magic. No such luck. I’ll stop there, but just take it from me. It is a waste of money, so don’t get your hopes up.

If I can give zero stars, I would, but it makes me pick at least one. Total waste of money. I’ve worked in the bar industry for years, I’ve seen barflies congregate around the alcohol for years. I bought this product because it said it was specifically formulated for barflies. At the three days only two barflies were caught in the syrup. I decided to buy another product and filled the bottom cup portion with Jim Bean. Within an hour, there was over a dozen flies caught in the trap. Don’t waste your money. This product does not work.
we were sent a product that was open, spilled all over the bag and one of the containers was missing half the liquid

I was having an awful problem with fruit flies in the kitchen and dining room. I read about using apple cider vinegar and water or, even better, Flypunch. I had apple cider vinegar on hand so I tried it first and ordered Flypunch on the same day. After two days of using the apple cider vinegar 95% of the fruit flies were gone. The Flypunch arrived and I figured it would clear up the remaining 5%. That was not the case. After three days of using only the Flypunch, two bottles, the fruit flies were back. I pulled out the apple cider vinegar and the next morning the flies were gone. If you want to get rid of fruit flies first try the apple cider vinegar.

This product did not work for me! I have had it out for about a week. There were only two fruit flies captured. I purchased another product which seems to have caught the ones that Aunt Fannies fly punch didn't catch. I absolutely wasted my money.

I purchased Aunt Fannie's fly punch fruit fly trap based on all of the encouraging reviews. More wasted money! It smelled just like vinegar when I opened it and left it out for quite a few days with quite a few fruit flies .They were not interested! I have a picture of the open container of the fly punch next to a tuna fish can filled with regular apple cider vinegar and a few pieces of old fruit. Regular apple cider vinegar caught more fruit flies compared to the fly punch which caught none.

Hasn't caught anything!! It ended up getting knocked over... what a mess!! It smells like vinegar. My son found a DIY recipe online and it's only caught a few but it was free!! I did a thorough cleaning with bleach on counters, floors, and cabinets and it's adequate a noticeable difference for today anyway!! Save your money... or throw it away but don't give it to these people!!

reviewTitle: Well it didn’t work, also leaked out before I got it…
reviewDescription: When I say this was the biggest disappointment I have come across in along time, I mean it. Now this worked for my friend and was recommended to me so I thought, trusted review from a trusted person so why not. Yeah no, smell is much stronger than regular soap and apple cider vinegar but I was willing to smell( it in a completely different room mind you) as long as it worked. Received the product and both bottles were leaking all over the Amazon package… oh well moving on. Left it out for a couple of days and didn’t catch one bug, not a gnat, not a fruit fly, only attention it caught was mine cause that smell was STRONG. You would have better luck just making a concoction yourself or buying a big light for gnats and fruit flies- that’s what I did when I returned this. Would not recommend to anyone but my enemy.

reviewTitle: Not worth it for the price
reviewDescription: I just don’t like it

reviewTitle: Good concept but not great results
reviewDescription: Still waiting. One fruit fly caught. My resolution - take caution using - pouring hot water from the tea kettle down all drains - it eradicates living fruit flies and the eggs if they have laid any in your drains. Haven't seen a bug since! To be sure, I have treated every night before bedtime for 4 days. Total success!

One can came empty. Amazon won’t respond I can’t get the seller to respond. So I pours half the liquid from one into the other and set them out. It’s been two days not a single fruit fly. :( I had high hopes but it really doesn’t work for me.
Stinks and leaking. I don’t know why I expected it to have a way too keep the bugs in, like you do when you set up a home made one. But there’s no way to trap them in.

reviewTitle: Works BUT see the photos attached and you decide
reviewDescription: The FlyPunch works, BUT,... I had much better luck with a simple household product that didn’t cost me but a few pennies. Both traps were set at the same time 48-52 hours ago. FlyPunch and a bowl of apple cider Venegar with 2-3 drops of dish soap stirred in. Traps were set 3-4 inches apart on the kitchen window. Pictures attached that shows a massive difference between the 2 traps. I was able to count 8 fruit flies in the FlyPunch and far too many to count in the apple cider vinegar mix Can’t say if the FlyPunch would have worked better with the cling wrap, often saw fruit flies sitting on the rim but not too many died. I simply followed the directions on the bottle and did the same with the apple cider trap I googled. 🤷🏽♂️ Left for Thanksgiving and forgot to throw out the bananas, and Shazam,... lots of fruit flies when we returned. 48-52 hours later 99% are gone. You decide which trap worked better.
Had high hopes this product would work… But… Not one fruit fly took the bate.. very disappointed!

I bought this product because I began to have fruit flies in my apartment. I wanted something easy, something that didn't require much work, so I thought this would be perfect. Just open and forget it. Well after almost two weeks it only caught two fruit flies, just TWO. I was pretty disappointed. I ended up just buying one of those electrical fan things that have a light that are made to trap fruit flies via the fan down onto a glue board. I ran this for two weeks and caught almost 60! So this fan thing worked way better than this liquid. And to top it off, no refunds are allowed. Very shady in my opinion. If my fan trap caught 60 and this liquid caught 2, then it obviously if faulty and I should have the option to get my money back. Do not waste your money on this product.

Sorry I ordered this and it will be going down the drain. The description says it’s “plant-based” and “safe around food”. But the label on the product contains several warnings, including to wear rubber gloves when handling and what to do if it gets on your skin. When it’s set up for use it’s an open bottle, just waiting to tip over. It contains Ethyl Lactate: “Ethyl lactate can be harmful if inhaled or absorbed through the skin. Prolonged contact can irritate the skin and eyes, and breathing it in can cause dizziness, lightheadedness, and passing out. Ethyl lactate is flammable and can cause serious or permanent injury”
Completely useless against gnats (fruit flies)...or any other fly infestation. A total BS product. Don't be fooled!

Otherwise 1. Guys. This overpriced apple cider vinegar with a drip of dish soap. The DIY way works way better. Get a clear up, put a few drops of dish soap in it. Pour in apple cider vinegar so that there is a light "soap lining" over the top. Then cover the glass with well stretched cling wrap. Use a fork to poke s few small holes (easy to get on, hard for them to get out which is a big thing this product misses). The fruit flies smell it and crawl into the holes, ho toward the food-smell and get stuck from the layer of soap. They can't crawl out because of the plastic wrap. Bingo. $1 worth of supplies

reviewTitle: Easy, it just did NOT work
reviewDescription: Fruit flies were not attracted to it. Easy to use, just remove the lid. The scent is fine, not even detectable, it is a great concept but the t e r r o apple shaped fruit fly traps work 100x better. I actually even used both in my home at the same time and this container was knocked over (This is easy to knock over & waste ALL) and hardly had any caught fruit flies in it when the t e r r o apples had caught as many flies as they could catch, so the one minute it takes to squeeze the liquid into the little apples is definitely worth it even though this natural product just requires a person to remove a lid. Would not buy again, and I hate that because this product is reasonably priced and easy to use. It just didn't work.

reviewTitle: But I've got to say I'm pretty disappointed this time
reviewDescription: I purchased Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch last summer due to an extreme amount of gnats and flies in our home. I felt the product worked extremely well and I knew I was going to buy it again a year later. A year later came, and i purchased 5 of them, one for each room that the gnats usually show up in. I received the 5 and not a single one of them I can get open due to the cap not coming off. I have to take a small flat head screwdriver and pry the cap off and then I'm able to get it off. When I finally get it off it also rips part of the seal off as well, so it makes a mess getting the liquid inside all over. I'm not sure if this was a production error or it's a problem with the new design of the product. But I've got to say I'm pretty disappointed this time.

I bought these because the description on the web site really seemed promising. I put them where we get these damn fruit flys. Bathroom sink, a few areas in the kitchen. The pictures above aren’t good, but there are only 2 out 3 flies in that bottle after over a week. Same with ALL of the bottles I placed. Please- don’t waste your time or money. NOT WORTH IT!!!

reviewTitle: Lot Of Money for Apple Cider Vinegar
reviewDescription: Not very effective. Caught some fruit flies but caught way more with homemade traps.

The positive is that I really like the bottle that it comes in. The negative is that it is just, in my opinion, diluted apple cider vinegar. This does not work! I did buy a three pack, and added a few drops of Dawn dishwashing liquid into each bottle and then it worked like crazy! I did have to tweak it in order to get it to work. The first 24 hours, before I added the Don dishwashing liquid, it did nothing

We just recently went on a vacation. Before we left one of our kids left a peach on the counter that rotted. When we got home we were super infested with fruit flys. So I bought this product to help get rid is of them. After 3 days it had caught just 4 fruit flies out of thousands of them.

Purchasing this product was a waste of money. First of all, the package was insecurely packaged. One bottle was empty when l opened the package. The liquid leaked all over the place. Secondly, the product caught very few gnats. I would have been better off just buying fly stickers or using apple cider vinegar and dish lotion.

My house has become infested with these stupid little fruit flies/drain flies/gnats whatever you want to call them. I have tried the homemade DIY traps and they catch some but not enough. This started out pretty good. I think my apple cider vinegar and dish soap attracted them more though. After having it out for a few weeks I finally just threw it away. I’m putting a pic of how many it caught. But keep in mind I still have them everywhere. And I stopped buying fresh fruit a couple months ago to try to help the situation(which it did improve but I miss fruit). Overall I would buy it again but there has to be a better way, a permanent way, to get rid of these things!!! Every time I purchase something online, I always check the reviews. I count on others to help me choose the best option. My reviews are always my honest opinion and I try to include photos if possible. If my review has helped you, please click the "helpful" button below, and if you have any questions feel free to ask! Thank you!

Bad fruit fly problem this summer. Got these amongst other products because I was desperate. Sad to say it didn't work. The flies would hover around it but never actually went in. If anything, I think they took a drink then flew away. Homemade apple cider vinegar traps worked better. It did arrive in a timely manner, however.
Este producto no es bueno no sirve bueno para mi no funcionó
Did not work at all. I have tons of fruit flies and it literally did not kill or trap a single one. Would not recommend at all.

I received the fly punch September 8, 2022. I have a bad fruit fly problem and opened it up and stuck it on counter along with a homemade fruit fly trap I made from cutting a plastic cup in half and placing a loose lid on top. I used Braggs organic apple cider vinegar and Palmolive dish soap but do not mix. You can use any dish soap. The pictures I shared are all 6 days. The Fly Punch has a couple of fruit flies and the homemade one has tons. I feel I wasted my money on this and the description made the fly punch sound appealing as to why I bought it. Thinking it would work better then homemade. Homemade is the way to go.

We have had really bad fruit flies in our area and I made this trap with a mason jar and within a few hours, all these flies were caught. I got Aunt Fannie’s Fly Punch hoping it would help and I set 2 traps out where I’ve been seeing the most flies and in 3 days, each trap only has 1 and my house smells HORRIBLE like vinegar from this stuff that DOES NOT WORK. Get a mason jar, some old fruit, a piece of paper rolled up and some tape and that actually works.

reviewTitle: Does not attract fruit flies more than other liquids
reviewDescription: I'm offended that I paid money for this. First, it's no better than laying out some apple cider vinegar out. Second, it will only work if you're super diligent about removing all other standing water sources: dry sink area, no plants retaining water in soil, no cups/pots with standing liquids, etc. I kept one by my kitchen sink and only caught 1 fruit fly over 5 weeks. Another was in an isolated grow room with visible fruit flies -- it only caught 2. I killed more with my bare hands by snatching them out of the area each time I was near these so called "trap". Honestly, this product is overpriced vinegar evaporating on my window sill. I won't be purchasing again.
I’ve waisted my money on a few things in life And this is definitely one of them it would not attract a fruit fly much less catch one !

Had high hopes but flies either never went in or just didn’t get trapped. They seemed to fly around it but didn’t go in. We have had it on our counter for a couple of weeks now. Agree with other reviews that perhaps the bottle design isn’t the best for trapping. It does smell like it is just strong vinegar. The smell was strong at first but weaker now. I will continue my search for fruit fly traps. Maybe the flies have now evolved to outsmart the vinegar traps.

First of all I only received two of the three bottles that were supposed to come in the package. I had a terrible fruit fly infestation when I bought these, so I used what came out of desperation and didn't return them. These were a waste of money. There were literally hundreds of bugs in my kitchen and maybe 10 flew into these, and I put them where I saw the highest concentration of them. Standing in my kitchen with the vacuum hose/wand in my hand for half an hour at a time (several sessions of this) was the only way to get rid of the infestation.

I put it into 2 different fruit fly traps and it has caught none. They will sit on it, but they just come right out. I usually use the Terro apples, but I wanted to try a more economical solution but this didn't work at all. My homemade trap has caught bunch, I just don't know if I got a bad batch or what?

I had high hopes for Aunt Fannies flypunch… but it didn’t get rid of the fruit flies. We had what seemed to be 5-10 fruit flies this summer… driving us insane. I was so hopeful that this flypunch would do the trick to get rid of them. Nope they would land on the lid, but that was it. I never saw one fruit fly that had gone for a swim in the punch. Smells just like apple cider vinegar. It’s in a bottle that spices would come in, so size is ok but the flies were not convinced and neither was I. I would probably not order this one again.
Purchased this for fruit flies, it seems to attract them only....they walk along the rim of the opened container but nothing happens! Except for the fact that they get bigger!!! Waste of my money.🤬

reviewTitle: Poor design only made the problem worse
reviewDescription: At first glance these appeared to be the same vinegar and dish soap traps in spice bottles I make myself and I was just tired of making them. There is only one big giant hole in the lid instead of a spice shaker pattern so they can fly in and out freely instead of having problems running into the grid. I don’t know if the viscosity of the vinegar is also a problem but in the current design it only appears to be working because they breed in it. Within two days the number of flies had doubled, today at one week I’m throwing them all out because I’m getting pelted by flies flying into me randomly all day.
This did nothing to help my net problem. Would not recommend buying this product.

Unfortunately, this product hasn’t worked for me. I’ve had it sitting out on my counter for a full week, and in that time, only two fruit flies have gone in. I deal with a high number of fruit flies, so I was really hoping this would help—but it just hasn’t made a difference. I’ll be trying other options instead.

This product smells horrible. I wouldn't know if it would work or not because I can't get past the smell. It actually made most of my house smell and caused my allergies to act up. So warning to anybody that has allergies you may not like this product.

These are terrible. They come in a bottle that does not have a funnel or a lid with holes. I had to dump the liquid in another jar I had with lid that had holes. In 72 hours, caught one fruit fly. Expensive--you can do this at home with liquid soap, vinegar etc. Live and learn.

I tried this product for several days, but it was not effective. It only caught one fruit fly during that time. In comparison, my homemade trap using apple cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap caught over 20 flies in the same period.
Fid not work in my kitchen. Caught two flys as the rest bothered everyone
Was hoping the poor reviews were wrong. Caught zero gnats. They landed on the top part and flew away shortly after. Idk if these new fruit flies are smarter or what!!
Made ZERO difference, even using both bottles at once. The bugs has no interest in the "Fly Punch"
Does not work. The flies aren’t attracted to the trap.

I bought two of these and left them out for three weeks. Neither caught a single fruit fly/gnat. I cannot believe I wasted $16 on these. I do not understand how anyone gave these good reviews! If I could give 5 negative stars, I would.

I have been struggling with fruit flies for a few weeks now and I have been trying every trick in the book. I did some research and heard about these and thought oh finally something easy to help with these annoying things. I have had them out for two days now and nothing….it attracts the fruit flys but they don’t go inside and if they do they come right back out. I thought I would try dumping some of it into a shallow plate but that did not work, I tried putting it in a bowl and that does not work either. I had got these little apple looking things prior and they worked pretty well so I just ordered a bunch of those alone with a drain killer to pour down my kitchen drain and hopefully soon I won’t have an issue anymore. But all in all I would not recommend these.

Purchased this product, and was very excited to use it since I have a pesky fruit fly problem. The bottles came broken. Some of the contents spilled into the packaging. Contacted customer support from the dealer and they do not do refunds since I bought through Amazon. Decided to use the product anyway, from what I had. I’ve had it open for 3 days now. It has caught one fly. All the other flies just fly around it. Product did not work for me. Don’t waste your money.

reviewTitle: It hasn't caught a single fly...
reviewDescription: When we open the back door, we've discovered flies congregating by the door, and invariably, at least a couple of flies fly in the door. (We have to go in and out a lot to walk dogs). I got one of those battery-operated fly eliminators that look like a tennis racket, thinking I could get them right at the door, but I'm not fast enough. We tried sticky fly paper, which works quite well, but it isn't attractive. I thought we'd give this a try. In four days, it hasn't attracted a single fly. There is nothing in containers. I got six. I put two out. I'm very disappointed because these containers sure look better than fly paper. I will update if there's any change but so far, not one fly...

This product doesn’t do much if you’re looking at drastically improving a gnat infestation. Get a drain cleaner instead. This is too gentle and doesn’t do much. Save your money. I wish I could return this lol

We had a lot of fruit flies upon returning from vacation. We tried these traps and caught only two flies with them in two weeks! The jars do not have any way to trap the flies, the lid is wide open, and this is a flaw of design. If the lid had smaller holes in it, the flies would get trapped in there. The flies fly into these traps and do not stay there--whatever is in it does not attract or kill them. We got rid of our fruit flies using yellow sticky glue traps and other liquid fruit fly traps that have a better attractant and design to keep the flies trapped. And also good old-fashioned home-made vinegar, water, dish soap traps helped--with a dash of apple-cider vinegar as attractant or small piece of fruit in it.
Strong vinegar smell, but tolerable. After 2 months, only 6 fruit flies. My leftover glass of wine works much better. Leave a small bowl of wine or juice overnight. Much more effective.
Literally did nothing. Left it out for 5 days and 0 flies went in. Waste of money

reviewTitle: It didn't work this time, formula change?
reviewDescription: Unlike the same product we purchased previously that worked really well, these two bottles were completely ineffective. Not a single fruit fly was caught, while my used wine glasses attracted many. I am not sure if there was any formula changes, but these simply didn’t work at all.

We had made our own fruit fly traps (apple cider vinegar with a few drops of dish soap) but read about these so decided to try them. When we received, I realized they are…apple cider vinegar with a few drops of dish soap. And, our homemade version has caught more fruit flies, are much cheaper, and these are more precarious and easier to accidentally knock over than if you build your own in a mug. Don’t bother.

reviewTitle: Does it get worse before it gets better?
reviewDescription: Could this be a “it gets worse before it gets better” situation? I bought the trap because I noticed only a few flies (maybe 5) around my kitchen I’ve only had the trap out for 24 hours now but I’ve noticed MORE fruit flies than before as if it’s attracting more flies with its smell. There are maybe 10 fruit flies in the trap but at least 10 more flies flying in places around the trap. I’ll try keeping it up a while longer to see if there is improvement but otherwise I might have to throw it away and try something else.

Basically the same as using a home vinegar mix except this worked less than a home mix. We had an infestation of fruit flies and gnats for a short period of time and even with using 2 of these in different locations of the house, we only caught 3 in a week long period. Caught more using home made mix which was much cheaper and a bug zapper.
Its not working and fruit fly still around
I did not care for the ACV all the time. Also with the completely open lid it was not very accident friendly or capable of keeping a bug in unless it was fully covered in ACV.

reviewTitle: Works decently, but homemade remedies are just as good
reviewDescription: This product is okay. It definitely attracted fruit flies but a homemade container of red wine vinegar and dish soap did the exact same level of job. I still have a fruit fly problem and it seems to have multiplied.
Didn't have any fruit so I guess they came up the drain. Too slow to work and too few dead. I can do the same with just apple cider vinegar

Basically, it did nothing but stink up the kitchen. I placed it in the kitchen where flies zooming around. In a week the flies were still airborne and not a single fly in the jar. I think it's design is poor. Just a tall and narrow jar, it is easy to tip over, and unlike similar products the top is nothing but an open jar. Other products to kill fruit flies I have used are lower and wider making them more stable and have several small holes in top so if it does tip it isn't a complete loss.
I don’t think it was worth the money or even works at all. I have had it out all night and all day today and only see 2 fruit flies in there. Not worth the money

reviewTitle: After the first week, fruit flies stop getting trapped - maybe the scent wears off?
reviewDescription: After the first week of using these fruit fly traps, fruit flies stop getting trapped - maybe the scent wears off? I wasn't impressed with these.

Based on what was advertised, it did none of that. And I didn’t read reviews where I saw some say nope and I still purchased it anyway. I was desperate. I had so many fruit flies in my house it was crazy. Placed both of them in designated areas and over 12 hours went by and it had only captured maybe 2 or 3. 24 hours maybe 6.
Bought this and the red terro traps and this. Placed them side by side for 2 weeks. Terro traps continually trapped fruit flies. This? Not even one. Complete waste of money.
DOES NOT WORK AT ALL! Did NOT capture 1 fly and there are plenty.

The container has no little holes for the fruit flies, its just open on top when you set it up. They don't list the ingredients but it's basically vinegar and some dish soap. You could make this yourself with an empty spice container. To add insult to injury it ABSOLUTELY DOES NOT WORK. Can fruit flies laugh? Because I'm pretty sure they're laughing as they swarm around our kitchen and completely ignore this "trap". Literally not even ONE fruit fly is stupid enough to fall for it. I however was dumb enough to purchase without checking all the reviews so Fruit Flies 1, stupid human 0.
Didn’t catch a single fruit fly.

I only gave this a one star because I couldn't give it a zero. When I read reviews I try to take all of them and try or not try based on what others say that is important. Please Don't waste your money on this product to me it doesn't smell like other say it does since I use apple cider vinegar at my house on a regular. But it doesn't help with the fruit gnats either. I placed a bottle right beside one of my plants and not one gnats went into the bottle. Even after two weeks when the smell was not strong it didn't have any gnats inside the bottle.

Product has been left out in kitchen counter for over a week, and just 1 little fly or gnat has been trapped in there. Definitely will not purchase again. I love AF products but not this one! Better stuff out there for sure
I was hopeful this would work based on the reviews. However, after two days, it has done nothing. I'm going to give it two more days. I will update my review if anything changes.

I purchased this item due to a recommendation by several websites. I had a MUCH better experience catching fruit flies with a home remedy than this product. I tried it in different areas of the kitchen and only caught one. The home remedy caught the large majority of the flies we were struggling with. The home remedy was apple cider vinegar and a drop of dish soap. I learned through trial-and-error that a shot glass works best instead of a bowl. I assume the steeper sides of the glass helped catch them.
Doesn't work
Dried up and didn’t catch any gnats.
DIDN'T WORK VERY WELL

I had such a bad gnats problem in the house and I was recommended these. As much as I wanted them to work, they barely did anything. I ended up investing is some Zevo lights and those work wonders. No more gnats or flies for that matter.

Didn’t work for me. I think I have even more bugs than before! I think the produce inside may work but the opening is narrow. I’m in Texas where these bugs are everywhere so it’s disappointing it didn’t work.

I had gnat-sized fruit flies in the kitchen, then noticed they would trawl around the house- bumbling just above the side tables in the living room, then I'd see them in the windows too. I thought I'd try Aunt Fannie's Flypunch (the vinegar scent smells CLEAN to me); it did attract them, but I watched them- this one-of-many species of fruit fly was smart enough to land on the sides of the jar inside, take a few steps to drink the liquid, then back up and fly out. When the jar was empty, there was a grand total of TWO flies in the bottom. I had high hopes, but no luck... My hubby had better luck with the little butterfly-shaped sticky stakes. He hung one a few inches under the shade on the side table lamp, and cut a slit in the lid of an old plastic margarine dish to slide another one into. Later, his little stakes had 40+ on each one. Of course right now we have no kids or pets around to spread the sticky all over, but the contest is done. 😄

reviewTitle: Great product but they shorted me a bottle.,
reviewDescription: This product works. I have ordered it before. But this time, even though the product is sold as a set, they only sent me one bottle.

reviewTitle: You can easily make this yourself..
reviewDescription: Vinegar with a squirt of dish soap in a small bowl works just as well. We did a side-by-side test. Use this only if you don’t have any vinegar and dish soap.
Didn’t really work, was trying to find way to refill Taro containers for fruit flies but all this did was make kitchen smell of vinegar.

The product attracted a few drain flys put didn't reduce their life cycle as advertised. It has the same vinegar smell that attracts the pests and a few will go in. Not worth the money. Won't buy again.

reviewTitle: Barely works, stick with vinegar and plastic wrap
reviewDescription: Apple cider vinegar in a small glass, Saran Wrap over the top, rubber band keeping the wrap on the glass. Wrap taut like a drum, poke three holes about 3mm long; long enough for the little so-and-so’s to crawl in. They can’t crawl out, and will eventually pass out and drown. Two or three of these glasses around the kitchen took care of everything. This product, by comparison, smelled worse and grabbed maybe 1/10th the amount of each wrapped glass.
Didn’t catch one single bug. My husband ended up making a mix of apple cider vinegar and soap that actually caught a bunch of them. Very disappointed.
Not worth the buy. Better off trying to real thing. Have not caught many since I’ve had it. Turned off every light and still does not work
This does not work like the other name brand. I bought this thinking it can't be that different from Zevo, but it was. It does not work nearly as well. It's also bigger and more of an eyesore.

I live on a farm and we have lots of flies and gnats that get in the house. I purchased this item for my room after using the Zevo brand bug trap elsewhere and being very pleased with the way it performed. Out of the bo,x this brand looked comparable, but I noticed very quickly it wasn’t performing as well as the Zevo brand ones we were using. Not only was it not attracting the flying insects in my room very well, but 2 weeks after purchase, the light quit working completely. I am very disappointed by this item and wouldn’t recommend it to anyone. I bought it trying to save a little money and I guess I got what I paid for. Lesson learned.
Ours didn’t catch very many bugs despite being by a backyard sliding door. Definitely don’t recommend!
This did not work. it was up for a week and did not get one gnat.

I'm really disappointed that this didn't work for me. I read a ton of reviews (and have friends that use similar ones), so am not sure what the issue is. I tried plugging it into 3 different outlets (areas) in my kitchen and it hasn't caught 1 fly. We had a ton of fruit flies and a few house flies in the kitchen during this time. I even put it in the outlet next to the fruit bowl. Literally did not catch 1 fly over the past week.

This did nothing for my fruit fly invasion. They were not attracted to the light whatsoever. When I did finally manage to capture one on the sticky part, by waving it around over a swarm of them, it was able to break free after a time and fly away unharmed. You are better off setting out a jar with a tiny bit of juice in it and then spraying the jar with bug spray once they congregate.
Don't waste your money. After 2 weeks of having it plugged in, not one fly on it.
Not effective at doing what it’s supposed to do. I didn’t order Zevo bc it was too expensive but I should have because this one doesn’t compare. My mother in law has zero and hers works really well.

reviewTitle: Not worth the time and effort
reviewDescription: It’s just a decorative blue light at nights no fruit flies caught
These do not work. One star for the one and only fruit fly caught in the month since I plugged it in. I never tried Zevo ones so I can’t compare.
18 days in I have 3 bugs. I can count 10 sitting on the wife's flowers. They are useless
They Do Not Work. Plugged in for a month and not one insect. Bought 2 sets, what a waste of about $35.

Recently I have mosquitoes and fruit flies in my house so I ordered this trap to help with the problem. I have used it about a week and so far I have not caught even one bug! They are easy to use, plenty sticky, look okay visually, but they do not catch bugs. Very disappointing!

reviewTitle: Worked great for 45 days and then died
reviewDescription: Worked great! Until it just stopped working 45 days later.
Light burned out in one and a half months. No way to replace bulb don’t waste your money.
Didn't attract one bug
I have had it up for 2 weeks now… we are infested with fruit flys, it’s so bad! and not one single fruit fly has been caught ):

This doesn’t work at all! I’ve had it plugged in for almost a month and nothing has stuck to the backside of the light and I’m seeing Nats fly around my place and not get stuck to the sticky pad. Don’t bother buying this just because it’s cheaper version than Zevo.
I have had this for awhile in a room where there is a lot of flys, so far I have not got anything. Don't work like advertised.

Every year, we have swarms of gnats for a few weeks. This year was worst and they drove me nuts. I ordered this and it killed a grand total of 3 gnats. I poured half a cup of apple cider vinegar into clear glass, added 1/2 tsp yellow dishwashing liquid (blue Dawn didn't work), stirred so there were no bubbles, sat it out, that killed dozens of them. I also poured several pots of boiling water into every drain to kill eggs. Good luck!
Light burnt out at 31 days - so now useless and out of "return window" Doesn't pay to buy off brand

reviewTitle: Failed with a month and a half of use
reviewDescription: Just outside of the return window and this device has stopped working entirely. What a waste of money. I highly recommend staying away from this product.
I have so many gnats it’s crazy, product has been plugged in for 4 days straight and not one gnat on the film.

I have to Zevo bug lights already in my house but packed them away to get out the Christmas decorations. I LOVE my Zevos but opted for this generic brand to save a little until you get the others out of storage boxes. These don't work at all! Not one bug has stuck to it whereas my Zevos work SO well. Buyer beware...
stopped working after 3 weeks.
Worked for a few weeks but now the blue light wont turn on. Spend a bit more to get a product that works
I would give ZERO stars if I could! Stopped working while I was out if town which happened to be 30 days out so I can't return! Waste of my money!
Does not do anything! Fruit flies are all over and nothing in the trap!
It don't work stop selling it b******* b******* b*******
Have had it plugged in for weeks and nothing has been caught.

I bought these thinking it would be a replacement for the real thing. That’s what u get for trying to save a buck. It’s been in place for two weeks and hasn’t caught a thing. I’ve used Zevo traps for over a year and they’ve been VERY effective so I know it’s in good spot. Spend the few extra bucks and get the real replacements.
Lasted 2 weeks and died.
Disappointed!!

reviewTitle: It may work…. Hasn’t worked for me yet
reviewDescription: The fly that I was trying to catch is still flying around after more than 3 day of using this device. Already threw away the package. So I guess I’m stuck with it! So far not worth the money!!!! It died on October 19th. What a waste of my money!!
Do not buy! It literally stopped working a week after the window to return it closed! I've had it less than 5 weeks!
Didn't catch one fruit fly yet, do not buy it !!!!!!!!!
Sorry to say, it dose not work I had it plugged in for 3 days and not 1 nat or fly ..😪😪
This is a "knock off" product, impersonating Zevo
I would give it a zero if I could. One week and no fruit flys caught though many flew near it. Tried different locations and still no good. DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY.

This did not work at all. Putting vinegar in a glass worked better. I used it for over 30 days and the initial cartridge caught maybe 10 nats and not the little fruit type flies that are all over the place This time of year. Definitely would not buy again.
Did not work. Bugs actually flew around it when they came to it. I do not know why but it just did not work.

reviewTitle: this electric product will not even last a year. poor quality.
reviewDescription: I bought this in june 2025 and it no longer works. this is the 2nd one that I have ordered and neither one last even a year.
Does not work at all. I even put apple cider vinegar in a bowl underneath it and it still did not attract any of the fruit flies!
Ordered a total of 9 different types, got two of these for an infestation. These were the worst of them, unfortunately. One light never worked and the other hardly caught anything.

Don't waste your money or time. We were invaded by flies this fall, so I bought a trap, I thought. It has been plugged in a week, the flies enjoy warming their feet but never venture inside. A moth and some small insects did. BUT NOT THE FLIES,

reviewTitle: $23 Neon Blue Night Light doesn’t catch flying insects
reviewDescription: Only buy this product if you’re interested in having a cool looking neon blue nightlight for $23 but not if you are interested in catching any flying insects. I left the product plugged in outlets in the two most heavily infested areas by fruit flies, first in the kitchen for two nights, and, when that didn’t work, in the bathroom for another four nights, and it only caught three fruit flies while dozens of fruit flies were flying around the areas surrounding the traps.
Do not buy house full of flies. This did not even catch one.

reviewTitle: It doesn't work - at least for fruit flies
reviewDescription: I was in desperate need for my fruit fly problem, which this says it specifically works for and it didn't work at all. The fruit flies kept multiplying and over the coarse of three weeks, I can count about 10 fruit flies it caught. I'm returning it.
